member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgePlan.ComputeSelfIntersection
member.summary: This only considers steps added in the latest command, it doesn't do the full intersection test. It is O(n^2) over those steps, but that's fairly cheap as there can't be many.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgeSegment.LaneSlots

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgeSegment.GetMinLaneIndexOfLaneAt(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the lowest lane index for lane at the given index. For single-lanes or empty lanes, returns the same index.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgeSegment.GetAllConnectedSegments
member.summary: Explores the bridge connections and returns an array with this segment and all the connected segments.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgeSegment.GetOppositeSegment(Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgeSegment)
member.summary: Gets the bride segment on the other side of the given one, if any. Example: For segments A-B-C, calling this method on B with A as argument would return C and vice versa. This is useful for exploring the whole bridge without needing to test for direction of each segments each step.
member.param: Segment of which we want the opposite of.
member.returns: None if this segment is not connected

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgeSegment.IsBridgeLaneComplete(System.Int32)
member.summary: Checks whether the bridge lane at the given index is "complete": the segment chain reaches entrances on both ends, and every segment along the way has a lane entity in the corresponding slot.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgeSegment.updateBridgeEntranceClosedStates
member.summary: Walks the connected bridge chain and updates the closed state of all BridgeRoadEntrance entities based on whether their lane is complete.

member.summary: Returns true if two bridge segments are part of the same bridge chain, not crossing a bridge-to-bridge boundary.

member.summary: Populates result with EndOffsetFromStart for all ramp entrance pieces (MustBeFirstPiece with elevation change) matching the given type and start direction.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Bridges.IBridgePathFinder.GetSlopeEndOverhead(Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgeProto)
member.summary: Returns the slope end overhead for the given bridge type: the extra cost of a slope end piece beyond its XY distance. Used to convert G8 costs to G0-equivalent for storage.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgePathFinder.VALID_TILES_SEARCH_RADIUS
member.summary: How many grid steps to expand around a position when searching for valid tiles.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgePathFinder.NodePool
member.summary: Pool for Node structs. Doubles in size when needed, never shrinks. Provides contiguous memory for cache-friendly access.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgePathFinder.GetCostTo_GeneratorOnly(Mafi.Tile3f,System.Nullable{Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainTrackNodeDirection})
member.summary: Intended for use in the generator. Assumes Options are set from a previous call.

Checks whether an exit/entrance piece collides with terrain using relaxed tolerances: 2.5-tile tolerance for entrance vertices, 0.5 for pillars, up to 25% of vertices allowed out of range, and at least one entrance vertex must be valid. Returns true if collision detected (placement should fail).

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgePathFinder.getCanonicalEndDirIndex(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Maps a canonical end direction to index 0-2 based on the canonical start direction. For start (1,0): (1,0)→0, (1,1)→1, (0,1)→2 For start (1,1): (1,1)→0, (1,0)→1, (1,-1)→2 Returns -1 for directions outside the canonical set (unreachable or > 90° from start).

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgePathFinder.GetMaxHeightForBridge(Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgeProto)
member.summary: Returns the maximum height delta for precomputed heuristics for a given bridge type. Uses 12 × GridSizeZ, clamped to PRECOMPUTED_HEURISTICS_HEIGHT for safety.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgePathFinder.isWithinDiamondConstraint(Mafi.RelTile3f)
member.summary: Checks if a position (relative to start) is within the diamond constraint region. The diamond extends from (start - V/2) to (goal + V/2) with width |V| at center. Uses pre-computed values to avoid per-node sqrt operations.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgePathFinder.getPrecomputedHeuristicsKey(Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgeProto,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Boolean@,System.Boolean@,System.Boolean@,System.Boolean@,System.Boolean@)
member.summary: Computes the heuristic key and symmetry transforms for grid-step heuristics. Start direction is canonicalized to (1,0) or (1,1) via mirrorX/mirrorY/swapXY. End direction is further reduced from 5 reachable (within 90° of start) to 3 canonical: Start (1,0): Y-mirror maps (1,-1)↔(1,1) and (0,-1)↔(0,1) → canonical (1,0),(1,1),(0,1) Start (1,1): X↔Y swap maps (0,1)↔(1,0) and (-1,1)↔(1,-1) → canonical (1,1),(1,0),(1,-1) Returns uint.MaxValue for end directions > 90° from canonical start or (0,0).

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgePathFinder.getExitEndDirectionIndex(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Maps a canonical end direction to index 0-2 for exit heuristic key encoding. (1,0)=East → 0, (1,1)=NE → 1, (0,1)=N → 2

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgePathFinder.getExitHeuristicsKey(Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgeProto,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Boolean@,System.Boolean@,System.Boolean@,System.Boolean@)
member.summary: Computes the exit heuristic key and symmetry transforms. Exit direction is canonicalized to East (1,0) via XY symmetry (mirrorX, mirrorY, swapXY). End direction is further Y-mirrored if needed to reduce to 3 canonical directions. Key: [BridgeProto:3][endDirIdx:2][heightSign:1][heightMag:6]

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgePathFinder.GetExitMaxHeightForType(Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgeProto)
member.summary: Returns the maximum integer height for exit heuristics for a given bridge type. Uses same multiplier as grid-step heuristics, clamped to EXIT_MAX_HEIGHT.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgePathFinder.GetTerrainBoostedHeuristic(Mafi.RelTile3f,Mafi.Fix32,Mafi.RelTile3f)
member.summary: Returns the terrain-boosted heuristic for a given position and base heuristic. If terrain boost is not active, returns unchanged.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgePathFinder.gridAlignHeightForGround(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Grid-aligns a height for TryToConnectToGround mode. When height is already grid-aligned, returns it unchanged. Otherwise returns the valid grid height (above ground level).

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgePathFinder.getPrecomputedHeuristicConnectToGround4Dir(Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgeProto,System.Int32,System.Int32,Mafi.Vector2i,Mafi.Fix32,System.Int32,Mafi.RelTile3f,Mafi.RelTile3f)
member.summary: Flattened heuristic lookup for TryToConnectToGround with no forced end direction. Computes the min cost across all 4 cardinal end directions, with shared computation for bridge type metadata, start direction symmetry, vector transforms, and height variants done once instead of 4x through the call chain.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgePathFinder.lookupSingleHeuristic(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.UInt32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Single-key heuristic array lookup. Returns -1 if height exceeds max or data is unavailable.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgePathFinder.lookupExitHeuristic(System.UInt32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Single-key exit heuristic array lookup. Returns -1 if data unavailable.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgePathFinder.GetExitToGroundHeuristic(Mafi.RelTile3f,Mafi.RelTile3f,Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainTrackNodeDirection)
member.summary: Looks up the exit-to-ground heuristic for a node at endRelPosition facing endDirection, wanting to reach goalRelative (ground level). Tries all 4 axis-aligned exit directions, applies symmetry transforms, and returns the minimum cost found. Returns -1 if no valid heuristic is available.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgePathFinder.getPrecomputedHeuristicWithFractionalHandling(Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgeProto,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,Mafi.Vector2i,Mafi.Fix32,System.Int32,Mafi.RelTile3f,Mafi.RelTile3f)
member.summary: Looks up the precomputed heuristic for a single (startDir, endDir) pair, handling fractional heights by taking the min across floor/ceil combinations.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Bridges.BridgePathFinder.checkHeuristicsIndicateUnreachable(Mafi.RelTile3f,Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainTrackNodeDirection)
member.summary: Checks both the standard precomputed heuristic and the exit-to-ground heuristic, returning true only if the best case of both indicates the goal is unreachable.

member.summary: Can be none if nothing is produced.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Buildings.Farms.CropProto.ConsumedWaterPerDay
member.summary: Consumed water to grow the crop (should be positive).

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Buildings.Farms.CropProto.ConsumedFertilityPerDay
member.summary: Consumed fertility per month (may be negative for crops that increase farm fertility).

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Buildings.Farms.CropProto.MinFertilityToStartGrowth
member.summary: Minimum fertility level to start growth.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Buildings.Farms.CropProto.DaysToGrow
member.summary: How many days this crop will grow.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Buildings.Farms.CropProto.DaysToSurviveWithNoWater
member.summary: Consecutive days the crop can survive without water. If null, crop never dies by lack of water.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Buildings.Farms.CropProto.PlantByDefault
member.summary: Used when crop rotation is not available yet.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Buildings.Farms.CropProto.Gfx.IconPath
member.summary: Icon asset path to be used in UI.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Buildings.Farms.Farm.CROP_SLOTS_COUNT
member.summary: Amount of slots in crops schedule.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Buildings.Farms.Farm.MAX_DAYS_DISABLED
member.summary: How many days can farm stay without workers or paused until crops die.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Buildings.Farms.Farm.FERTILITY_PENALTY_FOR_SAME_CROP
member.summary:
Extra fertility cost when the same crop is growing after itself, in other words it is a penalty for not rotating crops. This means less efficient farms at the start of the game and forces player to either rotate crops or pay extra fertilizer.
This does not apply to crops that give fertility.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Buildings.Farms.Farm.FERTILITY_REPLENISH_MULT_WHEN_ABOVE_100
member.summary: When farm fertility is above 100%, natural degradation is multiplied by this value. This slows fertility degradation down for over-fertilized fields. On the other hand, crop demand will raise by .

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Buildings.Farms.Farm.CROP_FERTILITY_DEMAND_MULT_WHEN_FERTILITY_ABOVE_100
member.summary: When farm fertility is above 100%, crop demand will be multiplied by this number, scaled by the difference from 100%. This makes crops consume more and more fertility on over-fertilized farms.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Buildings.Farms.Farm.STARTING_FERTILITY
member.summary: Farm starts at 80% fertility to discourage rebuilding farms to gain fertility.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Buildings.Farms.Farm.NO_YIELD_BEFORE_GROWTH_PERC
member.summary:
When crop is harvester prematurely, crop yield will be zero if it is grown less than this threshold.
In UI, the harvest button should be disabled when growth percentage is below this.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Buildings.Farms.Farm.IRRIGATION_START
member.summary: NOTE: Irrigation needs to keep space in soil buffer so heavy rain has a chance to replenish it. Otherwise rain has lowered benefit which means that dry season would have reduced impact on later difficulty and we don't want that. So keep space and make it rain!

member.summary: Currently planted crop.

member.summary: Previously harvested crop.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Buildings.Farms.Farm.FertilityNeededPerDay

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Buildings.Farms.Farm.recomputeYieldEstimates
member.summary: Recomputes yield estimates `m_avgYieldPerYear` and `FertilityNeededPerDay`. Needs to be recomputed when any of these change: * m_schedule * NaturalFertilityEquilibrium, m_avgFertilityUsedPerDay * FertilitySliderValue * MaxFertilityProvidedByFertilizer

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Entities.Blueprints.RecoverySaveConfig
member.summary: Legacy marker that previously identified recovery saves at the configs-section level. Replaced by dispatching on the next chunk header (HEADER_RECOVERY_SAVE vs HEADER_RESOLVER), which makes the discriminator a file-format detail instead of a serialized in-memory config. Kept so older recovery saves that still embed this marker can deserialize their configs section without erroring on an unknown type; the resulting instance is then ignored.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Entities.Blueprints.RecoverySaveManger
member.summary: Exports and imports a full game state in a version-agnostic format ("recovery save") that can be loaded as a fresh game. Uses string-based proto IDs and blueprint-style entity configs instead of binary serialization, making saves resilient to game updates that add, remove, or rename prototypes.
member.remarks:
FILE FORMAT =========== Extension: .recovery, stored in Saves/ alongside regular .save files. Wrapper: standard save file format (HEADER_MAIN, GZIP) so the load menu can show previews.
Layout inside the GZIP stream: HEADER_MOD_TYPES - mod manifest list HEADER_SAVE_INFO - GameSaveInfo (date, playtime, preview image, etc.) HEADER_CONFIGS - serialized IConfigs including marker HEADER_RECOVERY_SAVE RECOVERY_SAVE_FORMAT_VERSION (int) Section[] - named sections in fixed order (see below) HEADER_SAVE_END
Each section is prefixed by its ID string (via WriteStringNoRef/ReadStringNoRef). Sections are read in the exact order they were written.
VERSIONING AND COMPATIBILITY ============================ Proto-level compatibility: All prototype references are stored as string IDs (Proto.ID.Value). If a proto is renamed, removed, or added by a game update or mod change, the ID lookup fails gracefully - the entity/vehicle/research is skipped with a warning. This is the primary design goal: recovery saves survive content changes that break binary saves.
Format-level compatibility (RECOVERY_SAVE_FORMAT_VERSION): The format version is written after HEADER_RECOVERY_SAVE. It controls how sections are parsed. Rules for making changes:
* Adding new fields at the END of an existing section: Bump RECOVERY_SAVE_FORMAT_VERSION. In the reader, gate the new fields with "if (formatVersion >= N)" and provide defaults for older files.
* Adding a new section: Append it before HEADER_SAVE_END. Bump RECOVERY_SAVE_FORMAT_VERSION. In the reader, only read the new section if formatVersion >= N. Older files simply won't have it.
* Removing or reordering sections: Not allowed. Sections are read in fixed order; removing one shifts all subsequent reads and corrupts the stream. To deprecate a section, keep reading it (discard data) and stop writing meaningful data to it.
* Changing field types or semantics within a section: Bump RECOVERY_SAVE_FORMAT_VERSION and branch on version in the reader.
WHAT IS EXPORTED ================ * Terrain - height, surface, material layers, ocean flags for changed tiles (delta from generated). * Static entities - all with CloningDisabled == false, via . Preserves recipes, pause state, logistics, vehicle assignments, electricity priorities, custom titles. Transports: pivots, pillars, directions. Datacenters: server rack counts per type. * Vehicles - proto, position, direction, owner assignment. * Cargo ships - total count of functional ships (idle pool + actively in use). All ships return to the pool on load; each enabled depot auto-requests one on its next tick, so every depot that previously had a ship gets one back. Per-ship state (fuel, jobs, position) is not preserved. * Trains - car composition (proto IDs + orientation + product filters), depot assignment. * Train lines - name, color, icon, loco color mode, schedule (stations, priorities, product filters, depart conditions), load/unload settings, skip-if-fuel, skip-if-cannot-arrive, skip-if-next-can-arrive, train assignments. * Train station per-product rules (thresholds, require-full-service, allowed fuels, do-not-skip) and other per-station settings are preserved via the normal EntityConfigData path (). * Train networks - name, color, loco color mode, refuel threshold, cargo/waiting/fuel station classifications, train assignments. * Storage - product + quantity (regular storages + settlement food modules). * Transport contents - products on conveyors and pipes (product proto ID + quantity + position). Zippers and lifts are excluded (separate entity types, not transports). * Trees - removed generated trees (harvested areas stay clear), player-planted trees with growth state, and pending manual plantings. * Designations - mining/dumping areas with mine tower associations. * Research - researched nodes with TimesResearched count (supports incremental research like vehicle limit increases) + all unlocked protos as fallback for non-research unlocks. * Game state - sim step (exact), unity balance. Cap is restored naturally from settlements + research. * Population - total count. * Map data - WorldRegionMap + preview for terrain generation. * World map - full topology (locations, connections, home), exploration state, entity state (village reputation/pops, mine level/production/pause/title, repair status), loot, enemy flags. * Contracts - active contract proto IDs (force-established without cost or prerequisite checks). * Global product statistics - per product: lifetime production/consumption totals + daily/monthly/yearly history arrays, so production graphs are preserved. * Logistics zones - per zone: id, index, polygon area, color, custom name, construction-allowed-from links. Plus per-vehicle zone assignment. Entity zone masks are recomputed from polygon containment after zones load.
WHAT IS NOT EXPORTED ==================== * Vehicle/train transient state (pathfinding, cargo in transit) - spawn idle. * Machine processing state - restart from idle. * Zipper/lift contents - start empty (separate entity types, not transports). * Fleet state - fleet stays parked at home. * In-progress world map repairs/upgrades - player re-initiates. * Per-machine statistics - only global product stats are preserved. * Goals and messages - cleared after load. * Camera position. * Partially harvested trees and stumps. * Rocks and other terrain decorations (CloningDisabled entities). * Entity IDs - new IDs assigned, originals used only for internal cross-references during import.
ROBUSTNESS ========== * Unknown protos are skipped with warnings and tracked per category (with proto IDs). * Unknown terrain materials are replaced with bedrock. * Entity placement conflicts are logged and skipped. * Import results shown in a report window after load (with copy-to-clipboard). Also, available via console command: recovery_save_import_results. * Train depots can temporarily hold more trains than physical slots during import; trains without a slot still spawn correctly from the depot's spawn queue.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Entities.Blueprints.RecoverySaveManger.RECOVERY_SAVE_FORMAT_VERSION
member.summary: Format version for the recovery save chunked format. Bump when changing the layout of existing sections or adding new sections that must be read. Readers branch on for backwards compatibility.
member.remarks: History: v1 (v0.8.2c): initial version v2 (v0.8.3 ): added TRAIN_NETWORKS section; per-schedule-item SkipIfCannotArrive and SkipIfNextCanArrive bools v3 (v0.8.5 ): added LOGISTICS_ZONES section

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Entities.Blueprints.RecoverySaveManger.m_loadedFormatVersion
member.summary: Format version of the file being loaded. Set by so that section readers can branch on older formats.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Entities.Blueprints.RecoverySaveManger.FREE_RESOURCES_GRACE_DURATION
member.summary: Duration of the free electricity and computing grace period granted after loading a recovery save. Gives the player time to restore missing power plants and datacenters (which may have been lost to failed recipe migrations) before the game starts enforcing shortages again.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Entities.Blueprints.RecoverySaveManger.IsGraceActive
member.summary: Whether the 30-minute free-power/computing grace period is currently in effect on either manager.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Entities.Blueprints.RecoverySaveManger.HasSkippedEntities
member.summary: Whether any entities were skipped during import.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Entities.Blueprints.RecoverySaveManger.HasImportReport
member.summary: Whether a recovery save import was performed (loaded dict populated).

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Blueprints.RecoverySaveManger.ExportRecoverySave(System.IO.Stream)
member.summary: Exports recovery save using the standard save file format (HEADER_MAIN) so that the normal load infrastructure can show previews. Inside the compressed data, standard sections (MOD_TYPES, SAVE_INFO, CONFIGS) are written, followed by HEADER_RECOVERY_SAVE with map + terrain diffs + entities.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Blueprints.RecoverySaveManger.ReadMapData(Mafi.Serialization.BlobReader,Mafi.Core.Terrain.Generation.IWorldRegionMap@,Mafi.Core.Terrain.Generation.IWorldRegionMapPreviewData@)
member.summary: Reads map data (WorldRegionMap + preview) from the recovery save. Called from GameBuilder during loading.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Blueprints.RecoverySaveManger.LoadGameTimeSliced(Mafi.Serialization.BlobReader,Mafi.DependencyResolver,System.Int32)
member.summary: Loads recovery save overlay on top of an already initialized game (with terrain generated from the saved map). Reads terrain diffs, entities, vehicles, and designations.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Blueprints.RecoverySaveManger.writeSection(Mafi.Serialization.BlobWriter,System.String,System.Action{Mafi.Serialization.BlobWriter})
member.summary: Writes a section: section ID string marker followed by data. Sections are read in the same order they are written.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Blueprints.RecoverySaveManger.ReadSectionOrThrow(Mafi.Serialization.BlobReader,System.String)
member.summary: Reads the next section ID string and verifies it matches the expected value.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Blueprints.RecoverySaveManger.writeDecals(Mafi.Serialization.BlobWriter)
member.summary: Writes per-tile decal data. Run as its own section after BUILDINGS so entity reconstruction's auto-surface placement (which overwrites the entire TileSurfaceData via SetCustomSurface) cannot clobber decals. Iterates every tile rather than relying on IsChanged because decals are sparse compared to the full grid - the linear pass is proportional to the count of decal tiles, not all changed tiles.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Blueprints.RecoverySaveManger.ReadWorldMapData(Mafi.Serialization.BlobReader)
member.summary: Reads world map topology from the recovery save. Called from GameBuilder before game initialization so the custom world map generator can use the data.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Entities.Blueprints.RecoverySaveManger.DepartConditionData
member.summary: Helper struct for reading depart condition data before applying it.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Blueprints.RecoverySaveManger.writeLogisticsZones(Mafi.Serialization.BlobWriter)
member.summary: Per-zone payload format inside SECTION_LOGISTICS_ZONES. Versioned by the outer RECOVERY_SAVE_FORMAT_VERSION; bump it when adding fields here and gate the new fields with "if (formatVersion >= N)" in readLogisticsZones.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Entities.Blueprints.RecoverySaveManger.ZoneFilterEntry
member.summary: Per-zone truck-job filter payload buffered during deserialization, then applied in a second pass after every zone's existing filters have been wiped. The zone ID has already been translated from the saved value to the fresh runtime value via the savedId -> freshId map.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Blueprints.RecoverySaveManger.resolveColorIndex(Mafi.ColorRgba)
member.summary: Reverse-lookup a saved to its index in . Falls back to 0 if no exact match is found - the color won't be bit-identical, but the zone still gets a valid palette entry. We store the rgba (not the index) so a future palette change doesn't silently shift every zone's color.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Blueprints.RecoverySaveManger.writeAllProductStats(Mafi.Serialization.BlobWriter,Mafi.Core.Products.ProductStats)
member.summary: Writes every non-storage ItemStats on a in a fixed order. Scalars derived from live state (storage capacities / stored quantities / global quantity) are skipped since they are naturally reconstructed from storages + transports on import.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Blueprints.RecoverySaveManger.buildImportSummary
member.summary: Returns a short one-line summary of loaded/skipped counts.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Blueprints.RecoverySaveManger.BuildDetailedImportReport
member.summary: Returns a detailed multi-line report of what was loaded and skipped, including proto IDs.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Entities.Blueprints.RecoverySaveMarker
member.summary: Runtime DI marker registered into the resolver only while a recovery save is being loaded. Consumers (e.g. ) take an Option<RecoverySaveMarker> - presence indicates a recovery-load session. Not serialized: a normal save written from a recovery-loaded session will not contain it, so loading that save back behaves as a normal load.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Entities.Blueprints.RecoverySaveWorldMapGenerator
member.summary: World map generator that reconstructs a world map from recovery save data instead of procedurally generating one.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Entities.Blueprints.WorldMapSavedData
member.summary: Holds world map topology and entity state data for recovery save export/import. Registered with DI so that both the generator (pre-init) and the manager (post-init) can access it.

member.summary: Maximum backwards speed in discrete units per tick.

member.summary: Maximum sideways speed in discrete units per tick.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingData.SteeringSpeedMult
member.summary: Multiplier of speed at the max steering angle, is in range [0.1, 1].

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingData.Acceleration
member.summary: Forward acceleration in discrete units per tick.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingData.Braking
member.summary: Deceleration (breaking) in discrete units per tick. This should be generally more than acceleration.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingData.MaxSteeringAngle
member.summary: Max steering angle of the wheels. Value is in range [0, 80] degrees.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingData.MaxSteeringSpeed
member.summary: Maximal change of steering angle per tick.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingData.BrakingConservativness
member.summary: How conservative breaking should be, values around 2.0 are good, higher values causes more conservative driving.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingData.SteeringAxleOffset
member.summary: Distance from vehicle center to steering axle. This determines turning radius. If the offset is zero then the vehicle can turn in place.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingData.NonSteeringAxleOffset
member.summary: Distance from vehicle center to the non-steering axle. This determines turning radius.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingData.AxlesDistance
member.summary: Distance between steering and non steering axles.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingData.ShouldDrift
member.summary: Whether the vehicle should use "drift" steering.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingData.CanTurnInPlace
member.summary: Whether steering is independent of vehicle speed. For example tracks can turn in pace, wheels cannot.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity
member.summary: Driving entity is able to drive to given target ignoring all obstacles.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.FOLLOWING_BRAKE_DISTANCE
member.summary: Distance in tiles below which a following vehicle slowly brakes to open the gap.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.FOLLOWING_MATCH_SPEED_DISTANCE
member.summary: Distance in tiles below which a following vehicle matches the speed of the vehicle ahead. Beyond this distance the vehicle is unconstrained.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.s_goalBehindAngle
member.summary: The goal is considered behind if angle to goal is greater than this value.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.s_goalInFrontAngle
member.summary: The goal is considered in front if the angle is less than this value.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.DrivingData
member.summary: Driving data.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.Target
member.summary: Current target if any. Is valid only when is true.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.DrivingTarget
member.summary: Current target only valid when driving. When not driving, this is the target of the last drive command. This variable is intentionally not nullable to avoid null checks.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.DrivingTargetAngle
member.summary: Current direction target only valid when driving. Only used when "drifting".

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.IsDriving
member.summary: Whether this entity is actively driving somewhere. This is the main flag to query to know whether the entity is driving. Note that the vehicle may be still moving a little bit when is false.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.SpeedPercentOfPeak
member.summary: Speed as a percent of max a vehicle can achieve. -ve => reversing

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.AccelerationPercentOfPeak
member.summary: Acceleration as a percent of max a vehicle can achieve. Positive when speeding-up, negative when slowing down.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.SteeringAngle
member.summary: Current angle of steering relative to the vehicle's . For vehicles that can turn in place this is turning velocity per tick.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.DistanceToFullStop
member.summary: Braking distance of this entity. That's total distance traveled when braking at 100% every step. Returned value is always non-negative.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.LifetimeDistanceTraveled
member.summary: Odometer.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.m_targetTolerance
member.summary: Tolerance of reaching target. This tolerance is used when entity is about to stop or is already standing (new target). This should be large enough to allow entity to reach its target even if it is a little off but small enough to not introduce any noticeable imprecisions.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.TargetIsTerminal
member.summary: Whether current target is intermediate. Driver will not attempt to brake when reaching an intermediate target but rather drive through it at the full speed.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.m_state
member.summary: State of this vehicle. This controls its behavior.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.m_followingDistanceMult
member.summary: Per-vehicle multiplier for following distances. Range [85%, 115%]. Higher = more cautious (larger gap), lower = more aggressive (closer following). Computed deterministically from entity ID on creation.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.SpeedFactor
member.summary: Speed factor for animations. 100% means default speed, 50% means speed is halved (timings are doubled).

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.IsInRoadHeightTransition
member.summary: Whether the vehicle recently exited road-driving and still has road segment data for height projection. Used by rendering to maintain correct visual height while the vehicle is still physically above a road surface (e.g. bridge deck) but no longer actively following the road path.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.computeFollowingDistanceMult(Mafi.Core.EntityId)
member.summary: Computes a deterministic following-distance multiplier in range [85%, 115%] from an entity ID.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.TestOnly_SetSpeed(Mafi.RelTile1f)
member.summary: Sets speed of this entity. This is for tests.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.ShouldGetNextTarget
member.summary: Whether this vehicle can keep max speed to given target.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.SetDrivingTarget(Mafi.Tile2f,System.Boolean,Mafi.RelTile1f,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,Mafi.RelTile1f,System.Boolean,System.Nullable{Mafi.AngleDegrees1f},System.Boolean)
member.summary: Drives this entity to given . If is set to false this target is not terminal and the driver will not try to stop at the given goal but rather drive through it at full speed (and then just brake at full speed if no other target is given).

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.StopDriving
member.summary: Stops driving and resets the target.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.TryResumeRoadDriving
member.summary: Resumes road driving after it was interrupted (e.g., by pause). Returns false if road data is invalid or the road entity was destroyed, and clears road data in that case.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.SimUpdateInternal
member.summary: Performs driving logic. This should be called by any inherited class. This should be called always regardless of status because vehicle may be not driving but it still needs to decelerate and stop.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.handleStopped
member.summary: Vehicle is at complete stop and is waiting for target.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.handleStopping
member.summary: Vehicle is moving but is not driving. This effectively brakes the vehicle to complete stop or resumes driving.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.handleStopAndContinueForwards
member.summary: This is recovery state when vehicle hits steering singularity. This is solved by fully stopping. Driving forwards will keep steering when standing towards the goal.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.handleDrivingForwards
member.summary: Drives the vehicle forwards. If vehicle is currently moving backwards, it stops first and then drive forwards.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.ClearRoadDrivingState
member.summary: Clears road driving state (current segment, trajectory, indices) but preserves so that road height can still be projected during the road-to-terrain transition.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.TryGetRoadExitTile
member.summary: Returns the terrain tile at the exit of the current road segment chain, or null if the last segment is not a road entrance or the road data is empty.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.handleDrivingBackwards
member.summary: Drives the vehicle backwards. If vehicle is currently moving forwards, it stops first and then drive backwards.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.steerTowards(Mafi.Tile2f,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Steers the vehicle by setting towards the goal. If is true, it steers towards goal while vehicle is moving backwards. Returns true when max steering was performed.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.steerTowards(Mafi.AngleDegrees1f,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Steers the vehicle by setting towards the goal angle. If is true, it steers towards goal angle while vehicle is moving backwards. Returns true when max steering was performed.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.canDriveForwards(Mafi.RelTile1f)
member.summary: Whether area in front of the vehicle is pathable. This takes into account current speed.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.canDriveBackwards
member.summary: Whether area behind the vehicle is pathable. This takes into account current speed.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.PerformMoveStep
member.summary: Performs logic responsible for moving the entity.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.applyRoadHeightDuringTransition
member.summary: After exiting road-driving mode, the vehicle may still be physically above the road surface for a few ticks. This method projects the vehicle's position onto the retained road segments to maintain correct road height, preventing the vehicle from falling through the road. For bridge entrances, the road lane trajectory is shorter than the physical bridge deck (by ~3 tiles), so we also extend the projection past the lane end to cover the bridge surface overhang. Once the vehicle moves well past the road surface, the retained segments are cleared.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.isTargetUnreachable
member.summary: Tests whether current target is unreachable due to turning radius singularity. This assumes maximum steering.
member.remarks: This tests whether the target is inside of circles of maximal steering radius. This is assuming that we are going forwards. Reachability while backing up is complicated.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DrivingEntity.isTargetUnreachable(Mafi.Fix32)
member.summary: Tests whether current target is unreachable due to turning radius singularity. This assumes maximum steering multiplied by the conservativeness value. Values lower than 1 are more likely to say it is unreachable.
member.remarks: This tests whether the target is inside of circles of maximal steering radius. This is assuming that we are going forwards. Reachability while backing up is complicated.

member.summary: Returns center of rotation based on given steering angle. The center is relative to current position and is next to the rear axle. Given angle should be non-zero.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DynamicGroundEntityProto.Gfx.FrontContactPtsOffset
member.summary:
Front axle contact point offset from vehicle center. X = forward distance (positive = towards front), Y = lateral half-width (positive = left in game's RH system).
^ +Y in sim, +Z in Unity |
(-RearOffset.x, +RearOffset.y) ----- (+FrontOffset.x, +FrontOffset.y) | REAR O FRONT | --> +X (-RearOffset.x, -RearOffset.y) ----- (+FrontOffset.x, -FrontOffset.y)

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.DynamicGroundEntityProto.Gfx.RearContactPtsOffset
member.summary: Rear axle contact point offset from vehicle center. X = rearward distance (positive = towards rear), Y = lateral half-width (positive = left in game's RH system).

member.summary: Finds a path to the given goal and navigates there. Stops any ongoing navigation. Use to monitor ongoing navigation.
member.remarks: This will also perform re-planning when the current path is occupied or when the goal moved.
member.param: Goal specification.
Max number of retries. Each retry will extend goals according to the implementation of .
Whether to only allow simple mostly direct paths with no long detours. This restricts max path length.
Whether to disable automatic unstack trial when starting navigation.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.SmoothDriver
member.summary: Controls speed by smoothly accelerating and braking based on given parameters. This class provides simple API for controlling speed based on constraints such as maximal braking distance so the vehicle can always stop in time.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.SmoothDriver.Acceleration
member.summary: Current acceleration, positive when speeding-up, negative when slowing down.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.SmoothDriver.AccelerationPercent
member.summary: Acceleration in percent based on maximum possible speed. Positive when speeding-up, negative when slowing down. This takes speed factor into account.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.SmoothDriver.AccelerationPercentBase
member.summary: Acceleration in percent based on maximum possible speed. Positive when speeding-up, negative when slowing down. This does not take speed factor into account, so that absolute values may be larger than 100%.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.SmoothDriver.StepsToFullStop
member.summary: How many steps of braking will it take to reduce to zero.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.SmoothDriver.DistanceToFullStop
member.summary: Distance (accumulated speed per tick) that will be passed when we brake each step at 100%. Returned value is always positive.
member.remarks: This assumes that we perform braking before accumulation of the speed.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.SmoothDriver.ComputeDistanceToFullStop(Mafi.Fix32,Mafi.Fix32)
member.summary:
Computes the braking distance for a given speed and max braking rate. Every step, braking is applied before the distance is accumulated. This means that total braking distance D can be computed as:
B - 1 B - 1 B - 1 B - 1 D = Σ (S - i * S_b) = Σ (B * S_b - i * S_b) = Σ S_b * (B - i) = Σ S_b * i = S_b * B * (B - 1) / 2 i = 1 i = 1 i = 1 i = 1
where S is speed, S_b is braking speed, and B is number of braking steps computed as S / S_b. The sum is until (B - 1) because (S - B * S_b) == 0.
For floats the sum can be written as:
D = S_b * (Bf - 1 + Bf % 1) * floor(Bf) / 2 = S_b * (2 Bf - 1 - floor(Bf)) * floor(Bf) / 2

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.SmoothDriver.BrakeBy(Mafi.Percent)
member.summary: Performs braking by decreasing magnitude of by given percent.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.SmoothDriver.KeepSpeed(Mafi.Fix32)
member.summary: Tries to keep given speed that will be optionally constrained by total distance traveled.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.SmoothDriver.KeepSpeed(Mafi.Fix32,Mafi.Fix32)
member.summary: Tries to keep given speed that will be additionally constrained by maximum brake distance and optionally by total distance traveled.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.SmoothDriver.EstMaxSpeedTo(Mafi.Fix32)
member.summary: Estimates max speed in order to be able to brake in given distance. This estimation is optimistic because it uses simplified over-estimation of braking distance. The given distance has to be non-negative. Returned value is always non-negative.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.SmoothDriver.KeepSpeedNonConservative(Mafi.Fix32,Mafi.Fix32)
member.summary: Tries to keep given speed that will be additionally constrained by maximum brake distance and optionally by total distance traveled.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Dynamic.SmoothDriver.EstMaxNonConservativeSpeedTo(Mafi.Fix32)
member.summary: Estimates max speed in order to be able to brake in given distance. This estimation is optimistic because it uses simplified over-estimation of braking distance. The given distance has to be non-negative. Returned value is always non-negative.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Entities.EntityContext
member.summary:
Context that is accessible through any Entity in game. It should be used only for operations that are not performed often. For anything that happens on every simUpdate or every time a product is made, cache the references.
Benefit of this is that it reduces size of Entity classes that needs some of these deps rarely. It also reduces duplication of manages between individual entity helpers. Finally it is also not saved (faster save) and created from resolver on load. So deps can be added / removed anytime.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Entities.Static.ConstructionManager
member.summary: Handles construction and deconstruction of static entities.
member.remarks:
Static entity construction has following states and events:
ConstructionState.NotInitialized * Initialize() => NotStarted
ConstructionState.NotStarted * StartConstruction() => InConstruction * MarkConstructed() => Constructed with EntityConstructed invoked
ConstructionState.InConstruction * ContinueConstruction() => InConstruction -or- Constructed (EntityConstructed invoked) * StartDeconstruction() => InDeconstruction -or- Deconstructed (no EntityStartedDeconstruction) * MarkConstructed() => Constructed (EntityConstructed invoked)
ConstructionState.Constructed * StartDeconstruction() => InDeconstruction (EntityStartedDeconstruction invoked) * MarkDeconstructed() => Deconstructed (EntityStartedDeconstruction invoked if not already in deconstruction)
ConstructionState.InDeconstruction * ContinueDeconstruction() => InDeconstruction -or- Deconstructed * StartConstruction() => InConstruction -or- Constructed (EntityConstructed invoked) * MarkDeconstructed() => Deconstructed
Note that EntityConstructed and EntityStartedDeconstruction events are made symmetric. This means that the EntityStartedDeconstruction event is only invoked when the entity is entering deconstruction from constructed state.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Entities.Static.ConstructionManager.EntityConstructionNearlyFinished
member.summary:
Invoked when construction passes over (or deconstruction passes below) a threshold. This should be the point where construction cubes are fully covering the entity so rendering can for example swap models.
Note: This event will not be invoked if construction manager does not get to see this progress transition or if construction state is also changing on the same tick.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Static.Layout.EntityLayout.TransformRelative(Mafi.RelTile2i,Mafi.Core.TileTransform)
member.summary: Transforms given relative tile coordinate from this layout space to entity relative space (relative to its origin). To transform a coordinate to absolute coord use .
member.remarks: This transform does not simply rotate around (LayoutSize / 2) because it is off-center for layouts with even sizes. In order to prevent wobbling we need to transform around real center if possible. The only time this will introduce a little wobble is when one size is even and other odd. In this case there is no "stable" solution due to integer grid.

member.summary: If true, it is not allowed to place a custom surface for empty tiles under this entity. This for instance protects farms and settlements.

member.param: Entity will collapse if the number of vertices violating height constraints IS GREATER than this threshold.
Starting height. Value 0 is on the ground.
Final height (exclusive). Value 5 will occupy tiles up to and including height 4.
When specified, terrain surface will be set to this value during construction. The and will be also set to this value and will be added to .
When set, entity will be in danger of collapsing when the terrain is LOWER than the specified value.
When set, entity will be in danger of collapsing when the terrain is HIGHER than the specified value.
Height of vehicle surface.
When specified, one tile of terrain will be set to this material.
When specified, surface of specified ID will be placed on this tile.
Sets a custom specced port's height.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Entities.Static.Layout.LayoutEntityProto.CanMoveUpDownWhenInvalidPlacement
member.summary: Whether this entity can be moved up and down when its placement is invalid. For most entities, this should be false, otherwise we allow the player to move it well out of range of what is valid placement, and they have to spam their shortcut key to go back. But some large entities like bridges can have a wide range of placement locations, and therefore we want the player to have complete freedom to move them up and down during placement.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Entities.Static.Layout.LayoutEntityProto.Gfx.DisableEmptyChildrenStripping
member.summary: Whether we can strip out empty child gameObjects. If they are used in animation this is often safe but there are cases (e.g. if we use a child's transform to dynamically spawn something) where it's not. This is purely an optimisation. One measurement reduced cost of placing an entity with lots of empty children from 1.5ms to 0.5ms, and it's generally beneficial not to have to track stuff that does nothing.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Entities.Static.IStaticEntityWithReservedOceanV2.DockDirection

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Entities.Static.IStaticEntityWithReservedOceanV2.NarrowRatio
member.summary: Ratio of the dock-facing (narrow) edge to the ocean-facing (wide) edge of the trapezoid. 1.0 means no narrowing (rectangle), 0.5 means the narrow edge is half the wide edge. Only used when is non-zero.

member.summary: Checks if a tile is inside the trapezoid defined by the bounding rectangle and dock direction. The dock-facing edge width is times the ocean-facing edge width. Tiles intersected by the diagonal edges are treated as inside. When dockDirection is zero, the full rectangle is used (no narrowing).

member.summary: Monitors validity of ocean areas and notifies if they are blocked or unblocked.

This class groups areas monitoring and makes it way more effective than if we were to just monitor each area separately.
Areas are not saved and state is reconstructed on load.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Entities.Static.StaticEntityOceanReservationManagerV2.m_globalOccupiedBitmap
member.summary: Global bitmap with one bit per terrain tile. A set bit means at least one monitored entity's desired area bounding rectangle covers this tile. Used for O(1) early-out in tile change handlers. Uses the conservative bounding rectangle (not exact trapezoid) so false positives are possible but false negatives are not.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.Static.StaticEntityOceanReservationManagerV2.removeEntityFromGlobalBitmap(Mafi.Core.Terrain.RectangleTerrainArea2i)
member.summary: Clears bitmap bits for the removed entity's desired area, then re-sets bits for any remaining entities whose desired areas overlap.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.TileSurfaceCopyPasteData.DeserializeSurfacesForBlueprints(Mafi.Serialization.BlobReader,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Deserializes decals. Note we store surfaces and decals separately so we can do spatial compression better. If stored together, a decal in the middle of a square would break the square into at least 4 rectangles. This saves space as well as making later operations easier as we can use these groupings to operate in bulk rather than touching every tile.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Entities.TileSurfaceCopyPasteData.DeserializeDecalsForBlueprints(Mafi.Serialization.BlobReader,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Deserializes decals. Note we store surfaces and decals separately so we can do spatial compression better. If stored together, a decal in the middle of a square would break the square into at least 4 rectangles. This saves space as well as making later operations easier as we can use these groupings to operate in bulk rather than touching every tile.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Environment.IRadiationManager.ReportReactorRadiationLeak
member.summary: Called every sim update when a rector has a leak.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Environment.WaterPollutionManager.HEALTH_PENALTY_PER_QUANTITY
member.summary: We expect that reasonably early mid game settlements has approx 400 pops (20 waste water per month) and approx. 0-24 unused sour water. That leaves us to 40 as a nice spot from which we start reducing pop growth for pollution. Anything close to 90 gets 0.5% growth penalty which is close to devastating however that requires settlement with 7-800 pops + some extra waste, at that point waste treatment is expected. So we might need to increase the grow even a bit more.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Environment.WaterPollutionManager.MOVING_AVG_WINDOW_SIZE
member.summary: From heavily fertilized farm we can except 40+ fertility per year above allowance. In case of 10 farms that is 400 * 2 => 800 quantity ending up with 10% of pollution per year. That makes 0.66% per month on average.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Factory.ComputingPower.ComputingManager.FreeComputingGraceEndStep
member.summary: Sim step at which the free-computing grace period ends. when no grace is active. Treated as a separate source of "ignore missing computing" alongside so the two never interact: starting/ending a grace period does not touch the sandbox cheat flag and vice versa. Currently granted by recovery save import so the player can rebuild missing datacenters.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Factory.ComputingPower.ComputingManager.HasFreeComputingGrace
member.summary: True while the grace period has not yet elapsed. Independent of .

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Factory.ComputingPower.ComputingManager.StartFreeComputingGrace(Mafi.Duration)
member.summary: Activates (or extends) the free-computing grace period. The grace is purely time-based: it lapses automatically once the sim step reaches , no callback needed. Does not touch .

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Factory.ElectricPower.IElectricityGenerator.GetCurrentMaxGeneration(System.Boolean@)
member.summary: Returns current maximum electricity generation. If is false, the should not be called and returned capacity is ignored (set to zero). This can happen for example when the generator is disabled or broken. If it is set to true, the will be called even if returned max generation is zero.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Factory.ElectricPower.IElectricityGenerator.GenerateAsMuchAs(Mafi.Electricity,Mafi.Electricity)
member.summary: Generates as much electricity as requested. If this entity cannot scale production, it may return more than requested. The is returned value from .
member.remarks:
Note that this generation is requested when the internal electricity buffer is not full, despite this entity may not need to be generating because other higher-priority entities are already covering all electricity consumption.
This means that entity should NOT keep consuming some material and not returning any power because it can get stuck in this state indefinitely.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Factory.Machines.Machine.WorkedThisTick
member.summary: This allows to return progress for a super-fast recipes that take 1 tick. The reason is that fast recipes have m_recipeResult always None. Which would when calculating progress return 0. It is also correct because the entity really worked that tick (so even for slow entity it is correct. Another thing is that even machine has progress it doesn't mean that it actually runs. For instance if output ports are full, machine stops but progress is still 100%. For animations this is how to check that machine actually stopped to work.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Factory.Machines.Machine.ProgressPerc
member.summary: Progress on the current recipe.
member.remarks: If recipe takes 1 tick and is repeated all the time, you will get 1.0f from this method every time you ask.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Factory.Machines.Machine.m_recipesFast
member.summary: Provides great perf benefit. Instead of iterating over regular recipes we use this list of structs that also point directly to the corresponding buffers. Thanks to this we skip lots of dictionary calls and hops on RecipeProto.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Factory.Machines.Machine.m_recipeResult

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Factory.Machines.Machine.SpeedFactor

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Factory.Machines.Machine.UsesVirtualLimitedBuffers
member.summary: Introduced for maintenance depot which outputs its products into a global limited virtual buffer. The machine optimization is not able to handle that case and once it would reach full output buffer it would never try to send products again.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Factory.Machines.Machine.m_hasNoNeedToSearchForRecipe
member.summary: If we fail to start work on new recipe due to lack of input / output we set this to true. Thanks to this, in the next sim we can skip the expensive checking for recipes. Every-time a buffer receives more quantity (an input one) or sends away some quantity (an output one) it sets this back to false which means we try recipes again. This is also invalidated on recipes change. We do not need to save this and in fact we would have to invalidate it anyway because it can happen that some update lowers a recipe input requirements.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Factory.Machines.Machine.m_statusWhenNoNeedForRecipe
member.summary: The last status we had when we set m_hasNoNeedToSearchForRecipe to true. This is either full output or not enough input.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Factory.Machines.Machine.m_allConnectedOutputsBuffersEmpty
member.summary: This gets set to true if all output buffers were empty during last send attempt. This saves some perf on having to hop on each buffer and checking that there is nothing to save anyway. This gets invalidated anytime we store more products into output buffers or when a new port gets connected. We also don't save this as there is no advantage in doing so (we just try to send products once after we load).

member.summary: Total available power incl. input, output, and inertia buffer.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Factory.MechanicalPower.Shaft
member.summary:
Represents a physical shaft with inertial equal to sum inertia of all connected components.
Shaft has a max throughput .
member.remarks:
In order to make shaft input and output self-balancing, input and output is dynamically scaled and turned on/off based on total amount of stored power (inertia).
When there is less than 15% inertia, shaft output is turned off and only input is allowed. This allows the shaft to gain inertia all the way until 50% where output is turned off. To prevent constant oscillations between 15% and 50% when there is more demand than production, output is scaled based on the total inertia, being 25% at 15% of inertia, and 100% at 65% inertia.
Similar technique is used for input balancing, see diagram describing all the thresholds below.
Inertia 0% 10% 25% 35% 50% 80% 100% |==========|============|=========|===========|=========================|================|
Input scale: . . |================| . . 100% 0% Out: STOP «|............|» START . Out scale: |============| . . . . 25% 100% . . . Work area for not enough mech power: . . » » |............| « « .« « « « « . . Work area for excess mech power: . » » » » » . » » » » » » » |................| Work area for switching on/off mode: . TURN ON «|.....................................|» TURN OFF

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Factory.NuclearReactors.NuclearReactor
member.summary: Nuclear reactor that produces large amounts of steam.
member.remarks:
Reactor can start operation only when it is has people, is fully maintained, and is filled with of nuclear fuel. We require maintenance because reactor gets damaged during meltdown.
Fuel creates heat that is used to produce steam. If there is too much heat, emergency cooling is used to prevent overheating. If heat reaches over meltdown occurs, steam production stops and reactor is being shut down. When reactor heat is above , every following happens: 1) Fuel is wasted. 2) Maintenance is lost. 3) Radiation escapes. This means that the worse meltdown is the more is lost in process. Very short lasting meltdown won't cause much damage but completely uncontrolled meltdown (high power, no emergency cooling) may cause a lot of damage and release a lot of radiation.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Factory.Transports.StackerTower
member.summary: A stacker tower is an entity used to dump loose material, recieved from conveyors, directly on the ground. The boom can turn 360° and, when connected to a rail, it can move back and forth. The rail can be extended manually or automatically (to a limit). All this makes the tower a more flexible\autonomous solution for stockpiling material and a much better option for land reclamation (thanks its rail and the auto extension functionality)

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Factory.Transports.StackerTower.m_randomHash
member.summary:
Random value used as offset to stagger operations executed by different instances of this type\entity.
Example: (CurrentTick ^ m_randomHash) % 10.Ticks()
One instance will run at ticks: 2,12,22,32...
Another instance at ticks: 6,16,26,36...

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Factory.Transports.Transport
member.summary: Transport.
member.remarks:
Notes from transport optimization (Change-Id: I9988f39e22133491718cbe14b8135a69ac022232)
Transport trajectory representation was changed from segmented curve to discrete waypoints spaced out exactly by transport speed per tick. While this increases memory requirements for transports, it allows very significant optimizations for all processes. For example, transported products position on transport is just an index to waypoint array.
Changed transported product representation from large and complex class to 8-byte struct. This makes operations on transported products memory-efficient since they are all located very close together.
Significantly optimized transported product simulation, that is in many cases O(1), independent of the number of products transported: - This works by having transported product index split into absolute index which is per transport, and relative index per product. - If all or none products are moved, only the global index needs to be incremented. - Additionally, transported products remember if they are already at minimal spacing with previous product and can avoid spacing check when moving. - If all transported products are marked as "at minimal spacing", even transports that cannot move the first product can skip checking all remaining products and just increment global index (and not iterate all products to check if they can move).

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Factory.Transports.Transport.MAX_TRANSPORT_WAYPOINTS
member.summary: Transport length is limited so that the number of waypoints never gets close to `short.MaxValue`.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Factory.Transports.TransportPathFinder
member.summary:
Transport path-finder that is highly optimized for its purpose. It implements a variant of A* that is able to handle "jumps" (transport ramps) but may not be able to find a valid path in some cases (see remarks).
Both start and goals are not checked for collisions. Pillars checking is "best-effort" and found paths may not have valid solution for supporting via pillars.
Path-finder works in limited space domain 64 x 64 x 8 tiles. This means that the requested goal may get clamped to this domain during initialization. See .
member.remarks:
Reference: https://www.redblobgames.com/pathfinding/a-star/implementation.html
The core is a standard A* graph search algorithm but there are following changes to make work for transports.
===== RAMPS ===== Unlike standard A*, we need to be able to perform "jumps" between non-neighboring nodes which is not normally allowed in A*. These jumps correspond to transport ramps. Without jumps, we'd be limited only to straight up/down in the grid.
The ramps present a new issue with self collisions: A ramp occupies a set of extra nodes that should not be used by the path. Every node remembers an extra set of ramp-occupied-tiles that needs to be check for collisions when expanding. We use pooled arrays to avoid large number of small allocations here.
These jumps also cause a systematic issue with the algorithm. We can no longer guarantee that every path will be found, even if it exists, precisely because of the ramps that may block them. For example, imagine a path from A to C that goes through B. Then, a first explored path from A to B blocks C due to ramp. Unfortunately, the optimal path A -> X -> B -> C will be never explored because node B was already "expanded" and closed by the previous path. Concrete example: Path straight down from (0, 0, 1) to (0, 0, 0) where any ramp directly from start blocks the goal, but will be explored earlier than any other paths.
===== GOAL CHANGING ===== It is typical that for one start there may be many goals tested as the player moves their mouse while building a transport.
Goal-changing is implemented by re-computing priorities of all pending nodes. All already found nodes have optimal path lengths regardless of goal position (except when ramps screw us over).
It is important that goal position must not affect any logic during the path-finding. For example, since goal may be on a banned tile, we need to expand into banned tiles, bot not from them.
Support for goal changing also restricts us from only searching from the start. This means that we don't have an easy way of detecting blocked path before exploring ALL reachable nodes.
===== PILLARS CHECKING ===== Path-finder does its best effort to check pillars and discard paths that cannot be supported, but the checking is not perfect. Since we cannot predict when supports will be available, path-finder does search until `2 * pillar_radius` tiles are not supported. For example, it will fail in these cases: 1) Transport is shorter than `pillar_radius` and no pillars were found (a special case of #2). 2) Goal is reached but more than `pillar_radius` (but less than `2 * pillar_radius`) of the last tiles are not supported.
Due to challenges with jumps and goal changing, it is not possible to discard paths to goal that do not satisfy pillars constraints since such paths might be explored further when goal is changed.
===== WHY WE HAVE MULTIPLE NODES PER TILE ===== It is tempting to disallow certain path shapes by not expanding to certain neighbors. For example, we wanted to ban turns at start/end of ramps. This was easy, when expanding node and making a ramp, just check that parent was in the same direction as the extension. Similarly, after a ramp, only straight segment can be allowed.
The big problem is that such rules break an important assumption that we are searching on a graph. Every node of a graph must have a list of neighbors regardless of its parent.
For example, imagine we search a path A -> B -> C and node C has no neighbors coming from B. But there might be a longer path A -> X -> C where C does have a neighbor D, but this path will be never explored since A -> X -> C is longer than A -> B -> C and when expanding X to C, this expansion will be discarded since it is not the shortest path.
The takeaway is: Since we don't explicitly construct a graph, neighbor expansion have rules that does make it behave like it was a graph. For example, neighbors must not depend on current node's parent.
To fix this we added "overlapped" nodes, where a single point in space contains more than one node. Depending on where the parent node is we can move to a different node, and these nodes can then have unique connectivity. While this increases our memory consumption by a reasonable amount, performance was measured to be only 80% of the implementation without these overlapped nodes
===== PERF ===== Many optimizations were done to make the path-finding as fast as possible and allocation free.
1) All base node's data are pre-allocated structs around start node. Size of this pre-allocated area is 64 x 64 x 8 * 3 = 96k. This restricts the path-finder to search only in +-32 in X-Y, and +-4 in Z around start, and includes two extra arrays for nodes used by ramps (one for X, one for Y). Node's index can be easily translated to a coordinate using a few bit operations, but this also restricts domain dimensions to powers of two. 2) Node data itself is tightly-packed 8-byte struct. This makes path-finder's pre-allocated data only 774 kB. The smaller the data the less cache misses during path-finding will occur. 3) Collision checks for nodes are postponed. Instead of checking collisions during expansion and adding only valid nodes to the priority queue (as traditional A* does), we postpone expensive checks so that only nodes that are being taken out of the priority queue are being checked. This saves large amount of checks (proportional to queue size). Nodes that are never expanded are not even checked for collisions. The downsides are a slightly larger queue (can contain "blocked" nodes) and extra code complexity of collision checking. 4) Terrain height is cached so that we query terrain only once per unique 2D tile. Additionally, 3D tiles that are in collision are marked as banned for faster rejection next time. 5) Max pillars height is cached so that we pillars only once per unique 2D tile. 6) Occupancy collisions are also cached in nodes so that once a node has a collision, is marked as banned and collision on such node is never checked again. 7) Instead of tracking of used nodes so that they can be reset, clearing is done using `Array.Clear` that effectively clears ALL nodes at once. This turned out to be faster. The only downside is that we cannot store any persistent data in nodes such as node ID.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Factory.Transports.TransportProto
member.remarks:
Important note about transport throughput. We can affect throughput by three values: Transport speed, product spacing, and stack quantity. However, speed is fixed to spacing since speed has to divide spacing without remainder. This gives us following relationship:
Th = Spd / Spc * Stack, where Th is throughput, Spd is speed/tick, Spc is spacing, and Stack is stacking.
But Spc = Spd * w, where w must be positive integer, so we have
Th = Spd / (Spd * w) * Stack, which is Th = Stack / w
This means tha for a selected value of stacking, only some throughput values are possible, regardless of speed or spacing. Table below shows possible values of throughput per 10 ticks (1 second):
Throughput / sec (10 ticks) | w | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6 | 7 | 8 | 9 | 10 | | Stack +-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+ | 1 | 3.333 | 2.5 | 2 | 1.666 | 1.428 | 1.25 | 1.111 | 1 | | 2 | 6.666 | 5 | 4 | 3.333 | 2.857 | 2.5 | 2.222 | 2 | | 3 | 10 | 7.5 | 6 | 5 | 4.285 | 3.75 | 3.333 | 3 | | 4 | 13.33 | 10 | 8 | 6.666 | 5.714 | 5 | 4.444 | 4 |
To get speed and spacing for a chosen throughput, chose stacking row, in that row choose w with desired throughput. Then, choose spacing and speed is simply Spd = w / Spc.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Factory.Transports.TransportsConstructionHelper
member.summary: Helper class for computing how a new transport can be joined with existing ones. Only trajectory validation is performed by this class. No collision, terrain, or pillars validation is done.
Checks whether a transport with given parameters is buildable. This also considers joining the transport with other transports via mini-zipper or changing existing transports end direction.

Determines whether the given pivots can be joined to the given existing transport either at start (of the pivots array) or at the end (based on ). This assumes that the transports are overlapping at start/end.
There are two cases on connection: 1) Connection via mini-zipper. In this case the will contain details of the split of the existing transport and the will contain the new trajectory which will be shorter by the one tile where mini-zipper was placed. 2) Soft-join with neighboring transport by orienting current and existing transport's directions towards each other so that they will be merged once they are both built. This happens when they meet at the end. In this case the will contain details about change of direction of the existing transport and the will contain the new trajectory which will be shorter by the one tile where the transport overlap.
When false is returned, will contain useful info about the error.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Factory.Transports.TransportsConstructionHelper.TryFindPillarsForTrajectory(Mafi.Core.Factory.Transports.TransportTrajectory,Mafi.Collections.IReadOnlySet{Mafi.Tile2i},Mafi.Collections.ImmutableCollections.ImmutableArray{Mafi.Tile3i}@,System.Int32@,System.Int32@,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Finds supporting pillars for given trajectory. Returns whether the entire trajectory was successfully supported by pillars and the found supported tiles are at .
member.remarks: Rules for pillars placement are as follows: 1) Try to reuse all existing pillars along the trajectory that can be extended to the required height. 2) If there are no reused pillars on the first or last ceil(support_radius / 2) tiles, try to place extra new pillars there even if the tiles are already supported (start testing from start/end, moving inwards). This is to avoid visually unsupported transports that have long ends without pillars. 3) Add new pillars to all unsupported tiles, trying to support as many tiles as possible at once by finding the first unsupported tile at index i and trying to place a pillar furthest forward first (at i + support_radius), then trying to lower all the way to i - support_radius.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Factory.Transports.TransportsConstructionHelper.FindUnsupportedRegionAround(Mafi.Core.Factory.Transports.TransportTrajectory,Mafi.Tile3i,System.Boolean@,System.Int32@,System.Int32@,System.Nullable{Mafi.Tile2i})
member.summary: Checks whether the given transport is properly supported around the given position. The position may not be an occupied tile of the transport, in which case the closest supportable position will be checked.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Factory.Transports.TransportsManager.tryMergeTransports(Mafi.Core.Factory.Transports.Transport,Mafi.Core.Factory.Transports.Transport,Mafi.Collections.ReadonlyCollections.ReadOnlyArraySlice{Mafi.Tile3i},System.Boolean,Mafi.Core.Factory.Transports.Transport@)
member.summary: Tries to merge the given transports to one. The first transport's end point must be directed at the first connecting pivot, and the second transport's start point must be directed at the last connecting pivot. If no connecting pivots are given, both transports should point at each other. Both transport must be constructed.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Factory.Transports.TransportsManager.removeZipperIfNeeded(Mafi.Core.Factory.Zippers.MiniZipper)
member.summary: Removes the given mini-zipper if any of the following conditions are met: 1) Mini-zipper has no ports connected. 2) Mini-zipper has only 1 port connected, and the connected port is a transport, and the transport can be extended to replace that mini-zipper. If any of the previous conditions are not met, mini-zipper stays. 3) Mini-zipper has exactly 2 connected transports and they can be connected through the removed mini-zipper. If ports are not two transports, or the transports cannot be connected (due to direction for example), the mini-zipper stays. 4) If mini-zipper has connected 1 transport and 1 non-transport, and the transport can be extended to connect to the non-transport port.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Factory.Transports.TransportsManager.checkPillarAndReplaceIfNeeded(Mafi.Core.Factory.Transports.TransportPillar)
member.summary: Checks if pillar has transport at its top. If not, shortens or extends the pillar, or removes it when no transports are being supported anymore.

Tries to create a transport trajectory from given pivots and optional start/end directions and returns true if it was successfully created.
If start/end directions are not provided, they will "snap" to a first matching neighboring port, or they will be chosen based on transport direction so that they are valid.
This also verifies that transport has a correct direction and in case of miss match (e.g. start connected to input port) the order of pivots will be reversed. If both connected ports are input (or output), this will fail.
Note that may be set even when false is returned. This "invalid" trajectory can be still shown to user along with error message.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Factory.Transports.TransportsManager.IsMiniZipperPlacementNeeded(Mafi.Core.Factory.Transports.TransportProto,Mafi.Tile3i,System.Nullable{Mafi.Core.Factory.Transports.MiniZipperAtResult}@)
member.summary: Tests whether a mini-zipper is necessary to be placed based on the surrounding ports.
Port snapping used in .

Verifies whether a transport constructed from given parameters is buildable. This also considers joining a transport with mini-zippers or changing direction of existing transports.
If a transport is not buildable, the still may have some useful data on what was attempted (like a trajectory) that can be displayed to the player.
If start/end directions are not provided, they will "snap" to a first matching neighboring port, or they will be chosen based on transport direction so that they are valid.
This also verifies that transport has a correct direction and in case of miss match (e.g. start connected to input port) the order of pivots will be reversed. If both connected ports are input (or output), this will fail.
Note that resulting transport pivots may be different (shorter and/or reversed) than provided.

Tries to build a transport supported by pillars on given tiles.
This verifies that: 1) Transport itself can be built based on occupancy. The given trajectory is assumed valid. Use or to create one. 2) Transporting has no self-collisions (this is not guaranteed by ). 3) Transport is adequately supported on given 2D tiles by existing or new pillars.
It is OK to have extra or invalid pillars in the pillars array, but they will be discarded. If transport is still valid without them, it will be built.
Allows port snapping.
Exposes more info about the build attempt.

member.summary: Returns whether the pillar at the given position is redundant, meaning that removing it would not cause any transport or layout entity to become unsupported. Returns true if there is no pillar.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Factory.Transports.TransportTrajectory.TilesSupportInfo
member.summary:
Tiles ordered along the trajectory that need to be supported by pillars. A pillar will support all tiles around index i in radius indices. All pairs of neighboring tiles are at distance 1 to each other. The support data contains both positive (ie. tile can have a pillar) and negative (ie. it can't) information about supportability.
This differs from since that array has some extra entries where with null metadata. All supportable tiles have valid metadata.
