member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Factory.Zippers.Zipper.m_inputBuffer
member.summary: Input buffer that can accept one product per port to make input prioritization logic possible. Indexed by port index.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Factory.Zippers.Zipper.ForceEvenInputs
member.summary: When set, all connected inputs will accept same amounts of products.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Factory.Zippers.Zipper.m_outputBuffer
member.summary: Internal buffer of products waiting for output.
member.remarks:
|====================|====================|====================| | any input allowed |priority input only | |priority output only| any output allowed |
|» «| When buffer is in this range, any input is allowed (priority ports are preferred) but only priority output is allowed. This happens when there are not enough inputs. This range must be big enough to handle full throughput of priority output ports to no leak products to non-priority ports.
|» «| In the mid range, any inputs and outputs are allowed (priority ports are preferred). This happens when priority outputs cannot deal with the amount of products so non-priority ports output are being used. This range needs to be big enough to handle throughput of non-priority input and output ports
|» «| In the last range, buffer is too full and only priority inputs are allowed. This happens when too many products are flowing in the buffer. This range has to be large enough to allow priority input throughput.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Factory.Zippers.Zipper.ForceEvenOutputs
member.summary: When set, all connected outputs will receive the same amounts of products.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Fleet.BattleSimulator
member.summary: Battle simulator resolves battles using turn-based simulation.
member.remarks:
To evaluate a battle, use method.
State of a battle is held in the class so that the simulator can evaluate the battle in time-sliced manner over multiple game steps. Only one battle can be happening at a time and is accessible via .
Battle is evaluated in rounds. Each round, every entity from both fleets gets to perform an action using . Order of entities each round is fixed and based on . Note that defender has a small priority advantage .
Battle ends when at least one fleet has no entities with . There is a safety counter to prevent potential infinite battles. In this case or in case of draw (which currently should not happen), the defender is considered a winner.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.GameLoop.GameLoopEvents
member.summary:
Game loop events control the main loop of the game and are invoked on the main thread.
Inner game loop is running at the fastest possible rate which is usually the refresh rate of the monitor (60 Hz, can be 120 Hz). Frequency can slow down if the hardware is not fast enough.
All core game logic runs in the simulation thread and uses . Game loop events should be used only for rendering front-end and user input.
All events are invoked on the main (rendering) thread.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Game.ConfigIdentityFixer
member.summary:
Rebinds stale field references to the canonical DI-registered instances.
Why needed: V2 config serialization writes each config to its own length-prefixed blob with an isolated object reference table. References to a config from outside its blob - either from another config in the configs section, or from a manager class in the resolver section - cannot be encoded as back-references and get inlined as fresh duplicates instead. After load, those fields hold instances that differ from the canonical configs registered in DI.
V3 saves don't have stale refs (back-refs preserve identity on the wire), so this pass is a no-op for them. It runs unconditionally on load as a defensive measure for any V2 save still in the wild.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Game.ConfigIdentityFixer.StaleConfigRef
member.summary: One stale config field reference - the field's current value differs from what the resolver returns.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Game.ConfigIdentityFixer.EnumerateStaleConfigs(Mafi.DependencyResolver)
member.summary: Enumerates every -typed field on a resolved instance whose value is not the instance the resolver returns. Walks the inheritance chain with DeclaredOnly so inherited PRIVATE fields are also visited ( with NonPublic returns only declared private fields). Fields with a null value, or whose type cannot be resolved from DI, are skipped.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Game.ConfigIdentityFixer.RebindStaleConfigs(Mafi.DependencyResolver)
member.summary: Rebinds every stale field reference to the canonical DI instance via reflection (works on readonly fields). Idempotent - a no-op when nothing is stale. Returns the number of fields rebound.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Game.GameBuilder.OrderModsByDeps``1(Mafi.Collections.Lyst{``0},System.Func{``0,Mafi.Core.Mods.ModManifest})
member.summary: Sorts mods so that core mods come first (in order: CORE_MOD_ID, CORE_BASE_MOD_ID, CORE_UNITY_MOD_ID), followed by DLCs (sorted by dependencies), then third-party mods (sorted by dependencies). Mandatory dependencies are hard constraints (cycle throws ). Optional dependencies are best-effort (cycle skips the edge). The sort is stable: mods not related by dependencies preserve their original relative order.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Game.IConfig
member.summary:
Classes marked with this interface are global configs that are used to configure the game.
Configs marked with [GlobalDependency] attribute will be instantiated automatically (if not provided) via resolver and may take other configs in the constructor. However, no other dependencies are allowed.
Configs with Serialize method (or the ones marked with [GenerateSerializer] attribute) will be saved, and will be persisted. Serializable configs marked with [GlobalDependency] will loaded from the save file.
Configs that are not serializable or not global dependency will be only present in the first game launch and then will be discarded (for example map generation configs that are no longer needed when the map is generated).
member.remarks: Note that configs are handled in a special way, different from other global dependencies. See for more details.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Mods.IMod
member.summary:
A class marked by this interface is a game mod - a set of functionalities and data for the game.
Every mod can have dependencies which are listed as arguments of its constructor. All dependent mods are instantiated before the child mod. Cyclic dependencies are not supported. Optional dependencies are supported through class. All the registration and initialization methods are called in the topological order, that is, a method of a mod is called only after all of its dependencies were called so the mod can be sure that all of its dependencies are initialized first.
When mod is registered and instantiated the method is called. In this method the mod should use the provided class to register prototypes. It is also allowed to remove existing prototypes or replace them with different ones.
Then, mod's assembly is scanned for all types that are marked with the . These types are automatically registered to the dependency resolver builder. Additional types or instances can be registered with method using the provided . Note that automatic registration of global dependencies happens only one per assembly in case there are more than one mod per assembly.
All implemented methods should be implemented explicitly to avoid accidental call from dependent classes. All mods implementations should be sealed classes.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Mods.IMod.Manifest
member.summary: Manifest is a JSON file that is at the root directory of a mod. It is always passed as the first parameter of mod class constructor.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Mods.IMod.IsUiOnly
member.summary: Whether this mod is UI only and does not affect game state. All non-UI mods should not depend on or use Unity and UI-only mods should not contribute to game state changes.
member.remarks: UI mods are not instantiated in headless games such as determinism verification.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Mods.IMod.ModConfig
member.summary: Mod config. If set, it's instance will be registered in the resolver. If mafi-serializable, it will be persisted though saves and this property will be set after load if a config of the same type was loaded from the save file.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Mods.IMod.JsonConfig
member.summary: JSON-based mod configuration loaded from config.json in the mod's root directory. Define parameters with types, defaults, and optional validation rules (min/max, regex, maxLength). Values are persisted in save files and editable by players in the settings UI. Use , , etc. to read values, and subscribe to to react to mid-game changes. See for full documentation and config.json format.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Mods.IMod.RegisterPrototypes(Mafi.Core.Mods.ProtoRegistrator)
member.summary: Register all prototypes of this mod.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Mods.IMod.RegisterDependencies(Mafi.DependencyResolverBuilder,Mafi.Core.Prototypes.ProtosDb,System.Boolean)
member.summary:
Registers all dependencies such as components or custom implementations of any dependencies that should override default behaviors.
All prototypes of all mods are registered and prototypes database is locked before this method is called.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Mods.IMod.EarlyInit(Mafi.DependencyResolver)
member.summary: Early-initialization that is called right after the dependency resolver is created, before the map is even generated. Called for both new and loaded games. WARNING: During load, this is called after initial resolver load is finished, but before init-after-load methods marked with are called. Use this with care.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Mods.IMod.Initialize(Mafi.DependencyResolver,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Called exactly once before the game starts and after all the mods are registered and dependency builder is created.
member.remarks: This is the only place where mods can do some pre-processing, initialization, and checking after the whole game is loaded and ready to start. We use this for loading of UI that cannot be done in any other step since not all protos are loaded.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Mods.IMod.MigrateJsonConfig(Mafi.VersionSlim,Mafi.Collections.Dict{System.String,System.Object})
member.summary:
Called when loading a save that has JSON config values, before they are merged into the current config schema. Override this to migrate saved values when config parameters are renamed, removed, or need value transformations between mod versions.
After this method returns, the standard merge applies: for each entry in , if a parameter with the same name exists in the current config.json schema and the value passes type and constraint validation, it replaces the default. Entries that do not match any current parameter name are silently discarded. Entries whose values fail validation (wrong type, out of range, etc.) are also discarded and the parameter keeps its schema default.
member.param: The mod version that was saved. May be empty for saves created before version tracking was added.

Mutable dictionary of saved parameter names to their values. Modify this dictionary to rename keys, transform values, or add new entries before the standard merge runs. Values are boxed primitives: int, double, bool, or string.

member.remarks: If the map preview generation takes long (more than a few ms), it is recommended to implement this method using enumerator pattern and yield return previews are they are being generated. This will prevent the game lagging. TODO: Implement this.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Mods.IRegistrationMod`1
member.summary:
This interface marks a mod as registration service for another group of mods. This can effectively extend mod registrations system for new capabilities.
This mechanism is used by the game itself to extend Unity UI without Core knowing about any UI.
To use this interface, a mod has to define new interface that extends , for example IMySuperMod and then define new methods on this interface. All mods implementing the new IMySuperMod have to implement all new methods too.
Now, an existing mod can derive with TMod set to the IMySuperMod. This mod has now authority to register all instances of IMySuperMod with function.

member.summary: Given an array of all mods of requested type, any kind of registration can be performed. This always happens after all prototypes and dependencies are registered.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Mods.ModJsonConfig.MergeSavedValues(Mafi.Collections.Dict{System.String,System.Object})
member.summary: Merges saved values into this config's parameters. Parameters not present in saved data keep their defaults. Saved values for parameters that no longer exist are silently discarded. Numeric types (int/double) are coerced automatically when the schema type changes. Incompatible types fall back to defaults.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Mods.ModJsonConfigParam
member.summary: A single configurable parameter within a . Holds the schema (name, description, validation rules) and the current runtime value. The parameter type (int, double, bool, string) is derived from the default value in config.json. All JSON numbers are treated as double unless "is_integer": true is specified. See for full documentation.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Mods.ModManifest
member.summary: Mod manifest parsed from JSON.
member.remarks:
Example manifest:
{ "id": "awesome-mod", "version": "0.1.0", "display_name": "Awesome mod", "description": "This is my awesome mod.", "authors": ["Joe"], "min_game_version": "0.8", "primary_dlls": ["AwesomeMod.dll"], "non_locking_dll_load": true, "mod_dependencies": ["mandatory-other-mod"], "optional_mod_dependencies": ["maybe-this-mod"], "incompatible_mods": ["not-compatible-mod"], "can_add_to_saved_game": true, "primary_mod_class_name": "MyModClassName" }

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Notifications.NotificationProto.TimeToLive
member.summary: Duration until which the notification will be removed if not done by the player (0 is undefined). This is mandatory if you use . For instance we don't want to have notification like "New research unlocked" hanging there for 15 minutes. Don't use this for critical alerts. They should be removed immediately once the thing is fixed.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Notifications.NotificationType.Continuous
member.summary: Notification that is reported frequently and is also removed by the entity that reported it. It is usually about some state that holds for some amount of time like: "Not enough electricity". If the player dismisses such notification it is only internally suppressed (not removed) so it can be reported every update and still won't bother the player.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Notifications.NotificationType.OneTimeOnly
member.summary: Notification that is reported only once. And it is not expected from the entity that reported it to remove it. E.g. "Achievement unlocked". Notification is removed on dismiss so reporting this every update would spam the user heavily.

members.member: Formats current message with the given static data. This allows to have more generic messages without constants baked into them. .

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ClearancePathabilityProvider
member.summary:
Data structure that provides efficient querying of navigability based on terrain slope, height clearance, and tile pathability.
Additionally, this same data structure keeps track pf path-finding nodes. This is makes everything easier, even though the class is more complex.
member.remarks:
This class only stores data that has been queried (lazy evaluation). This may cause queries into new or stale chunks significantly slower.
This class can do a pathability query for area of NxN tiles in just N logical AND operations and one if. It is implemented using overlapping data chunks that store pathability data encoded in ulong.
Currently, chunks are 8x8 tiles with extra 4 tiles of overlap which makes them 12x12. Each tile stores 5 bits of data which results in 60 bits per row that is conveniently stored in an ulong.
The overlap allows queries for size up to (overlap + 1), in our case 5x5.
Note that this class will work with dynamic terrain (adding chunks during the game) only when there are no PF nodes on the newly created chunks. This can be achieved by having non-pathable areas around map edges.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ClearancePathabilityProvider.BeforeNodesDestroyed
member.summary: Called just before a destroys all its PF nodes. Allows the connectivity manager to decrement node counts before nodes vanish.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ClearancePathabilityProvider.BeforeChunkDirtied
member.summary: Called at the top of BEFORE any CapabilityChunkData is marked dirty or destroyed. Allows the connectivity manager to read component IDs from nodes that are about to become stale.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ClearancePathabilityProvider.RecomputedChunksCount
member.summary: This gets incremented every time a or gets recomputed. Since re-computation is relatively expensive, this can be used by caller to ensure PF is not taking too long when many chunks are being recomputed.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ClearancePathabilityProvider.CapabilityChunkData.EnsureNotDirtyIfNoNodesVisited
member.summary:
Recomputes PF data if it is dirty and no nodes are visited.
We do not recompute chunks that have nodes visited to not affect ongoing pathfinding. It is responsibility of the pathfinder to release visited nodes ASAP to not block recomputations (e.g. right after PF is node, not waiting until next PF request).

member.summary: Enqueues given task for path finding. Low priority numbers means higher priority. Each sim step we reduce priority by 1. This can be used to prevent low priority pathing from blocking higher priority pathing while ensuring the low priority pathing still completes.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.IVehiclePathFindingManager.AbortTask(Mafi.Core.PathFinding.IManagedVehiclePathFindingTask)
member.summary: Aborts given task and removes it from the queue.

member.summary: Returns home entity for given vehicle. Lost vehicles will return to its home entity. Returns None if there is no home entity available.

member.summary: Finds closest valid position for given pf params. This does not have to be the very closest empty space but rather some position that is known to be valid, like previously visited or explored position. This is used to unstuck vehicles.

member.summary: Pre-filters a pathfinding task by checking if start and goal tiles are in disconnected components. Returns true if the task should be rejected (no path exists).

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.IVehiclesConnectivityManager.MarkRootsAsStaleForRebuild(Mafi.Core.PathFinding.IManagedVehiclePathFindingTask)

member.summary: Accumulates the given tiles as seeds for the next DFS rebuild cycle at the specified capability index.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.IVehiclesConnectivityManager.SeedGoalsIfUnassigned(Mafi.Core.PathFinding.IManagedVehiclePathFindingTask)
member.summary: Seeds goal tiles whose PF nodes have no assigned component ID, triggering a DFS rebuild if any were seeded. Used after NavigateClosebyIsSufficient tasks that found a closest node but never explored the actual goal region.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.IVehiclesConnectivityManager.TryFindClosebyConnectedTile(Mafi.Core.PathFinding.IManagedVehiclePathFindingTask,Mafi.Core.Vehicles.IVehicleSurfaceProvider,System.Boolean@)
member.summary: For NavigateClosebyIsSufficient tasks where all retry levels are disconnected: scans PF nodes within MaxNavigateClosebyDistance of the goal tiles and returns the center tile of the closest connected node, or None if no reachable node exists.
member.param: True when definitively no reachable node exists within range. False when data is incomplete (caller should fall back to existing closeby tracking).

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.IPathFindingResult
member.summary: Read-only interface for path finding result.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.IPathFindingResult.ExploredTiles
member.summary: All explored tiles it they were requested. Otherwise, it's empty. Note that this is for debugging and is not saved.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFindingTask
member.summary: Re-usable path finder task. This class is intended to be instantiated by owning vehicle and it should be reused for all of its path-finding tasks.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFindingTask.Vehicle
member.summary: Vehicle that owns this task.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFindingTask.MaxNavigateClosebyDistance
member.summary:
This is distance at which path-finding tasks will succeed when require only to get close in order to complete, such as construction. Smaller values will result that such PF tasks fail more easily, larger values may cause that tasks are completed at too far distance, possibly creating ways for players to cheat.
Only works if is true.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFindingTask.MaxNavigateClosebyHeightDifference
member.summary:
Similar to , but limits the max height difference between the closest point and the target.
Only works if is true.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFindingTask.m_closebyOverrideGoal
member.summary: When set, the next call will override goal tiles with this single tile. Cleared after first use.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFindingTask.IsAbortRequested
member.summary: Set when is called while the task is still in a processing queue. Checked by the path-finding manager to skip stale tasks whose goal was already cleared (e.g., due to vehicle recovery with buffered abort in ).

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipPfStep.DirectionBoundA
member.summary: The direction of travel must lie between bound A and bound B

member.summary: The direction of travel must lie between bound A and bound B

member.summary: Whether the step is bound to a direction.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.PfNodeInfo.m_flags
member.summary: Packed flags: bits [0-3] direction (0-7 or 8=null), bit [4] isBackwards, bit [5] isSideways.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.PfNodeInfo.DirectionIndexRaw
member.summary: Returns the raw direction index (0-7 for directions, 8 for null). Avoids the nullable overhead.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipPfNode.PathFinderInstanceId
member.summary: There are (at least) two ways we could handle node clearing. 1) Save the pathFinder instance that hit this node, then clear if the current one is not the same. This costs us 4 bytes per node, which hurts caching and memory. 2) Clear every node we visited after each pathfinding. This adds extra cost to track all nodes we touched in the pathfinding, then clear them. We do 1), and this variable is to track this node's last used pathfinder instance. FWIW transport pathfinder currently does #2. Might be worth a perf examination.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipPfNode.IsVisited
member.summary: Whether this node was visited but its neighbors were not explored yet.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipPfNode.IsProcessed
member.summary: Whether this node is processed all its neighbors have been explored.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipPfNode.HasComputedPathability(System.Int32)
member.summary: Whether this node has ensured its neighbours have valid pathability information. This is required if we're to connect to them.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsClearancePathabilityProvider.m_dirtyTilesToReportTmp
member.summary: PERF: Lyst is preferred to a Set because processing duplicated entries is faster than de-duplicating them

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsClearancePathabilityProvider.RecomputedFineChunksCount
member.summary: This gets incremented every time a node gets recomputed. Since re-computation is relatively expensive, this can be used by caller to ensure PF is not taking too long when many chunks are being recomputed.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsClearancePathabilityProvider.m_pfNodes
member.summary: A dense array of "true" fine nodes for use by the pathfinder.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsClearancePathabilityProvider.m_chunkBlocking
member.summary: Each bit represents the pathability of the chunk at a given tile. The chunk is only treated as pathable if all bits are zero. Indexed by [heightClassOrdinal][chunkIndex] since blocking depends on ship height/draft. This is updated after any pathfinding work is done for the tick, synchronously with the sim thread.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsClearancePathabilityProvider.m_capabilityBlocking
member.summary: For each capability index, a direct reference to the blocking array for that capability's height class.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsClearancePathabilityProvider.m_chunkBlockedBitmaps
member.summary: One bit per chunk, set when for the corresponding height class is non-zero. Indexed by height class ordinal. Enables fast neighbourhood checks in .

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsClearancePathabilityProvider.m_capabilityBlockedBitmaps
member.summary: Per capability, references the entry for that capability's height class.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsClearancePathabilityProvider.m_capabilityToHeightClass
member.summary: Maps each capability index to its ordinal. Used by to look up height-dependent blocking.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsClearancePathabilityProvider.m_finePathabilityDirty
member.summary: A fine chunk is dirty if the data for ship orientations is dirty.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsClearancePathabilityProvider.m_nonDeterministicallyUpdated
member.summary: Bits are set if the pathability was computed from something outside which is called an unknown number of times. We have to be careful in this case as costs won't be attributed evenly in a multiplayer scenario.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsClearancePathabilityProvider.m_chunkPathability
member.summary: For every pathability index, for every fine chunk, whether it's pathable. Each bit of the byte represents a different angle. anti-clockwise from +X, in 45 degree increments. This can be dirty.

member.summary: Computes a blocked area in front of a docked ship to prevent other ships from clipping through it, and registers it.

member.summary: Tests whether an area specified by is pathable. Mask can be obtained via .

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsClearancePathabilityProvider.isChunkBlockedExcludingArea(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,Mafi.Core.Terrain.RectangleTerrainArea2i,Mafi.Core.EntityId,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns true if chunk at (chunkX, chunkY) would still be blocked after removing this entity's effects (dock blocking in and building occupancy identified by ). Uses a local copy of the blocking bits — does not modify global state.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsClearancePathabilityProvider.IsTilePathableExcludingOwnBlocking(Mafi.Tile2i,Mafi.Numerics.UInt128,Mafi.Core.Terrain.RectangleTerrainArea2i,Mafi.Core.EntityId)
member.summary: Checks if a tile would be pathable if the given entity's effects were removed (dock blocking in and building occupancy). Used by runtime monitoring to avoid self-blocking detection when a dock's own makes tiles in its ocean area non-pathable. Does NOT modify any global state — performs the clearance check locally - which means this is a fairly expensive function.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsClearancePathabilityProvider.ExtractClearanceFromMask(System.UInt64@)
member.summary: Extracts clearance information from given pathability mask.

member.summary: To be used if we're computing pathability in a way that will potentially differ between clients. eg. from the UI.12

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsClearancePathabilityProvider.JPS_MAX_JUMP_DISTANCE
member.summary: Maximum number of chunks a single JPS jump may traverse. Caps worst-case work per expansion on huge open-ocean maps.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsClearancePathabilityProvider.isConstrainedAt(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns true if the chunk at (,) is constrained (pathability != 0) or out of bounds. Handles dirty chunks by recomputing them.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsClearancePathabilityProvider.hasForcedNeighbor(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Standard JPS forced-neighbor detection. Returns true when the chunk at (,) has a forced neighbor relative to the jump direction (,).

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsClearancePathabilityProvider.tryJump(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Boolean,System.Int32@,System.Int32@,System.Byte@)
member.summary: Scans from (,) in direction (,) looking for a JPS jump point. A jump point is a chunk that is constrained, has a forced neighbor, is the goal, or lies at the map boundary. For diagonal jumps, cardinal sub-jumps are checked recursively.
member.param: True when called as a cardinal sub-jump from a diagonal jump. Sub-jumps return false for boundary/max-distance termination since those don't indicate an actual jump point.
member.returns: True if a valid jump endpoint was found.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipHeightClass
member.summary: Categorizes ships into height classes for blocking computation. Each class maps to a MinDepth and ShipHeight. Kept small (very few values) so we can use enum ordinal as a direct array index.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsPathFinder.MAX_DISTANCE_FROM_MAP_EDGE
member.summary: The max distance from the map edge ships can drive to while still being supported by PF graph.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsPathFinder.NOT_FULLY_PATHABLE_COST
member.summary: We try to avoid areas that are not fully pathable. They're generally slower to move though, more costly on the pathfinder and more likely to have ugly behaviour. We can't penalize them too much though or long channels which should be fine won't be used.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsPathFinder.NOT_FULLY_PATHABLE_DIRECTION_CHANGE_COST
member.summary: Changing direction in not-fully pathable areas is an additional penalty

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsPathFinder.REVERSE_COST
member.summary: Reversing is not a disaster, but should be avoided.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsPathFinder.SIDEWAYS_COST
member.summary: Last resort: go sideways.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsPathFinder.PATHFINDING_CLOSE_RANGE
member.summary: Distance at which we move to the "close" pathfinding costs. More expensive, but produces better paths.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsPathFinder.PATH_FOLLOWING_TURN_COST
member.summary: High values tend to over-prefer straight paths, but also reduce exploration of all directions of "bad" nodes.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsPathFinder.PATH_FOLLOWING_TIGHT_TURN_COST
member.summary: We should prefer non-tight turns to tight turns. For turning around 4 tight turns may save ~6 chunks worth of movement, so ideally should be higher than that 1.5 * side length + TURN_COST

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsPathFinder.PATH_FOLLOWING_TURN_IN_PLACE_COST
member.summary: We should prefer tight turns to turning in place.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsPathFinder.PATH_FOLLOWING_REVERSE_COST
member.summary: Reversing is not a disaster, but should be avoided. Ideally this would be fairly low, but it results in weird open ocean behaviour.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsPathFinder.PATH_FOLLOWING_SIDEWAYS_COST
member.summary: Last resort: go sideways.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsPathFinder.HEURISTIC_DISTANCE_WEIGHT
member.summary: Increase the heuristic distance by this amount. Values over 1 make the heuristic inadmissible, but also converges faster. Should not be less than 1.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsPathFinder.CLOSEST_NODE_IMPROVEMENTS_ATTEMPTS
member.summary: How many PF iterations to do when searching for the closest node and new nodes are not improving the current minimum.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsPathFinder.CLOSEST_NODE_IMPROVEMENTS_RESETS
member.summary: If we go past CLOSEST_NODE_IMPROVEMENTS_ATTEMPTS we'll just pick any valid closest. If there is not a valid closest, we'll end up getting stuck. If it can't find a valid closest, extend by CLOSEST_NODE_IMPROVEMENTS_ATTEMPTS up to this many times.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsPathFinder.m_startOutOfBoundsEdges
member.summary: 4 bits set describing our out-of-bounds stats: 0: +X, 1: +Y, 2: -X, 3: -Y

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsPathFinder.m_goalOutOfBoundsEdges
member.summary: 4 bits set describing our out-of-bounds stats: 0: +X, 1: +Y, 2: -X, 3: -Y

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsPathFinder.m_toProcessList
member.summary: Visited nodes that awaiting processing.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsPathFinder.m_processedList
member.summary: Nodes that were already processed (all neighbors were expanded).

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsPathFinder.m_trackedClosestStartSetNodeToGoal
member.summary: When this is set, path finding is also tracking the closest node from start set and terminates when new closer nodes are not found for attempts. This is used for cases when we need a path to the closes point even if the goal is blocked.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsPathFinder.ResetState
member.summary: Resets state and frees all nodes from internal lists. This should be called when PF is done and path is reconstructed.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsPathFinder.chunkStepDistance(Mafi.Tile2i,Mafi.Tile2i)
member.summary: Computes the distance between two chunk positions. Handles arbitrary distances for JPS jumps. On the chunk grid, moves are always purely cardinal (one component zero) or purely diagonal (both components equal), so no sqrt is needed — just multiplication by sqrt(2) for diagonals.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsPathFinder.estPathCost(Mafi.Tile2i,Mafi.Core.PathFinding.PfNodeInfo@,Mafi.Tile2i,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns cost estimate from to . This is A* heuristic function that should not overestimate. The better the heuristic matches the final cost function, the better A* will perform.
member.remarks: * The heuristic function h(n) is admissible if h(n) is never larger than h*(n) or if h(n) is always less or equal to the true value. * If A* employs an admissible heuristic and h(goal)=0, then we can argue that A* is admissible. * If the heuristic function is constantly tuned to be low with respect to the true cost, i.e. h(n) ≤ h*(n), then you are going to get an optimal solution of 100%

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsPathFinder.addNodesForTargetTile(Mafi.Tile2i,Mafi.Fix32,Mafi.Collections.Lyst{Mafi.Core.PathFinding.PfNodeInfo},Mafi.Option{Mafi.Collections.Lyst{Mafi.Direction45}})
member.summary: Creates PF nodes at for each valid direction/backwards combination, applying as the initial node cost (plus REVERSE_COST if backwards).

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsPathFinder.addOobEdgeNodes(Mafi.Tile2i,System.UInt32,Mafi.Collections.Lyst{Mafi.Core.PathFinding.PfNodeInfo},Mafi.Option{Mafi.Collections.Lyst{Mafi.Direction45}})
member.summary: For an OOB coordinate beyond one or more map edges, scans all pathable chunks along those edges and creates PF nodes with initial cost equal to distance from the OOB point.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsPathFinder.getClosestTileToGoalFromNodesVisitedFromStart(Mafi.Core.Vehicles.IVehicleSurfaceProvider)
member.summary: Returns the closest processed PF node (to goal) visited from any start nodes.

member.summary: Runs the path-finding for given number of iterations. Returns status of the operation. Should be called again only when is returned.

member.summary: Extends path from to . Returns whether a path was found.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipPathSegment.PathRawReversed

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ShipsPathFindingManager.NoOpConnectivityManager

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.UnionFind
member.summary: Disjoint-set (union-find) data structure with path halving and union by rank. Each element is identified by an integer ID allocated via . Supports recycling of unused entries via .

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.UnionFind.Entry
member.summary: Packed into 64 bits: Parent (bits 0-31), Rank (bits 32-62), IsAlive (bit 63).

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.UnionFind.Count
member.summary: Number of live entries (including those merged into other roots).

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.UnionFind.HighestUsedCount
member.summary: One past the highest allocated index. Use as iteration bound. Note that not all indices in [0, HighestUsedCount) are live.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.UnionFind.CreateNewId
member.summary: Allocates a new element as its own root. Returns the element's ID.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.UnionFind.FindRoot(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the canonical root ID for the given element. The tree is balanced, so this should be O(log(n)) in the worst case, but cheaper in reality.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.UnionFind.Union(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Merges the sets containing elements and . Uses union by rank. No-op if they are already in the same set.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.UnionFind.IsAlive(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns whether the entry at the given index is alive (not recycled).

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.UnionFind.CopyFrom(Mafi.Core.PathFinding.UnionFind)
member.summary: Copies all entries from into this instance, replacing any existing data. Index-to-entry mapping is preserved.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.UnionFind.RecomputeHighestUsedCount
member.summary: Recomputes after bulk removals. May lower the bound if trailing entries were removed.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.UnionFind.RemoveAllExcept(Mafi.Collections.Set{System.Int32})
member.summary: Removes all live entries whose IDs are NOT in .

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.UnionFind.Clear
member.summary: Clears all entries, resetting to an empty state.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFinder.CLOSEST_NODE_IMPROVEMENTS_ATTEMPTS
member.summary: How many PF iterations to do when searching for the closest node and new nodes are not improving the current minimum.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFinder.CLOSEST_NODE_IMPROVEMENTS_RESETS
member.summary: If we go past CLOSEST_NODE_IMPROVEMENTS_ATTEMPTS we'll just pick any valid closest. If there is not a valid closest, we'll end up getting stuck. If it can't find a valid closest, extend by CLOSEST_NODE_IMPROVEMENTS_ATTEMPTS up to this many times.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFinder.ROAD_DISTANCE_MULT
member.summary: Traveling on roads in 50% cheaper. Unfortunately this breaks A* metric admissibility (the estimation function may over-estimate), but if we'd make non-road travel more expensive, terrain only PF will spend way too much time exploring nonsense (metric would work worse).

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFinder.m_toProcessList
member.summary: Visited nodes that awaiting processing.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFinder.m_processedList
member.summary: Nodes that were already processed (all neighbors were expanded).

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFinder.m_trackedClosestStartSetNodeToGoal
member.summary: When this is set, path finding is also tracking the closest node from start set and terminates when new closer nodes are not found for attempts. This is used for cases when we need a path to the closes point even if the goal is blocked.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFinder.m_isKnownDisconnected
member.summary: Set by connectivity manager when start and goal are known to be in different components. Causes goal seeding to be skipped and closest-node tracking to activate immediately.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFinder.ResetState
member.summary: Resets state and frees all nodes from internal lists. This should be called when PF is done and path is reconstructed.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFinder.estPathCost(Mafi.Tile2i,Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePfNode,Mafi.Tile2i,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns cost estimate from to . This is A* heuristic function that should not overestimate. The better the heuristic matches the final cost function, the better A* will perform.
member.remarks: * The heuristic function h(n) is admissible if h(n) is never larger than h*(n) or if h(n) is always less or equal to the true value. * If A* employs an admissible heuristic and h(goal)=0, then we can argue that A* is admissible. * If the heuristic function is constantly tuned to be low with respect to the true cost, i.e. h(n) ≤ h*(n), then you are going to get an optimal solution of 100%

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFinder.GetPfNodeAt(Mafi.Tile2i)
member.summary: Returns PF node at given coord from the given capability index (or from the last used one).

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFinder.getClosestNodeToGoalFromNodesVisitedFromStart(Mafi.Core.Vehicles.IVehicleSurfaceProvider)
member.summary: Returns the closest processed PF node (to goal) visited from any start nodes.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFinder.continuePathFinding(System.Int32@,Mafi.Core.Vehicles.IVehicleSurfaceProvider,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Runs the path-finding for given number of iterations. Returns status of the operation. Should be called again only when is returned.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFinder.extendPath(Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePfNode,Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePfNode,Mafi.Fix32,Mafi.Option{Mafi.Core.Roads.RoadGraphPath})
member.summary: Extends path from to . Returns whether a path was found.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFinder.TryExtendGoals
member.summary: Adds new goal nodes to the ongoing or failed path finding run. This can be used to extend search without starting completely new rerun.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFinder.GetExploredTiles(Mafi.Collections.Lyst{Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ExploredPfNode})
member.summary: Fills given list with nodes that were explored during last or current path-finding run.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFinder.AStarHeap.VisitedFromStartSetSize
member.summary: Number of nodes in this list that were explored from start node ( set to true).

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFinder.AStarHeap.VisitedFromGoalSetSize
member.summary: Number of nodes in this list that were explored from goal nodes ( set to false).

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFinder.AStarHeap.Clear
member.summary: Clears this heap and calls on all saved nodes.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehicleTerrainPathSegment.PathRawReversed
member.summary: Path stored in reverse, first tile of the path is the last. This makes for efficient reconstruction and popping from end during driving.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFindingManager.m_currentPfStepsRemaining
member.summary: Maximum total steps for the entire PF task.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFindingManager.m_currentTaskIsKnownDisconnected
member.summary: Whether the current task was identified as disconnected by the connectivity manager. Used to skip rebuild seeding on failure (the manager already has accurate data).

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFindingManager.m_currentTaskConnectivityConfirmed
member.summary: Whether the connectivity pre-filter confirmed the current task is connected (allDisconnected was false). Suppresses goal seeding on successful completion.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFindingManager.m_waitingTasksQueue
member.summary: Tasks waiting to be processed.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFindingManager.m_currentTask
member.summary: Task of currently ongoing path-finding. This is to avoid passing it around. May be null if no path-finding is currently ongoing.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFindingManager.CompletedPfTasks
member.summary: This gets incremented every time PF task completes.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFindingManager.updatePathFindingTasks(System.Int32@)
member.summary: Processes current or next task. At most one task will be finished per call.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFindingManager.startTask(Mafi.Core.PathFinding.IManagedVehiclePathFindingTask,System.Int32@)
member.summary: Starts new path finding task. Returns true when new task was started and is ready for stepping. False is returned when new task either succeeded or failed right away and should not be stepped.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFindingParams.MaterialTraversalSensitivity
member.summary: Controls how sensitive is the vehicle for material penalties. For example, excavator with track is not very sensitive a should have this value below 100%.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFindingParams.RoadLaneTypeMask
member.summary: Masks road lane type when testing vehicles compatibility.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFindingParams.ConvertToCornerTileSpace(Mafi.Tile2i)
member.summary: Converts the given center-based coord to corner-based space where clearance is a square towards +XY, not around a point.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePathFindingParams.ConvertToCenterTileSpace(Mafi.Tile2i)
member.summary: Converts corner-based tile space to center-based coords.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePfNode.CurrentNeighbors
member.summary: Current neighbors that might not include all available due to non-existing or dirty neighbors.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePfNode.ComponentId
member.summary: Component ID assigned by for connectivity pre-filtering. Two nodes with the same ComponentId are considered connected (accounting for merges). Defaults to for new nodes.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePfNode.ComponentGeneration
member.summary: Generation counter for . Only valid when matching the current generation in .

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePfNode.IsVisited
member.summary: Whether this node was visited but its neighbors were not explored yet.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePfNode.IsProcessed
member.summary: Whether this node is processed all its neighbors have been explored.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePfNode.HasParent
member.summary: Whether this node has parent that is not equal to itself. By our convention, start/goal nodes have parents set to themselves.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePfNode.GetAllValidNeighbors
member.summary: Returns edges to all neighbors. This ensures that all surrounding chunks have valid non-dirty data.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePfNode.EnsureAllPfNeighbors
member.summary: Ensures neighbors that this node potentially touches are not dirty if no nodes are visited.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager
member.summary:
Connectivity oracle using seed-DFS from starts and goals.
When pathfinding fails, the manager accumulates the task's start and goal tiles as seeds. A time-sliced DFS in simUpdate propagates from these seeds, assigning component IDs to reachable nodes. Dirty chunks are cleaned lazily as the DFS encounters them.
Invariant: returning true guarantees no path exists. It may return false when no path exists (optimistic), but never the reverse.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.UNASSIGNED_COMPONENT_ID
member.summary: Sentinel value for nodes that have not yet been assigned a component ID.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.s_disabled
member.summary: When true, the connectivity manager is disabled: all queries return "connected" and the DFS does no work. Also reverts ClearancePathabilityProvider to the old destroy-unvisited behavior in markPfDataDirty.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.m_capabilityClearances
member.summary: Per-capability clearance values, used to convert road tile coords to corner space.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.m_capabilityRoadLaneMasks
member.summary: Per-capability OR of all road lane type masks for params sharing that capability. Used to skip capabilities that cannot use a given road type.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.m_activeCapabilities
member.summary: Bitmask of capabilities that have been used by at least one pathfinding query. Once set, bits are never cleared. Event handlers and DFS skip inactive capabilities.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.m_stableUFs
member.summary: Per-capability UF snapshots from the last completed DFS. Queries read from these. Indexed by capability index.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.m_activeUFs
member.summary: Per-capability accumulating UFs that persist across DFS cycles. DFS writes to these. Periodically compacted at DFS completion via . Snapshotted to at DFS completion. Indexed by capability index.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.m_lastCompactedRootCounts
member.summary: Per-capability count of distinct component roots after the last compaction. Used to decide when the next compaction is needed.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.m_dirtyChunks
member.summary: BitMap indexed by encodedIndex. Set when a chunk is dirty (tile data changed since last clean).

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.m_chunksWithComponents
member.summary: BitMap indexed by encodedIndex (chunkIdx * capabilityCount + cap). Set when DFS assigns component data to any node in the chunk+capability pair. Monotonically growing (never cleared except on full reset). Used by to skip chunks that have never been DFS-visited.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.m_dfsStack
member.summary: DFS traversal stack (LIFO via PopLast). Seeded from starts and goals. Queueue is used instead of Stak so Dequeue (BFS) vs PopLast (DFS) can be switched easily for comparison. DFS measured faster for me.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.m_pendingSeeds
member.summary: Accumulated seed tiles from and .

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.m_stableGenerations
member.summary: Per-capability generation of the last completed DFS. Queries accept any generation in [1, m_stableGenerations[cap]]. Starts at 0 so the first DFS (generation 1) won't collide with default node generation (0).

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.m_dfsGeneration
member.summary: Generation of the current/latest DFS cycle. Incremented at each beginDfs. Used only for DFS traversal to detect "visited this cycle".

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.m_dfsInProgress
member.summary: True while a DFS is in progress (stack not yet exhausted).

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.m_dfsSeedIndex
member.summary: Index into m_pendingSeeds for time-sliced seed processing in beginDfs. -1 when not actively seeding.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.m_compactionChunkIndex
member.summary: Resume position: next chunk index to process during compaction.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.m_compactionCapability
member.summary: Capability index being compacted. Set in .

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.m_compactionRoots
member.summary: Set of UF root IDs referenced by live nodes, collected during the Compacting phase. Used in Finalize to remove unreferenced entries from the active UF.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.m_dfsTriggeredCapabilities
member.summary: Bitmask of capabilities that need a DFS cycle. One bit per capability. Set when a pathfind fails or road connections change. Cleared when DFS starts for that capability.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.m_dfsUncertainCapabilities
member.summary: Bitmask of capabilities where we encountered genuinely unassigned/unexplored nodes (via or closeby-check deferrals). When set, cannot trust component data for that capability and must be optimistic. Cleared when DFS starts. Rebuilds triggered only by failed pathfinding do NOT set this bit.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.m_dfsActiveCapability
member.summary: Capability index being processed by the current DFS cycle, or -1 if idle.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.m_staleRoots
member.summary: Per-capability sets of active UF roots that may be invalid due to terrain/road changes. During DFS, nodes with stale roots are reset to UNASSIGNED and re-explored instead of union-skipped. Indexed by capability index.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.m_startRootsTmp
member.summary: Temporary set for collecting start component roots during queries.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.m_roadNodesSearchedThisCycle
member.summary: Road node IDs already searched during the current DFS cycle (dedup).

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.m_deferredDirtyEntries
member.summary: Entries deferred because their chunk had nodes visited by an active pathfinder. Retried each simUpdate before DFS work. Once cleaned, re-seeded into the next DFS cycle.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.m_pendingPathableConnectors_tmp
member.summary: Terrain connectors whose road search is deferred from to . Road graph entities register one at a time, so the DFS can't traverse the full road until all entities from the same step are registered.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.RebuildGeneration
member.summary: Incremented on each DFS completion. Used by overlay renderer to detect changes.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.initCommon
member.summary: Shared initialization for both new-game and load paths. Requires and to be set.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.beforeSave
member.summary: This is quite a heavy function - it throws away a lot of the "in-progress" work, while retaining the key data so that saves don't reset connectivity state back to a blank slate. Unfortunately, this means saving data for every pathfinding node.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.FindRoot(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the canonical root in the stable UF for a given UF element ID and capability. Returns if the input is -1.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.AreDisconnected(Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePfNode,Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePfNode)
member.summary: Returns true if the two nodes are provably in different connected components. Returns false (assume connected) when uncertain.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.findRootForNode(Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePfNode)
member.summary: Returns the UF root for a node's component, using the stable UF for nodes from completed DFS cycles and the active UF for nodes from the current cycle. Caller must ensure node has a valid ComponentId and ComponentGeneration >= 1.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.isChunkDirtyForCapability(Mafi.Chunk8Index,Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ClearancePathabilityProvider.DataChunk,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns true if the chunk+capability pair has dirty/stale state that makes its connectivity data unreliable.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.isDefinitelyUnpathable(Mafi.Tile2i,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns true when we can be certain no PF node exists at the given tile -- i.e. the chunk exists, its connectivity data is not dirty, and GetPfNodeAt returned None.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.NodeResolveResult.Valid
member.summary: Node found with a valid component; root is set.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.NodeResolveResult.Unassigned
member.summary: Node exists but has no component yet (needs seeding).

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.NodeResolveResult.DefinitelyUnpathable
member.summary: Tile is provably unpathable; caller should skip it.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.NodeResolveResult.Unknown
member.summary: State is uncertain; caller should assume connected.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.tryResolveNodeRoot(Mafi.Tile2i,System.Int32,System.Int32@)
member.summary: Resolves a tile to its UF root. Handles node lookup, destroyed/dirty checks, and unassigned detection. Seeds unassigned nodes for future DFS.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.shouldRejectSingleStart(Mafi.Core.PathFinding.IManagedVehiclePathFindingTask,System.Int32)
member.summary: Fast path for when there is exactly one start tile. Avoids Set overhead by comparing goal roots directly against the single start root.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.continueSeeding(System.Int32@)
member.summary: Processes pending seeds in a time-sliced fashion, consuming up to budget. Resumes from on subsequent calls. When all seeds are processed, clears the list and resets the index to -1.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.pickNextCapability
member.summary: Among triggered capabilities, picks the one to process next. Prefers uncertain capabilities (they degrade rejection accuracy), then within each group picks the one with fewest pending seeds (cheapest to resolve first). Ties broken by lowest bit index.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.cleanChunkForDfs(System.Int32,Mafi.Collections.ReadonlyCollections.ReadOnlyArray{Mafi.Option{Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ClearancePathabilityProvider.DataChunk}})
member.summary: Cleans a dirty chunk by ensuring its tile data and PF node data are up to date. Returns true if the chunk was fully cleaned, false if PF node cleaning was deferred because the chunk has nodes visited by an active pathfinding search.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.retryDeferredDirtyChunks(System.Int32@)
member.summary: Retries cleaning chunks that were previously deferred because they had nodes visited by an active pathfinder. Successfully cleaned chunks are re-seeded for the next DFS cycle.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.unionAllNodesInChunkIntoSuperNode(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Unions all non-destroyed nodes in the given chunk+capability that have stable component IDs into a single super-node component. Nodes that are unassigned or only explored by an in-progress DFS are skipped. Returns the (possibly updated) representative super-node component ID.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.createSuperNode(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates a super-node for a dirty chunk by unioning all nodes in the chunk and its 4 cardinal neighbors into a single component. This ensures the UF is always in the most optimistic state for connectivity queries. Returns the final super-node component ID, or if no assigned nodes were found in the chunk or any neighbor.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.onBeforeChunkDirtied(Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ClearancePathabilityProvider.DataChunk)
member.summary: Called before a DataChunk's PF data is marked dirty. Sets dirty bits for all capabilities in the chunk.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.onBeforeNodesDestroyed(Mafi.Core.PathFinding.ClearancePathabilityProvider.CapabilityChunkData)
member.summary: Called before nodes in a chunk are destroyed. If a DFS is in progress and any of these nodes had current-generation component IDs, re-enqueue them so the DFS can re-assign component IDs to the replacement nodes.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.onRoadConnectionRemoved(Mafi.Core.Roads.GraphTerrainConnection)
member.summary: When a road connection is removed, mark affected components as stale and seed for DFS rebuild. The road may have been the only link between two terrain areas.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.onRoadBecamePathable(Mafi.Core.Roads.IRoadGraphEntity)
member.summary: When a terrain connector entity becomes pathable (e.g. construction completed), immediately merge its own terrain tiles and defer the road graph search to where the full graph is available.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.onRoadBecameUnpathable(Mafi.Core.Roads.IRoadGraphEntity)
member.summary: When a terrain connector entity becomes non-pathable (e.g. deconstruction started, paused), mark their terrain connection components as stale.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.snapshotStableUF
member.summary: Snapshots the active UF for query-side use. Called at DFS completion (when no compaction is needed) or at compaction finalization.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.beginCompaction
member.summary: Starts time-sliced compaction of the active capability's UF. Subsequent calls to in simUpdate will process chunks incrementally.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.continueCompaction(System.Int32@)
member.summary: Continues time-sliced compaction, consuming up to budget units. Walks chunks from , performing root mapping and ID update in a single pass per node. Only processes nodes of .

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.convertToCornerSpace(Mafi.Tile2i,System.Int32)
member.summary: Converts a road connection tile (center space) to corner tile space for the given capability. Matches VehiclePathFinder's ConvertToCornerTileSpace usage at road shortcut destinations.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.expandViaRoads(Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePfNode,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32@)
member.summary: Expands DFS through road connections at the given node. Searches the road graph to find terrain tiles reachable via roads and enqueues them with the same component.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.addSeedAndTriggerUncertain(Mafi.Tile2i,System.Int32)
member.summary: Adds a seed tile and marks the capability as needing a DFS cycle. It also marks the capability as uncertain, which means the DFS will treat any queries for it as pathable.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.isNodeOnChunkBoundary(Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclePfNode)
member.summary: Returns true if the node's area touches a chunk boundary (edge of an 8x8 chunk). Used to filter auto-seeding to boundary nodes only; interior nodes are reached through DFS expansion from boundary seeds.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.getEncodedIndexFromTile(Mafi.Tile2i,System.Int32)
member.summary: Computes the encoded index for chunk+capability from a tile coordinate.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.searchRoadConnections(Mafi.Core.Roads.GraphTerrainConnection,System.Int32@,Mafi.Collections.ImmutableCollections.ImmutableArray{Mafi.Core.Roads.RoadGraphPath}@)
member.summary: Searches the road graph from a terrain-road connection, dispatching to exits or entrances based on the connection direction.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.getRoadDestinationTile(Mafi.Core.Roads.GraphTerrainConnection,Mafi.Core.Roads.RoadGraphPath)
member.summary: Returns the terrain tile at the other end of a road path, based on the connection direction.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.TriggerFullRebuild_TEST_ONLY
member.summary: Triggers a full rebuild by marking all existing chunks as seeds. Used by tests to establish initial component IDs.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PathFinding.VehiclesConnectivityManager.RemovePendingSeedsInRange_TEST_ONLY(System.Int32,Mafi.Tile2i,Mafi.Tile2i)

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Population.UpointsCategoryProto
member.summary:
Used for statistics and overview table in UI.
It works the following way: Each stats entry has to have UpointsCategoryProto assigned, this give it place in overview. However each has to have UpointsStatsCategoryProto assigned as well (but it can be this). - This one is used for further grouping in statistics (history chart data).
Example: A) Boost has UpointsCategoryProto called Boost, and UpointsStatsCategoryProto this (Boost again). So for Furnace and Boiler boost we get this: Table: - Boost => tooltip showing Furnace and Boiler boost Stats: - Boost B) Settlement service has each own special UpointsCategoryProto but they all share UpointsStatsCategoryProto So for Water and Food we get this: Table: - Water - Food Stats: - Services (containing sum of Water and Food).

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Population.UpointsCategoryProto.HideCount
member.summary: If true, UI will sum up all the values under same UpointsCategoryProto but it won't prefix the count. Used for services for instance as count does not make much sense there.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Products.CountableProductProto.Gfx.GetStackingOffsets(System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Returns 5 values determining how stacked products should be offset. These offsets are computed from the corner of the tile, to avoid having to deal with negative numbers in the packing code. First two represent the stacking for 2 items. Final three represent the stacking for 3 items. It is assumed that a single item goes in the middle of the tile (ie. 0.5, 0.5 offset)

member.summary: Path for the prefab that specifies the mesh and material to use.

member.remarks: We specify texture separately to be able to override it easily during runtime.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Products.DetailLayerSpecProto.UniformRandomSpawnProbability
member.summary: Spawn probability range (0, 1), max value is .

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Products.DetailLayerSpecProto.YOffsetWeightMult
member.summary: How much detail weight affects y-offset. Value of 1.0 means that weight of 30% will lower detail by 70% of tile. Value of 0.5 means that weight of 30% will lower detail by only 35% of tile.
member.remarks: Y-offset multiplier is here, not on variant proto, because we don't know variant index during update operation.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Products.DetailLayerSpecProto.WindSensitivity
member.summary: Sensitivity to wind. If set to 0, no wind animation is performed.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Products.DetailLayerSpecProto.UpNormalWeight
member.summary: Weight of UP normal compared to the true mesh normal. When set to 0, mesh normal will be used. When set to 1, all normals will be overriden wh UP normal. Linear interpolation is used in between. This is used to make grass and other billboard better blend with terrain.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Products.DetailLayerSpecProto.DetailVariant.PositionRandomnessOverrSbyteMaxMult
member.summary: Precomputed value for PositionRandomness / (sbyte.MaxValue + 1).

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Products.DetailLayerSpecProto.DetailVariant.ScaleVariationOverShortMaxMult
member.summary: Precomputed value for ScaleVariation / (short.MaxValue + 1).

member.summary: FYI: This is kinda tricky. We used to show globally stored quantity to the player but that was missing things like food because globally stored quantity is used also for quick buy and we don't want to remove food from settlement. So we started using global quantity but people found that confusing. So compromise is this. It shows globally stored quantity but also add in stored quantity that is not available for quick removal.

member.summary: Minimum height difference between neighboring tiles at which this material can collapse.
member.remarks: The probability of terrain collapsing is 0% at (or lower) and 100% at (and higher). The probability is linear interpolated between. Terrain tile is considered for collapsing only when its height or height of its neighbors is changed.

member.summary: Maximum height difference neighboring tiles at which this material can say without collapsing. Terrain always collapses if the height difference is greater than this value.
member.remarks: To make the material less likely to collapse set this value to high value (like 10-100).

member.summary: Special group that allows to apply modifier to the base value using CombineModifiersInGroup instead of via ApplyModifier. This for instance allows to redefine the initial value of Percent set to zero where multiplication wouldn't work.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.PropertiesDb.PropertyModifier`1.Group
member.summary: Modifiers in the same group can have different aggregation semantics. Read more info in Property implementations you are using.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.PropertiesDb.PropertyModifiers.Delta(Mafi.Percent,System.String,Mafi.Option{System.String})
member.summary: Set relative values such as -20% or +10%. That will be mapped to multipliers or not depends what the property is implementing. Read documentation for and to understand how this works.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.PropertiesDb.PropertyPercentMult
member.summary:
Applies modifiers in multiplicative way but between groups they get summed before applied.
NOTE: You need to provide deltas not absolute values. Initial value is typically 100% (0% would not make sense due to multiplications).
Example: Initial value: 100% + GameDifficultyGroup: +20% + MaintenanceEdictGroup: -5%, -15% Transformed into the following (adds 100% because values are deltas): + GameDifficultyGroup => 20% + 100% => 120% + MaintenanceEdictGroup: -5% + (-15%) + 100% => 80% And applied like this: 100% (base) * 120% (diff) * 80% (edicts) => 96%
You set same group for modifiers that should be summed up together. That is typically used for additive bonuses like edicts. Otherwise you should prefer multiplication of modifiers to make sure that they scale accordingly.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.PropertiesDb.PropertyPercentSum
member.summary:
Sum property just simply sums up all its modifiers. Groups don't actually matter in this case.
This is used a bit more rarely in cases where multiplier makes no sense.
Initial value is typically 0% or some other initial value.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Research.ResearchNodeProto
member.summary:
Represents a single item of research tree that can be unlocked by the player.
One ResearchProto consists of multiple that provide the individual research improvements.
For instance node proto called Nuclear energy can have following atomic protos: - Unlock: Nuclear reactor - Unlock: Nuclear waste truck - Permanent bonus: +5% company rating
And following required products: - 1 tons of Uranium per step - 1 Advanced Turbines per step - 10 Coolers per step

member.summary: Fills list with occupied tiles. Does not clear list.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Terrain.Props.TerrainPropsManager.PruneLevitatingProps(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Removes any props (rocks, decorations) whose terrain has shifted too far from their placement height, which would otherwise leave them levitating or buried. Called automatically on save load and from the recovery save loader after bulk terrain diffs are applied (those bypass per-tile height-change events that would otherwise prune individual props).

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Terrain.Props.TerrainPropsManager.PrunePropsOnCustomSurfaces
member.summary: Removes props (rocks, bushes, etc.) whose occupied tiles include any tile with a custom surface (concrete, asphalt). Used after recovery save load: the world map regenerates with procedurally placed props, then the saved terrain diffs restore custom surfaces, leaving props sitting on top of paved areas.

member.summary: Adds prop to the terrain. This should be only used by during map generation.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Terrain.Designation.SurfaceDesignationsManager.Reserve(Mafi.Core.Vehicles.Jobs.IVehicleJobWithOwner,Mafi.Core.Terrain.Designation.SurfaceDesignation)
member.summary: Despite designation can require multiple products and the truck can deliver just single type, we still reserve all the products. Because we don't allow designations to be reserved by multiple trucks and if we would leave that quantity hanging it would trigger "fake" designations searches.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Terrain.Designation.TerrainDesignation
member.summary: Terrain height designation.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Terrain.Designation.TerrainDesignation.SIZE_BITS
member.summary: Designations size in bits.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Terrain.Designation.TerrainDesignation.SIZE_TILES
member.summary: Designation size in tiles is 4.
member.remarks:
By making default terrain designation larger than 1x1 we decrease micro-management with terrain designation, make the visualization nicer, and mining less scary. Designation size also sets the steepness of ramps.
The size 4x4 was chosen so that they are aligned with terrain chunks to make rendering more efficient and to allow more flexibility during mine designation. Other options were 2x2 which is too small and 8x8 which is too large.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Terrain.Designation.TerrainDesignation.IsReadyToDump(System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Whether this designation is not fulfilled for dumping and also ready to be dumped on at the same time. When is true, amphibious ocean dumping is restricted to targets at or below sea level (). This prevents above-water ocean designations (e.g. islands) from showing as "ready" (green) in the renderer, which would confuse players using non-amphibious trucks. For actual gameplay logic, amphibious trucks can dump anywhere in the ocean since material falls through water.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Terrain.Designation.TerrainDumpingManager.m_eligibleDesignationsCached
member.summary:
These caches can bring tremendous savings when searching for dumping designations frequently.
For instance VehicleBuffersRegistry can search a lot. Because scanning large designated areas per several output buffers per 30+ vehicles adds up a lot.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Terrain.Designation.TerrainDumpingManager.HasEligibleDumpingDesignationsFor(Mafi.Core.Products.LooseProductProto)
member.summary: This is only used for goals and should stay like that is its performance is not optimized.

Important: This method has two different ways in how it searches for designations. 1.IF towersToEnforce is set: Searching ONLY in towersToEnforce 2.ELSE: Searching global designations + designations under towers that allow global input.
This is optimized so that if the method is run twice for both 1. and 2. it avoids doing redundant work which improves performance.
This is split because typically trucks first search their towers, then ore sorters and then all global dumping places. So they need to call this twice. When dumping from a truck assigned to buffer, we either dump into assigned towers or globally.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Terrain.Designation.TerrainDumpingManager.AddProductToDump(Mafi.Core.Products.LooseProductProto)
member.summary: Makes the given product to be allowed to be dumped.

member.summary: Removes the given product from the allowed dumpable products.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Terrain.Designation.TerrainDumpingManager.getAllEligibleCachedFor(Mafi.Core.Buildings.Mine.MineTower,System.Boolean)
member.summary: In many cases, caching per mine tower can reduce overall budget spent on searching by 75%.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Terrain.Designation.UnreachableTerrainDesignationsManager
member.summary: Manages unreachable designations for vehicles.
member.remarks:
Anyone (typically goals) can report destinations are unreachable. These are cached and can be filtered out when searching for new jobs.
Caches are cleared only after some time and only if a vehicle becomes idle. Thus it is important to call ReportVehicleIdle to update this manager. Also caches can be cleared in several phases.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Terrain.Designation.UnreachableTerrainDesignationsManager.VehicleData.CLEAR_PHASES
member.summary: If we have more than items to clear we clear in phases. This defines how many phases to use.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Terrain.Designation.UnreachableTerrainDesignationsManager.VehicleData.m_entitiesCleared
member.summary: We have cleared entities set to be able to "soft" remove them. Entities in the cleared set are not returned as unreachable when searching for jobs. But they still count toward entity's unreachable entities count. This way we prevent flickering notifications when we from time to time clear unreachable entities to test them again.

member.summary: Returns total number of generators that should be spawned in between terrain cells using according to probabilities given by . This is queried only once before any generators are spawned.
member.remarks: It may happen that not all generators can be spawned if there is not enough of eligible edges. Spawning is probabilistic with limited number of trials.

member.summary: Returns probability of spawning this resource on a border between the two given neighboring cell. Only one generator is allowed at each border.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Terrain.Generation.ICellEdgeResourceGeneratorFactory.Spawn(Mafi.Core.Map.CellEdge,Mafi.Fix32)
member.summary: Creates resource generator for an edge between the two given neighboring cells. The multiplier should control amount of the resource. Value 2.0 should spawn generator that creates roughly 2x of the resource. IMPORTANT: The multiplier already captures compensation for the cell size, distance from the original cell, and player settings.
