member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Terrain.Generation.TerrainGeneratorV2.writeChunkToTerrain(Mafi.Core.Terrain.Generation.TerrainGeneratorChunkData,Mafi.Core.Terrain.Generation.TerrainGenerationContext)
member.summary: Transfers data from chunk data structure to terrain data safely and efficiently.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Terrain.Generation.TerrainPostProcessorParallelizationStrategy.AnalyzeAllThenApply
member.summary: All chunks are analyzed in parallel and then all are applied on the main thread. Use this strategy when the amount of data that needs to be saved per chunk is small and data from the entire terrain can be stored before they are applied. This maximizes parallelization of the analysis steps by having only one sync at the end. This option also allows to have unlimited context for the analysis as no terrain data is written before all chunks are analyzed.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Terrain.Generation.TerrainPostProcessorParallelizationStrategy.AnalyzeInterleaveAndApply
member.summary: Analysis happens in parallel in stages. In the first stage, one or more rows of chunks are analyzed in parallel and in the second stage the data from all but the last row is applied. This then repeats for the rest of rows. This ensures that the analysis has at least one chunk worth of context available (terrain around analyzed chunk is guaranteed to not have changes applied). The advantage is that the changes are being applied gradually and their memory can be freed or reused. Disadvantage is that threads that perform the analysis have to be synced in between stages. Note that when the generated terrain area is small enough, could be used to improve performance.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Terrain.Generation.TerrainPostProcessorParallelizationStrategy.CustomSchedule
member.summary: Parallelization is handled by the post-processor. Use for this option.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Terrain.Physics.TerrainPhysicsSimulator.DIAGONAL_CHECK_PROBABILITY
member.summary:
Specifies probability of testing collapse diagonally.
By trying to expand diagonally only some time, pile spreads in more circular way. If diagonals are tested only ~30% of the time, we not only save on perf but the result looks better than ever!

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager
member.summary: Terrain manager controls the implementation details of infinite chunk-based terrain and hides the most of terrain-tiling mechanics while offering interface that uses global coordinates to interact with the terrain. NOTE: All write-actions have to be performed on the simulation thread.
member.remarks:
PERF: To eliminate implicit array bounds checks do the following (more details in JitTests):
TerrainData arr = m_data.Array; // 1) Cache queried array. Even a member array must be cached. if ((unchecked((uint)i) >= (uint)arr.Length) { // 2) Combined array-length check, do not check null if not needed. Log.Error("Bad index bro"); // 3) Do not format error message if not absolutely necessary. return default; } return arr[i]; // 4) Index into array with checked index. Wrapped index in a struct is OK.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.TERRAIN_DIMENSION_MULTIPLE
member.summary: Terrain dimension must be multiple of this value (256). Terrain dimension is guaranteed to be divisible by this number without remainder for easy chunking without the need for checking edge cases.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.TERRAIN_DIMENSION_MIN
member.summary: Minimum terrain size is naturally 1 dimension multiple.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.TERRAIN_DIMENSION_MULTIPLE_MASK
member.summary: Masks terrain size bits that must be zero.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.MIN_OFF_LIMITS_SIZE
member.summary: Size of the off-limits region around the map. This region makes some checks more efficient since we are able to detect when the edge of map is near. Off-limit tiles are flagged with .

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.TERRAIN_DIMENSION_MAX
member.summary: Maximum value for a terrain dimension is 32768 (2^16).
member.remarks: Only 15 bits is used so that a multiplication of two max coords still fits to a signed int32 without overflow and adding +1 to a max coord won't overflow ushort. Maxing-out 65k would require being extremely careful about arithmetic operations to not cause overflows which is just not worth the effort.

members.member:
Max area is dictated by Unity restriction of max 2 GB per object on heap. With 2^26 array elements we can have arrays of structs up to 32 bytes.
The UnsafeUtility.Malloc can be used to allocate larger arrays if needed.v

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.HEIGHT_JUST_ABOVE_OCEAN
member.summary: Default height used in .

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.BedrockLayerThicknessDefault
member.summary: Whenever bedrock layer is returned it will have this thickness. Note that bedrock has unlimited thickness. This value is used instead of to avoid overflow issues. This value was also chosen to fit to .

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.FourSideNeighborsDeltas
member.summary: Deltas of four neighbors in cache-friendly order: -X, +X, -Y, +Y. Use this to iterate through neighbors efficiently. WARNING: You must be sure that this delta is applied to a tile that is NOT on the map boundary.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.FourSideNeighborsDeltasIndices
member.summary: Delta indices of four neighbors in cache-friendly order: -X, +X, -Y, +Y. Use this to iterate through neighbors efficiently. WARNING: You must be sure that this delta is applied to a tile that is NOT on the map boundary.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.FourCornerNeighborsDeltas
member.summary: Delta indices of four neighbors in cache-friendly order: (-X, -Y), (+X, -Y), (-X, +Y), (+X, +Y). Use this to iterate through neighbors efficiently. WARNING: You must be sure that this delta is applied to a tile that is NOT on the map boundary.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.EightNeighborsDeltas
member.summary: Delta indices of eight neighbors in cache-friendly order: (-X, -Y), (0, -Y), (+X, -Y), (-X, 0), (+X, 0), (-X, +Y), (0, +Y), (+X, +Y). Use this to iterate through neighbors efficiently. WARNING: You must be sure that this delta is applied to a tile that is NOT on the map boundary.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.FourTileCornersDeltas
member.summary: Delta indices of four tile corners in cache-friendly order: self, +X, +Y, +XY. Use this to iterate through tile vertices efficiently. WARNING: You must be sure that this delta is applied to a tile that is NOT on the map boundary.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.Bedrock
member.summary: Bedrock is a default material that is not explicitly stored in tile layers data. Tile with no material layers will have this material.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.TerrainDataGenerated
member.summary: Called when terrain gets regenerated during new game or during game load (bool is true when loaded). When new game is created, this is called in NewGameCreated stage. When the game is loaded, this is called in the InitAfterLoad phase with priority InitPriority.High.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.TerrainGeneratedButNotLoaded
member.summary: Invoked at the end of terrain generation, before loaded data are applied to the terrain. This can be used to apply loaded data such as removing trees and props.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.HeightChanged
member.summary: Invoked when height of a tile is changed. This also means that relative layer thicknesses are likely changed. Called on simulation thread.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.TileMaterialsOnlyChanged
member.summary: Invoked when only tile material layers are changed (and not height). This is to save on height processing of height when height actually did not change (such as dirt changing to grass). Called on simulation thread.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.TileFlagsChanged
member.summary: Invoked when is changed.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.OceanFlagChanged
member.summary: Invoked when ocean tile flag is changed. This event is special since multiple classes listen to only ocean changes and it is more efficient to only make them process this event instead of the .

member.name: E:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.TileCustomSurfaceChangedUiOnly
member.summary: UI-only variant of . Fired by bulk-load paths (e.g. recovery save import) that wrote surface data via and need the renderer to refresh its surface texture buffers without invoking sim-side subscribers. The renderer subscribes to this event in addition to the regular one.

member.summary: Masks flags that blocks vehicles (path-finding).

member.summary: Number of assigned tile flags.

member.summary: All created flags reporters.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.TerrainMaterials
member.summary: All terrain materials that can be indexed by their . Shorthand for .

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.DisruptedMaterialIds
member.summary: Converts material ID to disrupted material ID. If material cannot be disrupted, it's ID stays the same.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.RecoveredMaterialIds
member.summary: Converts material ID to recovered material ID. If material cannot be recovered, it's ID stays the same.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.DisruptedMaterialParentsIds
member.summary: Converts material ID to the material IDs that disrupt into it. This is not the same as `RecoveredMaterialIds` as the material like rock disrupted does not recover to rock, but rock its it's disruption parent. Note that multiple materials can disrupt into one so the result is an array. If the material cannot be result of disruption, the array will be empty.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.InitialMapCreationSaveVersion
member.summary: Save version when the map was initially created. This allows to make backwards-compatible changes to the map generation code.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.SavedFlagsMask
member.summary: Mask of flag bits that are persisted across save/load. Bits outside this mask belong to runtime-only reporters (registered with isSaved: false) and must not be round-tripped through any save format - including recovery saves - or the live state diverges from the flag bits and physics/path-finding queries silently break.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.GetMutableTerrainDataRef
member.summary: Returns reference mutable terrain data. You must retain the ref, passing this by value will result in errors as data won't be properly stored. Be extremely careful with this one.

member.name: E:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.ChunkReloadRequiredUiOnly
member.summary: Signals that a whole chunk has been bulk-modified outside the normal per-tile event flow and renderers should reload its data. UI/rendering only, simulation listeners must not subscribe.

member.name: E:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.FlushChunkUpdatesUiOnly
member.summary: Signals that all previously-signalled chunk reloads should be processed synchronously right now instead of being streamed out over future sim/render frames. UI/rendering only, simulation listeners must not subscribe. Used by recovery save import so the loading screen covers the full renderer update and the player never sees stale chunks flash in.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.repairBrokenTerrainSimulators(System.Int32,Mafi.DependencyResolver)
member.summary: Pre-V305 recovery loads serialized no-op simulators into the save. Swap them back to the real ones on load. Delete this once the minimum supported save version reaches V304.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.RepairBrokenTerrainSimulators(Mafi.DependencyResolver)
member.summary: Inner repair body, exposed for testing. Production callers should go through the entry point above which gates on the saved version.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.IsAreaFullySurfaced(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns true if all tiles in the given area have custom surfaces that cover the terrain. The area is asymmetric: (2*maxRadius+2)^2 tiles from (-R-1,-R-1) to (R,R) relative to center, covering all tiles that share vertices with the disrupted vertex grid. A tile counts as covered only if it has a surface AND the terrain height is not above the surface.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.TryGetTileSurface(Mafi.Tile2iIndex,Mafi.Core.Terrain.TileSurfaceData@)
member.summary: PERF note: is slightly faster, avoid need for extra returned bool, which can be obtained via .

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.SetTileDataNoEvents(System.Int32,Mafi.HeightTilesF,Mafi.Core.Terrain.TileSurfaceData,System.UInt16,Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainMaterialThicknessSlim[],System.Int32)
member.summary: Sets all tile data (height, surface, flags, material layers) without firing any events. Used by recovery save import for bulk terrain restoration. The caller must call after all tiles are set, and should fire for each affected chunk so the renderers pick up the new per-tile state without going through simulation-side per-tile events.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.InvokeChunkReloadRequiredUiOnly(Mafi.Chunk2i)
member.summary: Fires for the given chunk. Expected to be called once per affected chunk after a bulk terrain write.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.InvokeFlushChunkUpdatesUiOnly
member.summary: Fires . Expected to be called once after all per-chunk reload notifications so the renderers can drain their pending update queues synchronously before control returns to the load loop.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.ValidateAndFixTileData(Mafi.Tile2iIndex,Mafi.Collections.Lyst{System.String})
member.summary:
Performs following validations and fixes:
Check layers count to be non-negative, set to zero if negative.
Check each layer for phantom material, remove phantoms.
Check each layer for invalid material ID, remove offending layers.
Check each layer non-positive thickness, remove offending layers.
Check that layers beyond Count are not set, clear them otherwise.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.TerrainData.Serialize(Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainManager.TerrainData,Mafi.Serialization.BlobWriter)
member.summary: Custom terrain data serialization that is highly optimized to save bytes and be fast.
member.remarks:
Only changed tiles are saved. To avoid need of storing (x, y) coordinates of changed tiles and to make saving more performant, the changed bit structure is used for natural tile grouping by 64 (ulong). Each group writes the bit mask of changed tiles and then writes data of tiles that have bit set.
To further save data and avoid writing many zeros in areas where there are no changes (nearly the entire map at the start of the game), a single byte representing sequence length is computed and written. This byte denotes whether the next group will be all zeros or all non-zeros (the changed tiles ulongs). This information is saved in the HSB. The remaining 7 bits denote length of the group. So value 0b1000_0011 means that the next group has 4 (0b11 + 1) values and each value contains changed tiles (0b1000_0000). On the other hand value 0b0000_0101 represents a group of 6 zeros and the zeros won't be even written. Note that the group length is saved as (len - 1) since we never need the value zero. This was the max group length is 128 (not just 127 :).

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainMaterialThicknessSlim
member.summary:
Slim version of . This struct is just 4 bytes: 1 byte for material ID and 3 bytes for thickness. The is 8 + 4 bytes (but needs 8 B alignment so it is 16 B effectively). This is used in terrain tile to represent layers.
This struct does not support negative values and the max positive representable value is ~2^12 = 4096. TODO: It is possible to hit thickness of this value, make this safe, possibly by reducing the fraction precision or by splitting really large layers by some thin layer of bedrock or something similar.
Use to resolve this to full .

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainTile
member.summary: Represents a terrain coordinate bundled with a terrain manager reference for easier work with tiles. Use this only where necessary, passing only or may be sufficient in many cases and is smaller in memory.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainTile.TILE_SIZE_M
member.summary: Size of an edge of a tile in meters. This serves as a conversion between tiles and meters.
member.remarks: WARNING: Many game decisions and game assets rely on this constant. DO NOT CHANGE IT!! Value of 1 is too small. Cubic tile would have only 1 m^3 and single scoop of a big excavator would take tens of them. We do not need to be this detailed. Also things like path finding that works with tiles would be slower due to additional work. Value of 2 seems good compromise with a nice property that 2 divides 10, so we can measure tens of meters by highlighting tiles. Value of 3 is weird and 4 is too much and does not divide 10.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TerrainTile.TILE_AREA_M

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TileSurfaceData.RawValue
member.summary:
Raw data (signed) are packed in the following way (from LSB to HSB):
[0-7] (8) surface ID, zero means no surface. [8-9] (2) texture rotation. [10] (1) is auto-placed. [11] (1) is ramp on/off [12-13] (2) ramp rotation [14] (1) can't be placed [15-16] (2) unused. Leave some bits for scale? Could steal them off decal! [17-18] (2) ramp vertical offset {1|3|5|7}/8 tiles. [19-31] (13) signed min height as int, +- 4k range

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Terrain.TileSurfaceData.RawValueDecal
member.summary:
Raw data (signed) are packed in the following way (from LSB to HSB):
[0-7] (8) decal ID, zero means no decal. [8] (1) decal flipped bit. [9-10] (2) decal rotation. [11-13] (3) 3-bit color key [14-31] (18) unused.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.World.Loans.ActiveLoan.LOAN_CAP_MULTIPLIER_FOR_INTEREST
member.summary: If player accumulates interest that is 5 times the original amount, we stop charging the interest rate until loan is repaid in full. This prevents edge cases such as accumulating insanely massive debts. The benefit of this is that we don't discourage repayments as the interest rate is zero.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.World.WorldMapCargoManager.CalculateCapacityUtilization(Mafi.Core.Buildings.Cargo.Ships.CargoShipV2)
member.summary:
This will pair this ship and all world entities and figure out what is the maximal capacity percentage utilization. So if we have capacity for 100 oil and there is mine with 30 we get 30% utilization. Because this is max this means that no other module has more than 30% utilization. This server to figure out whether ship should depart or not.
Note: This takes some time so don't call it too often.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.SaveGame.ConfigsSerialization
member.summary:
V2/V3 configs chunk serialization. Each is written as a length-prefixed self-contained blob with its own object/type reference scope. This makes unknown configs (from removed mods) trivially skippable - their length is known up-front and their internal string/object references cannot pollute the parent reader's reference tables.
The V2 format had a known issue: configs were not registered in the parent writer's back-reference table, so any reference to a config from outside its blob (other configs in their own blobs, or fields on classes serialized in the resolver section) got inlined as a fresh duplicate instead of resolving to the canonical instance. V3 fixes this by registering each successfully-handled config in the parent's back-reference table after its blob is processed, so subsequent references become back-refs.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.SaveGame.CustomSerializer.CustomTextReader.SetProtosDb(Mafi.Core.Prototypes.ProtosDb)
member.summary: This enables protos reading.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.SaveGame.GameLoader
member.remarks:
See for loading procedure. Save file is structured in chunks. Each chunk has 8 byte header. Some chunks may be optional.
Chunk #1 (mandatory) - Serialized that has save version and compression type. This chunk is read before instance is created. - All data after this chunk are encoded with compressor based on .
Chunk #2 (mandatory) - Serialized mod types using . We need to have a list of mods at the very beginning to instantiate configs from all assemblies of all mods. - Order of mods does not matter.
TODO: This is no longer a valid requirement, and if someone subscribes to a config for its changes they end up being resolved without a working resolver thanks to this foot-gun. Example is subscribing a LoansManager on DifficultyConfig event for config changes. Most likely the configs should stop receiving this special treatment. Chunk #3 (mandatory) - All persistent configs (implementations of that are serializable). We need all configs to be able to instantiate mods and create dependency resolver. - Serialized simply as .
Chunk #4 (mandatory) - Saved instance of that effectively saves the entire game.
Chunk #5 (mandatory) - Final chunk that verifies correctness of the load operation. This chunk has no contents, just a header.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.SaveGame.GameLoader.MAX_TRIAL_SKIP_BYTES
member.summary: Maximum number of bytes to try skipping for a single unknown config's data. Config data contains only primitives so this should be more than enough.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.SaveGame.GameLoader.deserializeConfigsWithUnknownTypeHandling(Mafi.Serialization.BlobReader)
member.summary:
Deserializes configs, gracefully skipping unknown config types from removed mods. Uses trial-skip to determine the byte size of unknown config data.
IMPORTANT: This only works if the unknown config's serialized data contains no object or string references, only primitive types (int, bool, float, etc.) and value-type structs. If the unknown config wrote objects/strings, the reader's object reference table (m_readObjects) would be corrupted because the skipped bytes would leave unregistered entries, causing all subsequent ID-based lookups to be off. Configs from mods are expected to only contain primitive fields.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.SaveGame.GameLoader.trySkipUnknownConfigs(Mafi.Serialization.BlobReader,System.Int32,System.Int32,Mafi.Collections.Lyst{System.Int32},System.Int32,Mafi.Collections.Lyst{System.ValueTuple{System.Object,System.Action{System.Object,Mafi.Serialization.BlobReader}}},System.Int32)
member.summary:
Recursively tries different byte skip amounts for unknown configs, validating by checking that known config data deserializes correctly and HEADER_RESOLVER follows all config data.
This approach is safe because the unknown config's data only contains primitives, no object or string references that would alter the reader's m_readObjects/m_readTypes tables. This means skipping bytes only affects stream position, and failed trials can be cleanly rolled back via . If a wrong skip is tried, subsequent known config deserialization will either throw (wrong bytes) or land on wrong data that fails the HEADER_RESOLVER validation at the end.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.SaveGame.GameLoader.FinishGameLoadAndDisposeTimeSliced(Mafi.Serialization.BlobReader,Mafi.DependencyResolverBuilder,Mafi.Core.Game.SpecialSerializerFactories,System.Action{Mafi.DependencyResolver},System.Action{Mafi.DependencyResolver},System.Boolean,System.Nullable{System.UInt64})
member.summary: Returns a loaded resolver.
member.remarks: Taking as an argument ensures that the new resolver was not used for resolving yet.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.SaveGame.SaveHeaders.HEADER_CONFIGS_V2
member.summary:
Marks a chunk that contains configs in the V2 format: each config is written as a length-prefixed self-contained blob with its own object/type reference scope. Unknown configs (from removed mods) are trivially skippable since their byte length is known up-front and their internal references cannot pollute the parent reader's tables. Distinct header (rather than relying on save version) so the V2 format can be back-ported to older branches without bumping save version - loaders only need to branch on the chunk header.
V2 has a known issue: configs are not in the parent writer's back-reference table, so any reference from outside the blob (other configs, or fields on classes serialized in the resolver section) gets inlined as a fresh copy instead of resolving to the canonical instance. V3 below fixes this.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.SaveGame.SaveHeaders.HEADER_CONFIGS_V3
member.summary: Same blob layout as V2 but each successfully-deserialized config is also registered in the parent reader's back-reference table (and symmetrically on the writer side). This preserves object identity for any reference to a config from outside its own blob, fixing the V2 staleness bug. Skipped blobs (unknown types from removed mods) register a null placeholder to keep IDs aligned.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.SaveGame.SaveHeaders.HEADER_RESOLVER
member.summary: Marks a chunk that contains data of the game as serialized resolver.

member.summary: Marks the total end of the save file.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train
member.summary: Logical grouping of that forms contiguous train. This class handles movement logic, track reservations, and (un)loading.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.ReservationHelper.ResetSearch
member.summary: Resets reservation search to initial state. Can be called at any point and should be used whenever path/reserved blocks may have changed.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.ReservationHelper.TryReserveNextBlocksOnPath(Mafi.Collections.Stak{Mafi.Core.Trains.TrackPathRecord},System.Boolean@,System.Boolean@,System.Boolean@,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Tries to reserve at least one block along the path, taking into account critical sections.
member.returns: Number of newly reserved blocks.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.VALIDATE_WITH_GRAPH_MANAGER_INTERVAL_TICKS
member.summary: In non-debug builds, each train validates once every this many ticks. TrainId is used to stagger validations across ticks so they don't all fire on the same step.
member.remarks: Validation cost is roughly equal to train routing cost, so 1/256 is very tiny overhead and performs validation every 25.6 seconds on every train (time-sliced).

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.PATH_RETRY_COOLDOWN
member.summary: Duration between repeated pathfinding attempts when failing to find a path. Not always respected.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.TIME_BEFORE_NO_PATH_NOTIF
member.summary: If a train is stopping then reversing we need a little time to figure out the new path.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.SELF_INTERSECTION_SAFETY_SCALE
member.summary: Amount to scale the train length by when considering self intersection. Trains already have to be over 85 tiles to possibly self intersect, so we don't need a large scale. Worst case we get slight clipping at the tail for a perfectly sized problem.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.MAX_THROTTLE_RAMP_PER_STEP
member.summary: Max throttle increase per step. Reduces fun factor a bit, but makes sounds smoother and removes some cases where we jam on 100% throttle only to drop it again very soon (eg. aligning)

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.MAX_SPEED
member.summary: Maximum speed for any train.
member.remarks: We may be able to increase, but check that following things are not broken: * Train visualization (fast trains should not be allowed on tight turns). * Train movement logic can keep up. * Block reservation can keep up.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.BRAKING_SAFETY_MARGIN
member.summary: A factor to multiply braking distance by to make sure we brake in time.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.FOLLOWING_SAFETY_MARGIN
member.summary: A factor to multiply our braking distance by to check if we are guaranteed to clear an area for other trains.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.SPEED_MODULATION_SPACE_FACTOR
member.summary: This controls how smoothly we approach things where we expect to have to slow down. Values closer to 1 will keep throttle high until the point where we absolutely have to brake. Larger values ease off throttle as we approach a likely braking point.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.MAX_ROLL_DRAG_SPEED
member.summary: Speed at which maximum roll drag is applied. Roll drag is scaled by speed to avoid it causing negative force when train is stationary.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.ALIGNMENT_THRESHOLD
member.summary: Distance at which station alignment is considered complete.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.ALIGNMENT_THRESHOLD_CREEP
member.summary: Distance at which station alignment speed eases down.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.NOTIFY_APPROACHING_DISTANCE_MULT
member.summary: Multiplier of braking distance for notifications of train approaching.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.NOTIFY_APPROACHING_MAX_INTERVAL
member.summary: Maximum interval of events when the train is moving. The faster train moves, the smaller interval (to prevent missed notifications).

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.NOT_APPROACHING_TIMEOUT
member.summary: When a train does not notify approach for this duration, the entity listening for the approach events can consider no longer being approached by the train (e.g. the train re-planned its path or stopped early).

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.ArrivalNotMetItemForUi
member.summary: As train can skip schedule items, this helps the UI to show the right schedule item that caused arrival conditions to not be met.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.TrainCars
member.summary: Train cars of the train in the order of how the train was initially designed. Cars with sub-cars only appear once. Indexed by .

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.TrainSubCars
member.summary: Train cars of the train in the order the train was initially designed. Car with sub-cars have an entry for each sub-car.

member.summary: Locomotives of the train in the order of how the train was initially designed.

member.summary: Locomotives of the train in the order of how the train was initially designed.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.SubCargoWagons
member.summary: All sub-cargo wagons across all cargo wagons, in design order.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.FuelTypes
member.summary: Distinct primary fuel products across all locomotives. Empty for fully electric trains. Computed once when the train is assembled; never saved.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.TrainCarsDataInDriveOrder
member.summary: Train cars data ordered by the current train direction such that the first element is always the front wagon in the travelling direction.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.IsReversed
member.summary: Whether the train is currently travelling "backwards".

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.LifetimeDistanceTraveled
member.summary: Odometer.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.LifetimeLoadedQuantity
member.summary: Lifetime loaded quantity statistic.

member.summary: Speed in tiles/tick.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.MaxSpeed
member.summary: Maximum speed in the current train direction.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.SpeedLimit
member.summary: Maximum speed of the train given the route it plans on taking.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.m_speedOfTrainAhead
member.summary: If we fail to reserve a block this is set to the speed of the train occupying that block, if applicable. Due to execution order it is not 100% accurate - this train may be computed before or after the other train, or you may be accessing it before it is computed for this step.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.Acceleration
member.summary: Acceleration in tiles/tick^2.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.SpeedLimitAhead
member.summary: The minimum raw speed limit from reserved blocks ahead (not envelope-adjusted). Used to determine when to coast (cut throttle) before reaching a speed-limited section.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.ClearanceThrottle
member.summary: Throttle that controls train slowing down due to insufficient clearance. When there is sufficient clearance, of if clearance is not considered (e.g. during manual driving), this value will be 100%.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.BrakingForceKnCurrent
member.summary: Current braking force. Always non-negative.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.GradeForceKn
member.summary: Current force on the train due to grade in kN (in direction of the train travel).

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.AirDragKn
member.summary: Current force on the train due to air drag in kN (in direction of the train travel).

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.AccelerationForceKnCurrent
member.summary: Force accelerating the train. Always non-negative.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.TractiveForceKnCombinedSmooth
member.summary: Smooth tractive force, equal to when positive, equal to minus when negative.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.RollDragKn
member.summary: Actual value of rolling resistance.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.RollDragWhenMoving
member.summary: Pre-computed current rolling resistance of the entire train (speed invariant).

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.MassTons
member.summary: Total current mass of the train.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.Waypoints
member.summary:
List of waypoints of as a queue oriented in the same order as train cars in , i.e. first train car is near the queue head, last one is near queue tail.
When the train is moving forwards, waypoint indices of cars are decreasing and new waypoints are pushed to the beginning of the queue.
Queue 0.......10........20......n [CAR1]=[CAR2]=[CAR3]

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.OccupiedBlocks
member.summary:
Occupied blocks by this train. While occupied blocks are also reserved, all reserved blocks are kept in separate list and are not in this list.
First item is at the train head.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.ReservedBlocks
member.summary: Reserved but not occupied blocks in front of this train. First item is the farthest from the train head, last item is next to the first occupied block.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.OverlapReservedBlocks
member.summary: Reserved tracks for train which are currently occupied by another train. First item is the closest to the train head. These tracks are reserved to allow trains to progress early through critical sections: it is (almost) guaranteed that they will be free and the train won't have to stop on a critical section.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.m_unreservedBlocks
member.summary: Remaining blocks of the last added track segment that were not reserved yet.
member.remarks: If there are any blocks in the unreserved queue, there must be at least one block already reserved or occupied. I.e. there cannot be an entity that has all blocks in the unreserved queue. This is because block IDs that are not reserve are not stable and may change, non-reserved entities may be removed, etc.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.m_reservedNonCriticalWaypointsCount
member.summary: Number of reserved (or occupied) non-critical waypoints. To ensure trains not stopping on critical blocks, this should not be lower than train's length. Note: this is saved, but we recompute it on load anyway. This saved value is very useful for debugging though.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.m_lastNonCriticalSectionBlock
member.summary: Last block of the current non-critical section. Where "last" is defined as the closest to the train tail. This is used to correctly shrink the non-critical section at the train tail.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.m_minNonCriticalSectionWaypointsCount
member.summary: Minimum number of waypoints for a non-critical section to be reserved.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.m_currentStation_doNotSetDirectly
member.summary: Do not set directly! Only via CurrentStation.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.CanStartDrivingToReplaceDepot
member.summary: True if a replacement is ready AND the train is free to start driving to the replace depot right now. A train in the middle of a network job must finish it first.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.StationInactiveDuration
member.summary: Time since station was last "active". Due to execution order being undefined within a sim step may be off by 1 tick.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.ForcedScheduleIndex
member.summary: Schedule index we're being forced to go to due to manual skipping.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.m_reservedNonCriticalWaypointsDirty
member.summary: At certain points we don't respect critical sections. This is typically at the end of our path. In these cases, critical section tracking can get into all sorts of weird states. Rather than handle all this complexity, if we're in a situation where we don't respect critical sections, we set this true, then recompute once we're once again wanting to respect them.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.SmartNavigateTo(Mafi.Core.Trains.IEntityWithTrainTrack)
member.summary: Starts navigation to the given entity. If this entity is too close on the path, the train may "overshoot" due to train's inertia. This function is "smart" in that if the train is on a schedule, we will consider it as requesting the train skip ahead it its schedule rather than going to explicit mode.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.NavigateTo(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Mafi.Core.Trains.IEntityWithTrainTrack})
member.summary: Starts navigation to the closest of the given entities. If this closest entity is too close on the path, the train may "overshoot" due to train's inertia.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.tryNavigateTo(Mafi.Core.Trains.ITrainScheduleItem,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Int32,Mafi.Option{Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationGroup})
member.summary: Tries to navigate to a schedule item. Returns true if pathfinding was scheduled or is already in progress.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.CanReverseTrain(System.String@)
member.summary: Whether the train can be reversed to drive "backwards". Train can turn around if all of its occupied blocks are part of bidirectional train tracks and the train is stopped.
member.remarks: Reversing does not visually affect the train or wagons, it's an internal representation change.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.TryReverseTrain(System.String@)
member.summary: Tries to reverse the train so that the last train car becomes the first one.
member.remarks: Reversing does not visually affect the train or wagons, it's an internal representation change.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.tryPullForwardInBidirectionalStation
member.summary: Pulls forward until the last occupied block is a station. If no blocks are currently a station, returns false. Returns true once conditions is satisfied.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.forceReleaseAllAlignedWagons
member.summary: Force-releases every wagon and locomotive that holds an AlignedStation reference. Used when an alignment is skipped from a state where wagons may already be aligned (the referenced module may have been destroyed or retasked). Force release is needed because CanReleaseWagon may never reach zero for a destroyed module. No-op when no wagons are aligned.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Trains.Train.forceReleaseAndSkipAlignment
member.summary: Force-releases any aligned wagons/locomotives and skips the current alignment. Use from Skip paths that may be reached while wagons are aligned (typically pre-phase or fallback skips in ). Aligning-phase-only skips do not need this since wagons are not yet aligned.

member.summary: Returns true if the current alignment index of the alignment plan has finished loading.

member.summary: Should be called when any values related to train such as mass or fullness of any train car changes.

member.summary: Returns the block ID of the occupied block that contains the given waypoint index.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainDepot.m_slotlessQueuedTrainsTmp
member.summary: Trains enqueued via that could not be given a depot slot (more trains than slots). Only populated by recovery save import. Normal gameplay never adds to this set, so the slot-missing error guards below remain effective for real bugs. Not saved: reconstructed in from the queue-vs-slots state (a queued train with no slot can only exist via recovery overflow, so the reconstruction is a no-op for normal saves and fully restores the set for saves taken mid-drain after recovery import).

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainNetwork.FindBestTrainForCandidate(Mafi.Collections.Lyst{Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainNetwork.TrainData},Mafi.Core.Products.ProductType,Mafi.Quantity,Mafi.Quantity,System.Boolean,Mafi.Tile2f,Mafi.Quantity@)
member.summary:
Selects the best idle train for a (source, dest, product) candidate. Returns the index into , or -1 if no train should be dispatched (either nothing fits or a train should wait for more cargo).
Idle trains must be sorted by capacity DESCENDING before calling.
Decision table (trains evaluated largest capacity first):
Train cap vs availQty | Train cap vs maxExch | Action -----------------------+----------------------+------------------------------- cap ≤ availQty | (any) | Dispatch FULL. Done. cap > availQty | cap ≤ maxExch | WAIT (could go full later). | | Also blocks all smaller trains. cap > availQty | cap > maxExch | Track as forced-partial candidate.
maxExch = min(source.Capacity, dest.Capacity) — the most the stations could ever exchange in one trip.
If all eligible trains fall into "forced partial" (cap > maxExch for all), the one with the smallest (cap - availQty) is dispatched.
Among trains that are equally good by capacity (full-load ties of largest cap, or forced-partial ties of smallest diff), the one nearest to the supply station is chosen so trains start trips faster.

member.remarks: A positive value means the train car is further from the head of the train than the station is from the head of the station; in other words, this alignment will cause the train to extend past the stations. Conversely a negative value means the train would not extend past the stations. This value can be multiplied by the wagon size to find the distance the train would extend past the station.

member.summary: Snapshot of at the time this plan was created. If the live group's version no longer matches, the plan's module indices are stale and the train will skip the alignment.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationAlignmentPlan.TrainIsInReverse
member.summary: If true, the train alignment indexing is for the reverse ordering of the train.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationAlignmentPlan.StationIsInReverse
member.summary: If true, the station alignment indexing is for the reverse edge of the station group.

Creates alignments between an ordered list of stations with ordered data a trains. This set of alignments should guarantee that any wagon which is compatible with the station and filters will interact with the station. The set isn't guaranteed to be optimal (ideally it's minimize alignment count and maximise total co-incidental alignments). It also does not guarantee that all modules are visited. It is implemented such that it favours alignments with higher station index, which are expected to be the first ones that trains reach.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationArrivalRule
member.summary: A rule that controls when a train station should allow trains to arrive for a specific product. The represents how aggressively trains are called, from 0% (most restrictive) to 100% (always call). The direction () determines how the threshold maps to the actual fill percentage check.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationArrivalRule.Product
member.summary: The product this rule applies to.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationArrivalRule.IsForLoading
member.summary: The module direction for this product. True = loading (station sends product out), false = unloading (station receives product).

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationArrivalRule.Threshold
member.summary: How aggressively trains are called, from 0% (most restrictive - only when empty/full) to 100% (always call). For unloading: maps directly to the fill percentage above which trains are called. For loading: inverted - a threshold of 30% means call when fill drops below 70%.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationArrivalRule.RequireFullServiceOnArrival
member.summary: When true, arrival is only allowed if the station can fully service the train for this rule's product (enough capacity to load or unload entirely).

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationArrivalRule.NetworkPriority
member.summary: Priority for train network dispatch.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationArrivalRule.LinesAlwaysAllow
member.summary: When true, trains are always allowed to arrive regardless of .

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationArrivalRule.ThresholdForNetworks
member.summary: Threshold used by train networks (independent from which is for schedules).

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationArrivalRule.IsNetworkSupplyActive(Mafi.Percent)
member.summary: Whether the given fill percentage makes this rule's station an active supplier in the network.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationArrivalRule.IsNetworkDemandActive(Mafi.Percent)
member.summary: Whether the given fill percentage makes this rule's station an active requestor in the network.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationGroup.StationAndDirection.Direction
member.summary: The direction of this station entity relative to the edge list traversal direction. Same-side modules have the same direction; opposite-side modules have a different direction. This is NOT the same as on the entity itself (which is typically Bidirectional for station modules).

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationGroup.StationGroupId
member.summary: The shared station ID of the group.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationGroup.StationRootEntity
member.summary: The root entity of the station group; the actual station.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationGroup.StationEntities
member.summary: The unordered union of the forward and reverse edge station entities.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationGroup.ForwardEdgeStationEntities
member.summary: The station entities in the group (including the root), ordered along the forward edge of the root. The first entity in this list will be the last entity the train reaches if travelling in the direction of the root's forward edge. When modules are placed on opposite sides of the track, they appear in the same list but with different values (Forward vs Backward). The entity's own remains Bidirectional - only the per-entry direction in this list reflects which side of the track the module is on.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationGroup.ReverseEdgeStationEntities
member.summary: The station entities in the group (including the root), ordered along the reverse edge of the root. The first entity in this list will be the last entity the train reaches if travelling in the direction of the root's backward edge. Same opposite-side rules as .

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationGroup.ReservedJobs
member.summary: Active jobs that have reserved capacity at this station. At a supply (loading) station, a job's quantity represents product reserved for pickup. At a demand (unloading) station, a job's quantity represents product reserved for delivery. Included in so the standard arrival system accounts for them.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationGroup.ArrivingTrains
member.summary: Cached list of trains that have reserved a slot at this station (heading here or currently here). IMPORTANT: This does not include all trains that made reservations over network

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationGroup.RemainingTrainSlotsTmp
member.summary: Remaining train slots for this group for the current dispatch tick. Used solely by TrainNetwork as a cache for better perf. Don't touch it!

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationGroup.AlignmentVersion
member.summary: Monotonically increasing counter bumped whenever the group's physical alignment surface changes - module added/removed/replaced, module direction toggled, module product cleared or changed, group rebuilt due to track direction flip. A snapshots this on creation; if the live value diverges, in-flight plans hold stale module indices and the train must skip. Changes that only affect dispatch (linked storages, arrival rule fields, train limit, allowed fuels) do NOT bump this.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationGroup.GetTotalServicingTrainsCount
member.summary: Returns the number of trains currently servicing or heading to this station.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationGroup.GetRemainingTrainSlots
member.summary: Returns how many more trains can currently be assigned to this group before hitting . Returns int.MaxValue when the limit is 0 (unlimited).

Determines whether a train should be allowed to arrive at this station.
Returns: Allowed - train can arrive and interact with this station (or pass through as noop). Denied - train could interact but conditions aren't met yet, train should wait. Skip - train can never interact with this station, advance to next schedule entry.
Decision table: Scenario | Result --------------------------------------------------------+-------- Station disabled | Denied Network train with reserved job at this station | Allowed Fuel type mismatch | Denied No rules (waypoint) | Allowed Threshold=100% and train can interact | Allowed (always call) Empty train at unload station | Allowed (pass-through noop) Full train carrying product at load station | Allowed (pass-through noop) Rule threshold met and train can interact | Allowed Rule threshold not met, train could satisfy rule | Denied Rules exist, train can't satisfy any rule | Skip Train limit reached + any of the above | Allowed becomes Denied, Skip stays Skip DoNotSkipThisStation + any of the above | Skip becomes Allowed (or Denied if at limit)
Pass-through logic: empty trains are allowed at unload stations and full trains carrying the station's product are allowed at load stations. These are noops - the train visits the station but performs no cargo transfer. This prevents trains from getting stuck when they have no actionable cargo for a station on their route.
"Compatible product" = wagon can transport it (type + filters), module direction matches (loading/unloading), and schedule allows interaction (not disabled, not filtered). "Can't satisfy rule" = wrong wagon type/filter, or rule direction doesn't match schedule restrictions.
Note on empty wagons: when a wagon has no product filter and no cargo, its free capacity is attributed to all products of the matching type at this station. This intentional overcount errors on the conservative side, slightly reducing the chance of calling additional trains.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationGroup.GetNetworkSupply(Mafi.Core.Products.ProductProto,System.Boolean@)
member.summary: Returns how much of a product this station can supply to the network. indicates whether the station's fill% meets the loading threshold (active provider) or not (passive buffer). The returned quantity is always the available supply regardless of threshold.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationGroup.GetNetworkDemand(Mafi.Core.Products.ProductProto,System.Boolean@)
member.summary: Returns how much of a product this station can accept from the network. indicates whether the station's fill% meets the unloading threshold (active requestor) or not (passive buffer). The returned quantity is always the available demand regardless of threshold.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationGroup.GetCapacityForProduct(Mafi.Core.Products.ProductProto)
member.summary: Returns the total storage capacity for a product at this station (modules + linked storages). Used by the network to determine the maximum a station could ever exchange.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationGroup.getAdjustedStored(Mafi.Core.Products.ProductProto,Mafi.Quantity,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Returns the adjusted stored quantity for a product after accounting for in-transit trains. For loading stations: subtracts incoming free capacity (trains coming to pick up). For unloading stations: adds incoming cargo (trains coming to deliver).

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationGroup.ComputeStorageTotals(Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationArrivalRule,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Computes aggregate storage for a specific product across all modules in the group, including assigned storages and port-connected storages. Returns (totalStored, totalCapacity).

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationGroup.updateStorageTotalsIfStale
member.summary: Returns the per-sim-step cached product totals. Refreshes the cache if stale.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationGroup.updateStorageTotals
member.summary: Aggregates per-product storage totals across station modules, assigned storages, and port-connected storages. A product can only be in one direction (loading or unloading) within a station group, so no direction filtering is needed here. Assigned storages only augment products already established by modules.
BFS from a module through connected storages. Uses and to avoid iterating raw ports. The caller owns clearing of - this method does not clear it, allowing cross-module dedup when called in a loop.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationGroup.ComputeInTransitFor(Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationArrivalRule)
member.summary: Computes the in-transit quantity relevant to the given rule across all trains heading here. For unload rules: returns incoming cargo (will be unloaded into storage). For load rules: returns incoming free capacity (will load from storage). Returns zero for inactive rules. For UI display.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationGroup.getInTransitTotalsCache
member.summary: Computes per-product in-transit data from all trains heading to this station and all zone job reservations. For each product, returns: Stored = incoming cargo quantity (from trains carrying that product here). Capacity = incoming free capacity (empty wagon space for that product heading here). Zone-managed trains are excluded from wagon scanning — their exact reservations are included via instead to avoid double-counting. The evaluating train (the one asking whether to arrive) is never a servicing train yet, so it is naturally excluded.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationGroup.gatherTrainInTransitData(Mafi.Core.Trains.Train,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{Mafi.Core.Products.ProductProto,Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationArrivalRule},Mafi.Collections.Lyst{Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationGroup.TrainAdjustment})
member.summary: Gathers per-product in-transit data (cargo and free capacity) for a single train's wagons. Only tracks products handled by the station (as defined by ).

member.remarks: Traverses either forward or backwards from the provided . The provided edge is excluded. Terminates early if another station group or another station root is encountered, which would only happen if the placement tool created an invalid configuration.

member.summary: Returns true if a station root is connected by continuous station entities to the edge, assigning that entity to the out parameter.
member.remarks: This does not check for ambiguous configurations where two station roots are connected to the edge. It returns the first station root it finds.

member.summary: Returns true if the neighboring track entity is a station or station module, assigning that entity to the out parameter.
member.remarks: The parameter switches between checking the forward neighbor of the edge end node and the backwards neighbor of the edge start node.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStationManager.tryGetNextStationEntity(Mafi.Core.TrainGraphNodeId,System.Boolean,Mafi.Core.Trains.ITrainStationBase@,Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainGraphEdge@,Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainTrackTrajectoryDirection@)
member.summary: Returns true if the neighboring track entity is a station or station module, assigning that entity to the out parameter and the next edge to the out parameter. Use to switch between checking the forwards or backwards neighbors.
member.remarks: This always returns false if the node is connected to multiple edges; stations cannot be connected across track branches.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainTracksGraphManager
member.summary: Manages a directed train track graph formed by . On top of common functionality from the base class, this class adds super-blocks feature as well as strict graph update policy to allow pathfinders to run in a separate thread.
member.remarks:
The track graph cannot be updated between and . Thanks to this, any read-only methods that queries train graph can be called from background threads in between these events.
The graph is highly optimized to use small structs and no objects for fast path-finding and train reservation operations.

Fills the passed set with all tracks connected to this track in either direction until a branch is hit, the specified endTrack is hit, or a change which isn't ignored occurs. This method also adds the original track to the collection.

member.summary: Caller must ensure that is true before calling this. WARNING: this function is dangerous - there are zero checks that the train hasn't already promoted the given block to a super block. member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainTracksGraphManagerBase`1
member.summary:
A common logic for train graph management used by train tracks and track previews.
Directed train track graph is formed by . Nodes of this graph are the piece start/end positions and directions. Each graph edge is associated with and is based on . If the entity piece is , two edges a will be added to the graph.
Each track piece has an array of blocks that are holding the track reservation state. This sub-array is necessary as some track pieces are too long to be considered a single block for the train reservation purposes.
This class should not contain any functionality that is not needed by both train tracks and the previews.

member.summary: Outgoing edges, indexed by .
member.remarks:
Edges are stored in an array-backed linked list to avoid allocation of array in each node. This is especially beneficial in cases where a node has just a single outgoing edge.
Nodes connectivity is stored separately from nodes data to avoid fetching unnecessary bytes from memory during path-finding.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainTracksGraphManagerBase`1.BlockData.ReservedByTrainId
member.summary: Train that reserved this block for travel. This reservation is exclusive as only one train can occupy one block at a time to prevent collisions.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainTracksGraphManagerBase`1.BlockData.BlockedByTrainId
member.summary:
Trains that blocked this block because they need to have it clear to avoid collisions. Note that blocked blocks should not be reserved or occupied by any train.
Multiple trains may block one block for collision prevention, e.g.:
=A======+==== / C / =B==+========
where train on A reserves A and blocks C, similarly train on B reserves B and blocks C. The C block is blocked by both trains.

member.summary: UV.y threshold above which vertices are culled from the shadow caster pass.
member.remarks: Vertices with UV.y greater than this value will have their position set to NaN, causing the GPU to discard entire triangles containing such vertices. Use this to prevent thin geometry (e.g. thin railings) from casting shadows. Default is (effectively no culling).

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Trains.INotifyTrainApproachingEntity.NotifyTrainApproaching(Mafi.Core.Trains.Train,Mafi.RelTile1f)
member.summary:
Called periodically when a train is approaching (every when the train is moving). Notifications start when the entity is at the braking-distance * from the train.
Note that only entities on train's path are notified. If an entity is right behind a station, it might get notified last-second about approaching train that just departed.

member.summary: This should update anything that touches train track graph. For anything else use regular SimUpdate.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Trains.ITrainFriend.NotifyPathChangedVerifyValidity(Mafi.Collections.Stak{Mafi.Core.Trains.TrackPathRecord})
member.summary: Called by the pathfinder when PF task is done and new path is computed and available.
member.remarks:
This gives the train an opportunity to check whether the new path is valid (starts near the train head). There may be cases where the path is being searched while following an old path and the train moves so much that the new path took a turn that is no longer possible to take, or, it would require train to brake way too fast (or even stop immediately). If the path is for any reason not suitable, false should be returned and the new path will be discarded (old path will be reverted).
Train is free to mutate the new path in the PF task by adding/removing elements as necessary, even in case of it being reverted later.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainCarBaseProto.T1_CAR_LENGTH
member.summary: Standard train car length. Train stations are made to match this size.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainCarBaseProto.CarLength
member.summary: Total length of the car including couplers. Train cars will be spaced-out exactly this distance from each other in a train.

member.summary: Distance from the car center (origin) to the bogie pivot offset.

member.summary: Distance between bogie pivots, effectively 2 x .

member.summary: Build duration per one quantity of product used.

member.summary: Build duration at the end of the build (after all products are provided).

member.summary: Distance between bogie pivot and end of the car.

member.summary: Distance between bogie axles. Can be zero for single-axle bogie. If the bogie has more than two axles, this is the distance between the outermost ones.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainCarBaseProto.MaxSpeed
member.summary: Maximum speed of this train car imposed by its construction. Train will not go faster than minimum of all max speeds of its wagons. This also applies when going down-hill where train could accelerate beyond its theoretical max speed given by the locomotive power.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainCarBaseProto.MassTonsWhenEmpty
member.summary: Mass of the train car in tons, not taking into account any cargo or fuel.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainCarBaseProto.MassTonsWhenFull
member.summary: Maximal mass of the train car in tons, taking into account any cargo or fuel.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainCarBaseProto.BrakingForceKn
member.summary: Braking force of this train car in kN. For locomotives, this is usually around 70-90% of max tractive effort to prevent wheel slip. For wagons, this is usually 15-20% of their weight.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainCarBaseProto.RollingResistanceCoefficientTimesThousand
member.summary: Rolling resistance coefficient pre-multiplied by thousand. A typical value for steel wheels on steel tracks is 0.001 - 0.004 (1.0 - 4.0 pre-multiplied).

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainCarBaseProto.FrontalAreaM2
member.summary: Frontal area in m^2. This is used for air drag computations.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainCarBaseProto.LengthDragAsExtraFrontalArea
member.summary: Extra frontal area that simulates length-wise air drag. This is so that longer trains have larger air drag force. Typical values are 1 - 2.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainCarBaseProto.DragCoefficientStandalone
member.summary: Drag coefficient of this train car when it is traveling on its own, not part of a longer train. Typical values are 2.0 (highly streamlined) - 10.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainCarBaseProto.DragCoefficientInline
member.summary: Drag coefficient of this train car when connected in-line as part of a long train. This is typically less than , e.g. 1.0 - 5.0.

member.summary: Base cost before being divided by the grade factor. In general if we go up we also go down.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainsPathFinderConfig.GradeFactorExponent
member.summary: Exponent applied to the grade factor before dividing the . Value of 1 will make all grades equally expensive (given that lower grades need to climb for longer distances). Larger values will cause smaller costs for lower grades.
member.remarks:
Values for: 5 / gradeFactor^1.5:
per tile normalized (per 1 height) Grade 1/4: 0.625 2.500 Grade 1/8: 0.221 1.768 Grade 1/12: 0.120 1.443

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainsPathFinderConfig.GradeCostHeuristic
member.summary: Cost for a 1 tile delta height during path cost estimation. This should be less than the cheapest grade to keep the heuristic admissible (non over-estimating).

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainsPathFinderConfig.StationRootWithModulesCost
member.summary: Cost added for any non-waypoint station root that currently has at least one module attached. Discourages routing trains through real loading/unloading stations.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainsPathFinderConfig.WaypointWaitingBayCost
member.summary: Fixed cost added for any waypoint station that is part of a network.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainsPathFinderConfig.BlockedCostPerTile
member.summary: Extra cost for tiles reserved by other trains. This only applies directly if the other train is not moving. This controls how much extra distance is the train willing to go on a detour to avoid blocked but shorter path.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainsPathFinderConfig.StationBlockedCost
member.summary: Cost added when any entity in a station-with-modules group/networked waypoint is reserved or blocked.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainsPathFinderConfig.BlockedCostPerTileMovingFastMultiplier
member.summary: Cost for blocking trains is multiplied by this value if the train is moving faster or the same speed we can.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainsPathFinderConfig.CriticalCostPerTile
member.summary: Extra cost for tiles which are critical sections. This controls how much extra distance is the train willing to go on a detour to avoid critical sections.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainsPathFinderConfig.SpeedCostPerTilePerTickLost
member.summary: Extra cost for tiles which could slow the train down per tile per tick they are expected to slow the train. For 10 steps/second, 1 tile/tick is 72 kph, so a value of 2 will give a penalty of ~1.1 for a 80kph max speed train being forced to go at 40kph.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStaticData
member.summary: Stateless precomputed data for a train.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStaticData.TIME_TO_TRAVEL_TILES
member.summary: Train benchmark of 400 meters run (1/4 mile, 200 tiles).

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStaticData.DATA_AT_SPEED_DELTA
member.summary: Speed interval for computation of .

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStaticData.AIR_DRAG_COEFF_SUM_DIVISOR
member.summary: Reference car count for normalizing the drag coefficient sum. The drag coefficient is computed as a sum of individual car contributions (not averaged by car count) to ensure that adding more cars always increases total drag. This constant normalizes the sum to keep values in the right ballpark.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStaticData.PUSH_PULL_WAGONS_MULT
member.summary: When a group of wagons is being both pushed and pulled, the number of pushed wagons gets multiplied by this number to give a little extra advantage to push-pull setups.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStaticData.PUSHED_WAGON_POWER_PENALTY
member.summary: Multiplicative penalty for pushed wagons.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStaticData.TotalLength
member.summary: Combined length of all train cars.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStaticData.MaxSpeedBasedOnConstruction
member.summary: Maximum speed limited by train cars construction (not by train power) defined as minimum of of all cars.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStaticData.MaxForwardsSpeedCombined
member.summary: All max speed constraints combined. Use this value to limit train forwards speed.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStaticData.MaxBackwardSpeedCombined
member.summary: All max speed constraints combined. Use this value to limit train backwards speed (value is positive).

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStaticData.MaxSpeedAtGrade0
member.summary: Maximum achievable speed (at grade 0).

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStaticData.MaxSpeedAtGrade0Unrestricted
member.summary: Max speed purely based on power, with no artificial restrictions.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStaticData.TimeToTravelNTilesAtGrade0
member.summary: Duration to travel tiles.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStaticData.ForcesAtSpeeds
member.summary: Detailed overview of forces at various speeds. This property is lazy-computed on the first access.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStaticData.LocosMaxPowerFactorsForwards
member.summary: Max power factors of all locomotives when travelling forwards (in the same order as ).

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStaticData.LocosMaxPowerFactorsBackwards
member.summary: Max power factors of all locomotives when travelling backwards (in the same order as ).

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStaticData.TotalMaxPowerForwards
member.summary: Combined max power based on loco positions when travelling forwards.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Trains.TrainStaticData.TotalMaxPowerBackwards
member.summary: Combined max power based on loco positions when travelling backwards.

member.summary: Computes stopping distance estimate. The given grade factor assumes that the entire train is on such grade. For slightly more precise estimates, you can supply average grade factor. Note that air drag is currently not taken into account, so this function will over-estimate.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Vehicles.BufferStrategy.LowestNoQuantityPreference
member.summary:
Strategy where the entity is fine with logistics trying to utilize full trucks.
Strongly not recommended for situation where entity needs to get / remove all the products.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Vehicles.BufferStrategy.OptimalQuantity
member.summary:
Optimal quantity is used by entities to inform logistics on what quantities are considered optimal when delivered / removed to / from them. For instance in case of a machine that takes 8 quantity per recipe in does not make sense to rush to give it 1 quantity. The same example is when finishing construction, if there is 5 parts left, 4 will not cut it anyway.
Optimal quantity can be even null which is equivalent of MaxQuantity. By this the entity state that it does not care about specific quantity. This is for storages. Their priority is not to be full or empty, their priority is to make logistics run smoothly. This would obviously backfire for entities that need all the products to finish, like for instance construction.
When pairing two buffers we apply the following rules: 1) Take minimal from both optimals => at least on buffer's optimal should be satisfied 2) If optimal is not set it gets set to MaxQuantity 3) Clamp optimal with max truck capacity (this means entities don't need to worry with overshooting truck cap) 4) IMPORTANT: Balance iff quantity to exchange is at least as big as optimal => So if there is less to exchange then is optimal, balancing won't happen.
This has several nice implications. 1) Entities don't need to care much about truck sizes. They can help logistics by using nice sizes of buffers and optimal quantities but it will not affect their correctness. 2) Balancing is not performed unless it provides value for at least one party or it utilizes full truck 3) Priority implementors don't need to decide their prios / optimal quantities based on available quantities as this is decided for them.
There is one tricky case that could go wrong: If we return optimal quantity greater than what we can exchange we might never be able to finish. Or in fact we are at mercy of other buffer to hopefully have optimal quantity close to what we can exchange. That is why optimal = null is not recommended for things that need exact amount of products to finish.
There is a cheat to force things to always happen which is Optimal = 1. We use it in very special cases like deconstruction where if we wouldn't do it, things would end up in shipyard. We also use it when clearing storage but it might not be that necessary.

member.summary: Truck job provider providing jobs to trucks assigned to a .

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Vehicles.Trucks.JobProviders.MineTowerTruckJobProvider.RequestRebalanceForStarvingExcavator
member.summary: Called by an excavator when it enters starving state (WaitingForTruck with no trucks). Triggers rebalancing to try to redirect trucks from other queues.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Vehicles.Trucks.JobProviders.MineTowerTruckJobProvider.balanceTruckExcavatorQueues
member.summary:
Balances truck distribution across excavator queues within this mine tower.
Previously, this method was only called when a queue was enabled (excavator started mining). That was insufficient because: - Multiple trucks could commit to the same queue between enable events. - The balance ran before all trucks had committed, missing late arrivals. - No rebalancing occurred when trucks left a queue (after loading).
Now this method is also called by excavators entering starving state via (catches cases where all trucks are already committed and none is idle). This trigger fires at most once per excavator per starving episode (on StateChanged), preventing rapid release-and-reassign cycles.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Vehicles.Trucks.JobProviders.MineTowerTruckJobProvider.tryGetQueuedAtExcavatorJob(Mafi.Core.Vehicles.Trucks.Truck,Mafi.RelTile1i)
member.summary: Tries to get the truck queued at an Excavator that is currently working (it has its TruckQueue enabled). It searches among Excavators assigned to our MiningTower and looks for the Excavator with the shortest queue. If more queues have the same length, it chooses the closest Excavator with the shortest queue.

Tries to get rid of current cargo.
If the cargo is something we really need to get rid of mark it as . This is anytime the cargo is in way. However don't use it for situations where it is expected such as mining truck as we don't want to fill shipyard with mined products.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Vehicles.VehicleBuffersRegistry.m_buffersTmp
member.summary:
We are using list because we sort via Comparison instead of Comparator. Array.sort causes allocations when used with Comparator. Comparison cannot be used for Lyst because Array.sort with Comparison arg does not accept a range selection (it would be touching nulls in Lyst). However I still see some allocations from GetHashCode (but not coming from sort) and I'm not sure. Maybe we might need to implement our own Sort. Or there is some other work causing besides Sort.
Some perf tests are in SortPerformanceTest.cs but results are mixed.
TODO: Get rid of allocations entirely.
