member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Vehicles.VehicleBuffersRegistry.GetBalancingLatency
member.summary:
TODO: No longer true. Called also from our balancing code. For UI. Don't call often.
Logistics scans balancing jobs by taking each product one by one. If there are some products that we did not manage to balance for some time already it means logistics is getting overloaded with work. Idle logistics will have latency close to 0 sec. While a busy one will reach 10's of seconds.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Vehicles.VehicleBuffersRegistry.balanceBuffers(Mafi.Percent)
member.summary:
This method handles balancing for multiple trucks at once. It is actually optimized to run for multiple trucks at once and the cost is almost "constant" in number of trucks. This is achieved due to several tricks: 1) If job is found, we refresh only the buffers per product that were assigned a job, the rest stays as it was. We also skip pairing buffers for which we failed to pair in that run. 2) To make this even better, if there is a same priority level for multiple products, we try to pair all of them before even refreshing priorities and going again (as in step 1)). 3) We also reject buffers early on by checking min. truck capacity to avoid running checks for all trucks 4) Finally we also filter trucks that are not eligible for a particular product. So a tank truck will not participate in balancing of loose products.
Some measured observations: Observed durations to assign trucks in one go (in debug): - 6 trucks in 1.4ms - 3 trucks in 0.7-2.9ms - 1 truck in 0.5-2.3ms The reason for the variance is that it depends more on the buffers and designations instead on the number of trucks.
We also save a bit on CPU by not having all trucks call this individually. It went from ~40ms to ~30ms of CPU time spent in balancing code until it assigns 50 jobs in an end-game factory. Note that this was measured on a busy logistics. Players with idle vehicles will benefit more.
There is also a big benefit in running this for multiple trucks as we always assign the truck that is closest which saves on driving. So there is a trade-off on how long should we let trucks wait hoping they get a job nearby. In general, we don't let trucks wait too long.
TODO: We can consider assigning large trucks to large cargo jobs (using some weight + distance).

Picks one representative buffer per construction chunk for the given product, mirroring the non-chunk preprocessing path. For each chunk: 1) If any member already has pending cargo, boost the priority of non-pending members so the pending bump doesn't skew chunk balancing. 2) Pick the highest-priority available member, breaking ties by distance to the chunk center, skipping any buffer already chosen as representative for another chunk. 3) Add it to and update . Used by both input and output sides via .

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Vehicles.VehicleBuffersRegistry.clearChunkRepresentatives
member.summary: Clears the chunk-representative dicts and resets the matching flags. Called once per balancing pass before the first preprocessing round.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Vehicles.VehicleBuffersRegistry.clearRepresentativesForProduct(Mafi.Core.Vehicles.VehicleBuffersRegistry.RegisteredBuffers)
member.summary: Drops the rep mapping and flag for every construction representative currently sitting in this product's tmp lists. Called at the top of preprocessBuffersFor (before the lists are cleared) so that re-preprocessed products invalidate their old reps without disturbing untouched products.

Tries to add more nearby targets to an already existing scheduled job. So if we have a delivery target we try to find other delivery target nearby as long as we are able to increase reservation of cargo that we can pick up. We also do the same the opposite way, we try to add multiple pick up points. However, there are still strong limitations. The first limitation is that we only expand delivery or pick up targets, not both at the same time due to combinatorial costs. Second limitation is that we do this only for (de)construction / upgrade buffers. Otherwise we would have to go through the pain of handling assigned routes and all that stuff. Also performance wise this does not hurt much for constructions because we will definitely get savings on unnecessary jobs.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Simulation.ISimLoopEvents
member.summary:
Simulation events for anybody to subscribe. All events are called on the simulation thread.
The order of events called is following: 0) Input commands are processed 1) UpdateStart 2) Update 3) UpdateEnd

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Simulation.ISimLoopEvents.CurrentStep
member.summary: Current sim step.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Simulation.ISimLoopEvents.CurrentState
member.summary: State of sim loop. What method is currently being invoked. This is useful for assertions and debugging.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Simulation.ISimLoopEvents.IsSimPaused
member.summary: Whether the simulation is paused. In such case only commands are executed.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Simulation.ISimLoopEvents.SimSpeedMult
member.summary: Game speed multiplier.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Simulation.ISimLoopEvents.IsInSimLoop
member.summary: Whether the simulation loop is active (commands execution included).

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Simulation.ISimLoopEvents.UpdateBeforeCmdProc
member.summary: Called before commands were processed. Note that this will be also called during pause.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Simulation.ISimLoopEvents.UpdateAfterCmdProc
member.summary: Called after commands were processed. Note that this will be also called during pause.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Simulation.ISimLoopEvents.UpdateAfterSync
member.summary: Called once after sync. Unlike other events, this one is called only after sync. This makes difference when there is more sim steps performed between syncs.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Simulation.ISimLoopEvents.UpdateStart
member.summary: Called at the very beginning of the simulation loop right after commands are processed. Invoked on the simulation thread.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Simulation.ISimLoopEvents.ParallelUpdateStart
member.summary: Called just before . Use this solely to schedule parallel tasks that run alongside of the sim update.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Simulation.ISimLoopEvents.Update
member.summary: Called after and should be used for core computations. Invoked on the simulation thread.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Simulation.ISimLoopEvents.ParallelUpdateEnd
member.summary: Called just after . Use this solely to join parallel tasks that run alongside of the sim update.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Simulation.ISimLoopEvents.UpdateEnd
member.summary: Called after as the very last event before sync state. This can be used for exchange of computed data between simulation components or for preparation of the synchronization operation. Invoked on the simulation thread.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Simulation.ISimLoopEvents.UpdateEndForUi
member.summary: Called at the end of update and should be used only in UI. Unlike other events, this one is invoked even if the game is paused so that UI can process stuff during pause. Subscribers MUST NOT change game state here!

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Simulation.ISimLoopEvents.ReadGameStateFrequent
member.summary: Called after every sim step and should be used only in UI. It is invoked when the game state is paused. It is also called repeatedly after the sim thread completes one step but before it starts the next. While it is fine to do some work here, each chunk of work should be small: instead let the work run over many calls to this event. Subscribers MUST NOT change game state here!

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Simulation.ISimLoopEvents.Sync
member.summary: Called when the sim thread is in sync with the main thread before any other actions are called. This is a place where to read values and swap buffers.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Simulation.ISimLoopEvents.BeforeSave
member.summary: Invoked just before the game is saved. This can be used to adjust game state before saving to make it more efficient. Keep in mind that this may be invoked even if the game is not actually being saved for determinism purposes (for example during replay). Invoked during .

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Simulation.SimLoopState.None
member.summary: No game loop method is invoked right now.

Holds every piece of state that would otherwise be lost across a recovery import: historical data (daily / monthly / yearly / quarter-century), the aggregated values (current / this-year / lifetime), and the date-alignment metadata ( / ) that ties the annual data to real game years.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Stats.RleSequence
member.summary: Sequence of numbers compressed using RLE (run-length encoding). This compresses sequence like [0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 2, ...] into [3 x 0, 2 x 1, 1 x 2, ...] Max stored value is +-2^54 ~ 18,000,000,000,000,000.
member.remarks: Values are stored as longs, the high bits are used as (number of repetitions - 1). WARNING: Mutable struct, do not store in readonly or nullable fields.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Stats.RleSequence.REPS_BITS
member.summary: Number of bits for the repetition count. The value will have the rest of ulong.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Stats.RleSequence.Count
member.summary: Number of stored values (uncompressed).

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Stats.RleSequence.m_rleValues
member.summary: RLE values. Length of this array must be a power of two to be able to use bit operations instead of modulo.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Stats.RleSequence.m_rleValuesCount
member.summary: Number of stored RLE Values.

member.summary: Returns max of last n elements. Works directly on compressed data.

member.summary: Returns average of last n elements by working directly on compressed data.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Utils.XorRsr128PlusGenerator
member.summary: Xor Rotate-shift-rotate pseudo-random generator has 128 bits of state and period of 2^128 - 1. Every 64-bit value expect zero is guaranteed to appear exactly 2^64 times in this period.The state must be seeded so that it is not everywhere zero.
member.remarks:
Written in 2016 by David Blackman and Sebastiano Vigna (vigna@acm.org) * License: http://creativecommons.org/publicdomain/zero/1.0/ * Original source: http://xoroshiro.di.unimi.it/xoroshiro128plus.c
This is the successor to xorshift128+. It is the fastest full-period generator passing BigCrush without systematic failures, but due to the relatively short period it is acceptable only for applications with a mild amount of parallelism; otherwise, use a xorshift1024* generator.
Beside passing BigCrush, this generator passes the PractRand test suite up to (and included) 16TB, with the exception of binary rank tests, which fail due to the lowest bit being an LFSR; all other bits pass all tests. We suggest to use a sign test to extract a random Boolean value.
TODO: Improve https://www.pcg-random.org/

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Utils.XorRsr128PlusGenerator.#ctor(Mafi.Core.RandomGeneratorType,System.UInt64,System.UInt64)
member.summary: WARNING: Do not seed with hashes from `.GetHashCode()`, they are not guaranteed to be stable!

member.summary: WARNING: Do not seed with hashes from `.GetHashCode()`, they are not guaranteed to be stable!

member.summary: WARNING: Do not seed with hashes from `.GetHashCode()`, they are not guaranteed to be stable!

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Utils.XorRsr128PlusGenerator.NextUlong
member.summary: Generates 64-bit random number using XorShift128+ algorithm. This method never returns 0.
member.remarks: WARNING: If the state is 0 this method always returns 0!

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Utils.XorRsr128PlusGenerator.Jump
member.summary: This is the jump function for the generator. It is equivalent to 2^64 calls to next(). It can be used to generate 2^64 non-overlapping subsequences for parallel computations.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.TileTransform
member.summary: Immutable representation of transformation in tile space. Represents translation, rotation, and reflection. The order is: reflect, rotate, translate. This class also contain some pre-computed convenience members to help with application of this transformation.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.TileTransform.Position
member.summary: Absolute global tile coordinates.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.TileTransform.Rotation
member.summary: Rotation around the origin.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.TileTransform.IsReflected
member.summary: Reflection around x-axis. Reflection is applied before rotation. Reflection around y-axis is not needed, the same effect can be achieved by rotation and x reflection.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.TileTransform.TransformMatrix
member.summary: Transform matrix that can be used to apply rotation and reflection of this transform around (0, 0). It does not apply any translation. This matrix is pre-computed for efficient transformations.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.TileTransform.Transform(Mafi.Rotation90)
member.summary: Transforms given rotation according to this transformation.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.TileTransform.Transform(Mafi.Direction90)
member.summary: Transforms given rotation according to this transformation.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.TileTransform.Transform(Mafi.AngleDegrees1f)
member.summary: Transforms given angle according to this transformation.

member.name: F:Mafi.Core.Transform90RotFlip.RawValue
member.summary: Raw value in range 0-7 (three bits) where the two lower bits are rotation and third bit is reflection.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.IInitializer
member.summary: Allow to run conditional code based on whether game is being loaded or not. Usage of this reduces the amount of constructor overloads that were typically needed.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.IInitializer.IsBeingLoaded
member.summary: Whether the caller is being instantiated during loading process.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.IInitializer.DoOnNewGameOnly(System.Action)
member.summary: The given action gets invoked only when new game is created (not from save file). NOTE: This should be used only in constructors of root classes.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.IInitializer.DoOnNewGameOrAfterLoad(System.Action)
member.summary: The given action gets invoked only (and immediately) when new game is created (not from save file) or after the game was fully loaded. NOTE: This should be used only in constructors of root classes.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Syncers.CollectionSyncer`2.#ctor(System.Func{`1},Mafi.Core.Syncers.ICollectionComparator{`0,`1},System.Action{`1,Mafi.Collections.Lyst{`0}})
member.summary: Use instead this ctor.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Syncers.CompareFixedOrder`1
member.summary: Compares two collections value by value in fixed order. Be careful when using this! This is expensive. Optimally try to use or at least set interval on your updater.

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Syncers.CompareByCount`1
member.summary: Very efficient collections comparator using count but also very error prone. This works only with a very hard guarantee that collection is changed only by adding / removing values. So if you swap the observed collection with different one but with same size this won't pick it up. Currently we can use it in the UI because we invalidate onShow which forces the collection re-compute. That guarantees that only user actions can change the collection. And for user it is impossible to change more than one item per sync update (add recipe, vehicle, product).

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Syncers.CompareByCountIndexable`1
member.summary: for more info.

member.name: M:Mafi.Core.Syncers.IUiUpdater.AddChildUpdater(Mafi.Core.Syncers.IUiUpdater)
member.summary: Adds a child updater. The given updater will have its render, sync methods called by this updater. Keep in mind that if this updater is using lower it will affect the child updater. So setting the child updater with anything below critical might be too much (with a delay x in both it would lead to x*x delay for the child).

member.name: T:Mafi.Core.Syncers.PeriodicSyncUpdateSyncer
member.summary: Syncer that doesn't store any value. Just invokes user specified action periodically on sync.

member.name: P:Mafi.Core.Syncers.ISyncer`1.HasChanged
member.summary: It is important that the this boolean is not reset by Syncer until it gets read by a Trigger. Otherwise next sync would clear this value and if no render (Trigger) was running at that time the change would be lost. This can happen when view gets hidden between sync and render phases.

member.name: M:Mafi.Chunk256.PseudoCross(Mafi.Chunk256)
member.summary: Returns pseudo-cross product of this vector with vector. That is, Z component of 3D cross product between the two vectors with Z equal to 0.
member.remarks: This operation is very efficient and has following properties: * v1.PseudoCross(v2) = -v2.PseudoCross(v1). * v1.PseudoCross(v2) = |v1| |v2| sin(θ) where θ is signed angle from v1 to v2 (v1.AngleTo(v2)). * Returns 0 for parallel (or anti-parallel) vectors. * Returns positive values when v2 is to the left of v1. * Returns negative values when v2 is to the right of v1. * Is equal to a determinant of matrix [v1,v2]. * Is equal to v1.LeftOrthogonalVector.Dot(v2).

member.name: M:Mafi.Chunk256.AngleTo(Mafi.Chunk256)
member.summary: Returns signed angle from this vector to vector. Returned angle represents how much this vector has to be rotated to obtain vector. Returned value is [-τ/2, τ/2). This means that v1.AngleTo(v2) == -v2.AngleTo(v1) and v1.Rotate(v1.AngleTo(v2)) == v2.

member.name: M:Mafi.Chunk256.AngleBetween(Mafi.Chunk256)
member.summary: Returns absolute angle between this and vectors. Returned angle is in range [0, τ/2].

member.name: M:Mafi.Chunk256.IsParallelTo(Mafi.Chunk256)
member.summary: Whether this and vectors are parallel and not anti-parallel.

member.name: M:Mafi.Chunk256.IsAntiParallelTo(Mafi.Chunk256)
member.summary: Whether this and vectors are anti-parallel and not parallel.

member.name: M:Mafi.Chunk256.IsParallelOrAntiParallelTo(Mafi.Chunk256)
member.summary: Whether this and vectors are parallel or anti-parallel. This is more efficient than calling and .

member.summary: Exact equality of two vectors.

member.summary: Exact inequality of two vectors.

member.name: M:Mafi.Chunk256.op_LessThan(Mafi.Chunk256,Mafi.Chunk256)
member.summary: Component-wise less-than operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are less than respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A < B is not equal to A >= B. For example vectors (1, 2) and (2, 1) are not less-than nor greater-than-or-equal.

member.name: M:Mafi.Chunk256.op_LessThanOrEqual(Mafi.Chunk256,Mafi.Chunk256)
member.summary: Component-wise less-than-or-equal operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are less than or equal to respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A <= B is not equal to A > B. For example vectors (1, 2) and (2, 1) are not less-than-or-equal nor greater-than.

member.name: M:Mafi.Chunk256.op_GreaterThan(Mafi.Chunk256,Mafi.Chunk256)
member.summary: Component-wise greater-than operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are greater than respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A > B is not equal to A <= B. For example vectors (1, 2) and (2, 1) are not greater-than nor less-than-or-equal.

member.name: M:Mafi.Chunk256.op_GreaterThanOrEqual(Mafi.Chunk256,Mafi.Chunk256)
member.summary: Component-wise greater-than-or-equal operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are greater than or equal to respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A >= B is not equal to A < B. For example vectors (1, 2) and (2, 1) are not greater-than-or-equal nor less-than.

member.remarks: This operation is very efficient and has following properties: * v1.PseudoCross(v2) = -v2.PseudoCross(v1). * v1.PseudoCross(v2) = |v1| |v2| sin(θ) where θ is signed angle from v1 to v2 (v1.AngleTo(v2)). * Returns 0 for parallel (or anti-parallel) vectors. * Returns positive values when v2 is to the left of v1. * Returns negative values when v2 is to the right of v1. * Is equal to a determinant of matrix [v1,v2]. * Is equal to v1.LeftOrthogonalVector.Dot(v2).

member.name: M:Mafi.Chunk2i.Rotate(Mafi.AngleDegrees1f)
member.summary: Returns rotated vector by given angle. Positive angle values represent in counter-clockwise rotation. This means that (1, 0).Rotate(90°) == (0, 1). WARNING: Please keep in mind that rotating integer vectors may not be precise for vectors with small magnitudes due to rounding errors.

member.name: M:Mafi.Chunk2i.Rotate(Mafi.Rotation90)
member.summary: Returns rotated vector by given angle. Positive angle values represent in counter-clockwise rotation. This means that (1, 0).Rotate(90°) == (0, 1).

member.name: M:Mafi.Chunk2i.AngleTo(Mafi.Chunk2i)
member.summary: Returns signed angle from this vector to vector. Returned angle represents how much this vector has to be rotated to obtain vector. Returned value is [-τ/2, τ/2). This means that v1.AngleTo(v2) == -v2.AngleTo(v1) and v1.Rotate(v1.AngleTo(v2)) == v2.

member.name: M:Mafi.Chunk2i.AngleBetween(Mafi.Chunk2i)
member.summary: Returns absolute angle between this and vectors. Returned angle is in range [0, τ/2].

member.summary: Whether this and vectors are parallel and not anti-parallel.

member.name: M:Mafi.Chunk2i.IsAntiParallelTo(Mafi.Chunk2i)
member.summary: Whether this and vectors are anti-parallel and not parallel.

member.name: M:Mafi.Chunk2i.IsParallelOrAntiParallelTo(Mafi.Chunk2i)
member.summary: Whether this and vectors are parallel or anti-parallel. This is more efficient than calling and .

member.name: M:Mafi.Chunk2i.op_LessThan(Mafi.Chunk2i,Mafi.Chunk2i)
member.summary: Component-wise less-than operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are less than respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A < B is not equal to A >= B. For example vectors (1, 2) and (2, 1) are not less-than nor greater-than-or-equal.

member.name: M:Mafi.Chunk2i.op_LessThanOrEqual(Mafi.Chunk2i,Mafi.Chunk2i)
member.summary: Component-wise less-than-or-equal operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are less than or equal to respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A <= B is not equal to A > B. For example vectors (1, 2) and (2, 1) are not less-than-or-equal nor greater-than.

member.name: M:Mafi.Chunk2i.op_GreaterThan(Mafi.Chunk2i,Mafi.Chunk2i)
member.summary: Component-wise greater-than operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are greater than respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A > B is not equal to A <= B. For example vectors (1, 2) and (2, 1) are not greater-than nor less-than-or-equal.

member.name: M:Mafi.Chunk2i.op_GreaterThanOrEqual(Mafi.Chunk2i,Mafi.Chunk2i)
member.summary: Component-wise greater-than-or-equal operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are greater than or equal to respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A >= B is not equal to A < B. For example vectors (1, 2) and (2, 1) are not greater-than-or-equal nor less-than.

member.name: P:Mafi.Duration.MinutesAsFix32
member.summary: Minutes as Fix32, will be imprecise for smaller durations.
member.remarks: To compute per-minute value more precisely, instead of dividing duration by minutes, multiply the value by SimStep.STEPS_PER_SECOND * 60 and divide by ticks.

member.name: M:Mafi.Duration.FromKeyframes(System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates duration from unity key frames count. This assumes that Unity animations runs at fps (usually 30). The duration is always rounded up so that animations are never cut short. Note that animation FPS and game FPS are independent.

member.name: M:Mafi.Duration.FromKeyframes(System.Int32,System.Single)
member.summary: Same as but also applies the given speed.

member.name: M:Mafi.Duration.SpeedToMatch(Mafi.Duration)
member.summary: Calculates the speed needed to be applied on the current duration to achieve the given one.

member.name: T:Mafi.GameDate
member.summary: In-game date representation, does not have a predefined relationship with real time. Precision is one day.

member.name: P:Mafi.GameDate.Day
member.summary: Day in the month. Value is in [1, RelGameDate.DAYS_PER_MONTH].

member.name: P:Mafi.GameDate.Month
member.summary: Month in the year. Value is in [1, RelGameDate.MONTHS_PER_YEAR].

member.name: P:Mafi.GameDate.Year
member.summary: Year of the date. Years start with value 1 for first year.

member.name: P:Mafi.GameDate.FloorMonths
member.summary: Rounds the date down to months - year and month stay and day is set to 1.

member.name: P:Mafi.GameDate.CeilMonths
member.summary: Rounds the date up to months - year stays the same; if day is zero, month stays, otherwise month is increased by one; day is set to 1.

member.summary: Returns normalized vector.

member.summary: Returns absolute angle of this vector. Returned angle is in range [-180, 180].

member.name: M:Mafi.RelTile2f.PseudoCross(Mafi.RelTile2f)
member.summary: Returns pseudo-cross product of this vector with vector. That is, Z component of 3D cross product between the two vectors with Z equal to 0.
member.remarks: This operation is very efficient and has following properties: * v1.PseudoCross(v2) = -v2.PseudoCross(v1). * v1.PseudoCross(v2) = |v1| |v2| sin(θ) where θ is signed angle from v1 to v2 (v1.AngleTo(v2)). * Returns 0 for parallel (or anti-parallel) vectors. * Returns positive values when v2 is to the left of v1. * Returns negative values when v2 is to the right of v1. * Is equal to a determinant of matrix [v1,v2]. * Is equal to v1.LeftOrthogonalVector.Dot(v2).

member.name: M:Mafi.RelTile2f.Rotate(Mafi.AngleDegrees1f)
member.summary: Returns rotated vector by given angle. Positive angle values represent in counter-clockwise rotation. This means that (1, 0).Rotate(90°) == (0, 1).

member.name: M:Mafi.RelTile2f.Rotate(Mafi.Rotation90)
member.summary: Returns rotated vector by given angle. Positive angle values represent in counter-clockwise rotation. This means that (1, 0).Rotate(90°) == (0, 1).

member.name: M:Mafi.RelTile2f.Rotate(Mafi.AngleDegrees1f,Mafi.RelTile2f)
member.summary: Returns rotated vector by given angle around given pivot point. Positive angle values represent in counter-clockwise rotation. This means that (1, 0).Rotate(90°) == (0, 1).

member.name: M:Mafi.RelTile2f.AngleTo(Mafi.RelTile2f)
member.summary: Returns signed angle from this vector to vector. Returned angle represents how much this vector has to be rotated to obtain vector. Returned value is [-τ/2, τ/2). This means that v1.AngleTo(v2) == -v2.AngleTo(v1) and v1.Rotate(v1.AngleTo(v2)) == v2.

member.name: M:Mafi.RelTile2f.AngleBetween(Mafi.RelTile2f)
member.summary: Returns absolute angle between this and vectors. Returned angle is in range [0, τ/2].

member.name: M:Mafi.RelTile2f.IsParallelTo(Mafi.RelTile2f)
member.summary: Whether this and vectors are parallel and not anti-parallel.

member.name: M:Mafi.RelTile2f.IsAntiParallelTo(Mafi.RelTile2f)
member.summary: Whether this and vectors are anti-parallel and not parallel.

member.name: M:Mafi.RelTile2f.IsParallelOrAntiParallelTo(Mafi.RelTile2f)
member.summary: Whether this and vectors are parallel or anti-parallel. This is more efficient than calling and .

member.name: P:Mafi.RelTile2f.LeftOrthogonalVector
member.summary: Returns this vector rotated by 90 degrees to the left (counter clockwise).

member.name: P:Mafi.RelTile2f.RightOrthogonalVector
member.summary: Returns this vector rotated by 90 degrees to the right (clockwise).

member.name: M:Mafi.RelTile2f.Reflect(Mafi.RelTile2f)
member.summary: Reflection of this vector from plane specified by given normal (has to be normalized). This vector should be pointing to the plane and reflected vector is pointing away from the plane.

remarks.code: Normal vector * this \ | * reflected vector \ | / vector ____*|/____ reflection surface given by the normal

member.summary: Exact equality of two vectors.

member.name: M:Mafi.RelTile2f.op_LessThan(Mafi.RelTile2f,Mafi.RelTile2f)
member.summary: Component-wise less-than operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are less than respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A < B is not equal to A >= B. For example vectors (1, 2) and (2, 1) are not less-than nor greater-than-or-equal.

member.name: M:Mafi.RelTile2f.op_LessThanOrEqual(Mafi.RelTile2f,Mafi.RelTile2f)
member.summary: Component-wise less-than-or-equal operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are less than or equal to respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A <= B is not equal to A > B. For example vectors (1, 2) and (2, 1) are not less-than-or-equal nor greater-than.

member.name: M:Mafi.RelTile2f.op_GreaterThan(Mafi.RelTile2f,Mafi.RelTile2f)
member.summary: Component-wise greater-than operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are greater than respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A > B is not equal to A <= B. For example vectors (1, 2) and (2, 1) are not greater-than nor less-than-or-equal.

member.name: M:Mafi.RelTile2f.op_GreaterThanOrEqual(Mafi.RelTile2f,Mafi.RelTile2f)
member.summary: Component-wise greater-than-or-equal operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are greater than or equal to respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A >= B is not equal to A < B. For example vectors (1, 2) and (2, 1) are not greater-than-or-equal nor less-than.

member.summary: Component-wise division of two vectors.

member.summary: Component-wise modulo of two vectors.

member.name: M:Mafi.RelTile2i.PseudoCross(Mafi.RelTile2i)
member.summary: Returns pseudo-cross product of this vector with vector. That is, Z component of 3D cross product between the two vectors with Z equal to 0.
member.remarks: This operation is very efficient and has following properties: * v1.PseudoCross(v2) = -v2.PseudoCross(v1). * v1.PseudoCross(v2) = |v1| |v2| sin(θ) where θ is signed angle from v1 to v2 (v1.AngleTo(v2)). * Returns 0 for parallel (or anti-parallel) vectors. * Returns positive values when v2 is to the left of v1. * Returns negative values when v2 is to the right of v1. * Is equal to a determinant of matrix [v1,v2]. * Is equal to v1.LeftOrthogonalVector.Dot(v2).

member.name: M:Mafi.RelTile2i.Rotate(Mafi.AngleDegrees1f)
member.summary: Returns rotated vector by given angle. Positive angle values represent in counter-clockwise rotation. This means that (1, 0).Rotate(90°) == (0, 1). WARNING: Please keep in mind that rotating integer vectors may not be precise for vectors with small magnitudes due to rounding errors.

member.name: M:Mafi.RelTile2i.op_LessThan(Mafi.RelTile2i,Mafi.RelTile2i)
member.summary: Component-wise less-than operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are less than respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A < B is not equal to A >= B. For example vectors (1, 2) and (2, 1) are not less-than nor greater-than-or-equal.

member.name: M:Mafi.RelTile2i.op_LessThanOrEqual(Mafi.RelTile2i,Mafi.RelTile2i)
member.summary: Component-wise less-than-or-equal operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are less than or equal to respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A <= B is not equal to A > B. For example vectors (1, 2) and (2, 1) are not less-than-or-equal nor greater-than.

member.name: M:Mafi.RelTile2i.op_GreaterThan(Mafi.RelTile2i,Mafi.RelTile2i)
member.summary: Component-wise greater-than operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are greater than respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A > B is not equal to A <= B. For example vectors (1, 2) and (2, 1) are not greater-than nor less-than-or-equal.

member.name: M:Mafi.RelTile2i.op_GreaterThanOrEqual(Mafi.RelTile2i,Mafi.RelTile2i)
member.summary: Component-wise greater-than-or-equal operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are greater than or equal to respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A >= B is not equal to A < B. For example vectors (1, 2) and (2, 1) are not greater-than-or-equal nor less-than.

member.name: M:Mafi.RelTile3i.op_LessThan(Mafi.RelTile3i,Mafi.RelTile3i)
member.summary: Component-wise less-than operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are less than respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A < B is not equal to A >= B. For example vectors (1, 2, 3) and (3, 2, 1) are not less-than nor greater-than-or-equal.

member.name: M:Mafi.RelTile3i.op_LessThanOrEqual(Mafi.RelTile3i,Mafi.RelTile3i)
member.summary: Component-wise less-than-or-equal operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are less than or equal to respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A <= B is not equal to A > B. For example vectors (1, 2, 3) and (3, 2, 1) are not less-than-or-equal nor greater-than.

member.name: M:Mafi.RelTile3i.op_GreaterThan(Mafi.RelTile3i,Mafi.RelTile3i)
member.summary: Component-wise greater-than operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are greater than respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A > B is not equal to A <= B. For example vectors (1, 2, 3) and (3, 2, 1) are not greater-than nor less-than-or-equal.

member.name: M:Mafi.RelTile3i.op_GreaterThanOrEqual(Mafi.RelTile3i,Mafi.RelTile3i)
member.summary: Component-wise greater-than-or-equal operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are greater than or equal to respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A >= B is not equal to A < B. For example vectors (1, 2, 3) and (3, 2, 1) are not greater-than-or-equal nor less-than.

member.summary: Returns scaled vector to requested length. This method is faster and more intuitive than normalization followed by multiplication.

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2f.IsNear(Mafi.Tile2f,Mafi.Fix32)
member.summary: Tests whether corresponding components of this and given vectors are within tolerance.

member.name: P:Mafi.Tile2f.IsNormalized
member.summary: Whether this vector length is (nearly) one using default epsilon . Note that This uses efficient check of length squared without the need for square root computation.

member.name: P:Mafi.Tile2f.Normalized
member.summary: Returns normalized vector.

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2f.Dot(Mafi.Tile2f)
member.summary: Returns dot product of this vector with given vector.

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2f.DistanceTo(Mafi.Tile2f)
member.summary: Returns distance from this vector to the other vector.

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2f.DistanceSqrTo(Mafi.Tile2f)
member.summary: Returns squared distance from this vector to the other vector.

member.name: P:Mafi.Tile2f.Angle
member.summary: Returns absolute angle of this vector. Returned angle is in range [-180, 180].

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2f.PseudoCross(Mafi.Tile2f)
member.summary: Returns pseudo-cross product of this vector with vector. That is, Z component of 3D cross product between the two vectors with Z equal to 0.
member.remarks: This operation is very efficient and has following properties: * v1.PseudoCross(v2) = -v2.PseudoCross(v1). * v1.PseudoCross(v2) = |v1| |v2| sin(θ) where θ is signed angle from v1 to v2 (v1.AngleTo(v2)). * Returns 0 for parallel (or anti-parallel) vectors. * Returns positive values when v2 is to the left of v1. * Returns negative values when v2 is to the right of v1. * Is equal to a determinant of matrix [v1,v2]. * Is equal to v1.LeftOrthogonalVector.Dot(v2).

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2f.Rotate(Mafi.AngleDegrees1f)
member.summary: Returns rotated vector by given angle. Positive angle values represent in counter-clockwise rotation. This means that (1, 0).Rotate(90°) == (0, 1).

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2f.Rotate(Mafi.Rotation90)
member.summary: Returns rotated vector by given angle. Positive angle values represent in counter-clockwise rotation. This means that (1, 0).Rotate(90°) == (0, 1).

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2f.Rotate(Mafi.AngleDegrees1f,Mafi.Tile2f)
member.summary: Returns rotated vector by given angle around given pivot point. Positive angle values represent in counter-clockwise rotation. This means that (1, 0).Rotate(90°) == (0, 1).

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2f.AngleTo(Mafi.Tile2f)
member.summary: Returns signed angle from this vector to vector. Returned angle represents how much this vector has to be rotated to obtain vector. Returned value is [-τ/2, τ/2). This means that v1.AngleTo(v2) == -v2.AngleTo(v1) and v1.Rotate(v1.AngleTo(v2)) == v2.

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2f.AngleBetween(Mafi.Tile2f)
member.summary: Returns absolute angle between this and vectors. Returned angle is in range [0, τ/2].

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2f.IsParallelTo(Mafi.Tile2f)
member.summary: Whether this and vectors are parallel and not anti-parallel.

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2f.IsAntiParallelTo(Mafi.Tile2f)
member.summary: Whether this and vectors are anti-parallel and not parallel.

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2f.IsParallelOrAntiParallelTo(Mafi.Tile2f)
member.summary: Whether this and vectors are parallel or anti-parallel. This is more efficient than calling and .

member.name: P:Mafi.Tile2f.LeftOrthogonalVector
member.summary: Returns this vector rotated by 90 degrees to the left (counter clockwise).

member.name: P:Mafi.Tile2f.RightOrthogonalVector
member.summary: Returns this vector rotated by 90 degrees to the right (clockwise).

member.name: T:Mafi.Tile2i
member.summary: Immutable 2D global tile coordinate. Depending on context, this can represent a terrain vertex (corner of a tile) or an entire tile area. Consider using in cases where there is no worry about overflows when doing math on the coordinates.

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2i.OfLength(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns scaled vector to requested length. This method is more precise, faster and more intuitive than normalization followed by multiplication. WARNING: Setting length of integer vector may not produce exact requested length do to rounding error.

member.summary: Returns dot product of this vector with given vector.

member.summary: Returns dot product of this vector with given vector as int32. Note that result of this method may overflow if magnitude of any component is larger than ~30,000.

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2i.DistanceTo(Mafi.Tile2i)
member.summary: Returns distance from this vector to the other vector.

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2i.DistanceSqrTo(Mafi.Tile2i)
member.summary: Returns squared distance from this vector to the other vector.

member.name: P:Mafi.Tile2i.Angle
member.summary: Returns absolute angle of this vector. Returned angle is in range [-180, 180].

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2i.PseudoCross(Mafi.Tile2i)
member.summary: Returns pseudo-cross product of this vector with vector. That is, Z component of 3D cross product between the two vectors with Z equal to 0.
member.remarks: This operation is very efficient and has following properties: * v1.PseudoCross(v2) = -v2.PseudoCross(v1). * v1.PseudoCross(v2) = |v1| |v2| sin(θ) where θ is signed angle from v1 to v2 (v1.AngleTo(v2)). * Returns 0 for parallel (or anti-parallel) vectors. * Returns positive values when v2 is to the left of v1. * Returns negative values when v2 is to the right of v1. * Is equal to a determinant of matrix [v1,v2]. * Is equal to v1.LeftOrthogonalVector.Dot(v2).

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2i.Rotate(Mafi.AngleDegrees1f)
member.summary: Returns rotated vector by given angle. Positive angle values represent in counter-clockwise rotation. This means that (1, 0).Rotate(90°) == (0, 1). WARNING: Please keep in mind that rotating integer vectors may not be precise for vectors with small magnitudes due to rounding errors.

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2i.Rotate(Mafi.Rotation90)
member.summary: Returns rotated vector by given angle. Positive angle values represent in counter-clockwise rotation. This means that (1, 0).Rotate(90°) == (0, 1).

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2i.Rotate(Mafi.AngleDegrees1f,Mafi.Tile2i)
member.summary: Returns rotated vector by given angle around given pivot point. Positive angle values represent in counter-clockwise rotation. This means that (1, 0).Rotate(90°) == (0, 1).

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2i.AngleTo(Mafi.Tile2i)
member.summary: Returns signed angle from this vector to vector. Returned angle represents how much this vector has to be rotated to obtain vector. Returned value is [-τ/2, τ/2). This means that v1.AngleTo(v2) == -v2.AngleTo(v1) and v1.Rotate(v1.AngleTo(v2)) == v2.

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2i.AngleBetween(Mafi.Tile2i)
member.summary: Returns absolute angle between this and vectors. Returned angle is in range [0, τ/2].

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2i.IsParallelTo(Mafi.Tile2i)
member.summary: Whether this and vectors are parallel and not anti-parallel.

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2i.IsAntiParallelTo(Mafi.Tile2i)
member.summary: Whether this and vectors are anti-parallel and not parallel.

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2i.IsParallelOrAntiParallelTo(Mafi.Tile2i)
member.summary: Whether this and vectors are parallel or anti-parallel. This is more efficient than calling and .

member.summary: Returns this vector rotated by 90 degrees to the left (counter clockwise).

member.name: P:Mafi.Tile2i.RightOrthogonalVector
member.summary: Returns this vector rotated by 90 degrees to the right (clockwise).

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2i.op_LessThan(Mafi.Tile2i,Mafi.Tile2i)
member.summary: Component-wise less-than operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are less than respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A < B is not equal to A >= B. For example vectors (1, 2) and (2, 1) are not less-than nor greater-than-or-equal.

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2i.op_LessThanOrEqual(Mafi.Tile2i,Mafi.Tile2i)
member.summary: Component-wise less-than-or-equal operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are less than or equal to respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A <= B is not equal to A > B. For example vectors (1, 2) and (2, 1) are not less-than-or-equal nor greater-than.

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2i.op_GreaterThan(Mafi.Tile2i,Mafi.Tile2i)
member.summary: Component-wise greater-than operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are greater than respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A > B is not equal to A <= B. For example vectors (1, 2) and (2, 1) are not greater-than nor less-than-or-equal.

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2i.op_GreaterThanOrEqual(Mafi.Tile2i,Mafi.Tile2i)
member.summary: Component-wise greater-than-or-equal operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are greater than or equal to respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A >= B is not equal to A < B. For example vectors (1, 2) and (2, 1) are not greater-than-or-equal nor less-than.

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile3f.op_LessThan(Mafi.Tile3f,Mafi.Tile3f)
member.summary: Component-wise less-than operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are less than respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A < B is not equal to A >= B. For example vectors (1, 2, 3) and (3, 2, 1) are not less-than nor greater-than-or-equal.

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile3f.op_LessThanOrEqual(Mafi.Tile3f,Mafi.Tile3f)
member.summary: Component-wise less-than-or-equal operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are less than or equal to respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A <= B is not equal to A > B. For example vectors (1, 2, 3) and (3, 2, 1) are not less-than-or-equal nor greater-than.

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile3f.op_GreaterThan(Mafi.Tile3f,Mafi.Tile3f)
member.summary: Component-wise greater-than operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are greater than respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A > B is not equal to A <= B. For example vectors (1, 2, 3) and (3, 2, 1) are not greater-than nor less-than-or-equal.

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile3f.op_GreaterThanOrEqual(Mafi.Tile3f,Mafi.Tile3f)
member.summary: Component-wise greater-than-or-equal operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are greater than or equal to respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A >= B is not equal to A < B. For example vectors (1, 2, 3) and (3, 2, 1) are not greater-than-or-equal nor less-than.

member.name: M:Mafi.TileInChunk2i.OfLength(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns scaled vector to requested length. This method is more precise, faster and more intuitive than normalization followed by multiplication. WARNING: Setting length of integer vector may not produce exact requested length do to rounding error.

member.name: M:Mafi.TileInChunk2i.DistanceTo(Mafi.TileInChunk2i)
member.summary: Returns distance from this vector to the other vector.

member.name: M:Mafi.TileInChunk2i.DistanceSqrTo(Mafi.TileInChunk2i)
member.summary: Returns squared distance from this vector to the other vector.

member.name: P:Mafi.TileInChunk2i.Angle
member.summary: Returns absolute angle of this vector. Returned angle is in range [-180, 180].

member.name: M:Mafi.TileInChunk2i.PseudoCross(Mafi.TileInChunk2i)
member.summary: Returns pseudo-cross product of this vector with vector. That is, Z component of 3D cross product between the two vectors with Z equal to 0.
member.remarks: This operation is very efficient and has following properties: * v1.PseudoCross(v2) = -v2.PseudoCross(v1). * v1.PseudoCross(v2) = |v1| |v2| sin(θ) where θ is signed angle from v1 to v2 (v1.AngleTo(v2)). * Returns 0 for parallel (or anti-parallel) vectors. * Returns positive values when v2 is to the left of v1. * Returns negative values when v2 is to the right of v1. * Is equal to a determinant of matrix [v1,v2]. * Is equal to v1.LeftOrthogonalVector.Dot(v2).

member.name: M:Mafi.TileInChunk2i.Rotate(Mafi.AngleDegrees1f)
member.summary: Returns rotated vector by given angle. Positive angle values represent in counter-clockwise rotation. This means that (1, 0).Rotate(90°) == (0, 1). WARNING: Please keep in mind that rotating integer vectors may not be precise for vectors with small magnitudes due to rounding errors.

member.name: M:Mafi.TileInChunk2i.Rotate(Mafi.Rotation90)
member.summary: Returns rotated vector by given angle. Positive angle values represent in counter-clockwise rotation. This means that (1, 0).Rotate(90°) == (0, 1).

member.name: M:Mafi.TileInChunk2i.AngleTo(Mafi.TileInChunk2i)
member.summary: Returns signed angle from this vector to vector. Returned angle represents how much this vector has to be rotated to obtain vector. Returned value is [-τ/2, τ/2). This means that v1.AngleTo(v2) == -v2.AngleTo(v1) and v1.Rotate(v1.AngleTo(v2)) == v2.

member.name: M:Mafi.TileInChunk2i.AngleBetween(Mafi.TileInChunk2i)
member.summary: Returns absolute angle between this and vectors. Returned angle is in range [0, τ/2].

member.summary: Whether this and vectors are parallel and not anti-parallel.

member.summary: Whether this and vectors are anti-parallel and not parallel.

member.summary: Whether this and vectors are parallel or anti-parallel. This is more efficient than calling and .

member.name: M:Mafi.TileInChunk2i.op_LessThan(Mafi.TileInChunk2i,Mafi.TileInChunk2i)
member.summary: Component-wise less-than operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are less than respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A < B is not equal to A >= B. For example vectors (1, 2) and (2, 1) are not less-than nor greater-than-or-equal.

member.name: M:Mafi.TileInChunk2i.op_LessThanOrEqual(Mafi.TileInChunk2i,Mafi.TileInChunk2i)
member.summary: Component-wise less-than-or-equal operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are less than or equal to respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A <= B is not equal to A > B. For example vectors (1, 2) and (2, 1) are not less-than-or-equal nor greater-than.

member.name: M:Mafi.TileInChunk2i.op_GreaterThan(Mafi.TileInChunk2i,Mafi.TileInChunk2i)
member.summary: Component-wise greater-than operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are greater than respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A > B is not equal to A <= B. For example vectors (1, 2) and (2, 1) are not greater-than nor less-than-or-equal.

member.name: M:Mafi.TileInChunk2i.op_GreaterThanOrEqual(Mafi.TileInChunk2i,Mafi.TileInChunk2i)
member.summary: Component-wise greater-than-or-equal operator. Returns true if all components of the left-hand side vector are greater than or equal to respective components of the right-hand side vector. WARNING: A >= B is not equal to A < B. For example vectors (1, 2) and (2, 1) are not greater-than-or-equal nor less-than.

member.name: T:Mafi.Tile2iAndIndex
member.summary: Very efficient way to represent a tile and its index (8 bytes). Only valid coordinates are supported.

member.name: F:Mafi.Tile2iAndIndex.IndexRaw
member.summary: Represents a raw value of , an index to a contiguous array of all tiles on the map. This is computed as X + Y * TerrainWidth.

member.name: M:Mafi.Tile2iAndIndex.#ctor(System.UInt16,System.UInt16,System.Int32)
member.summary: WARNING: It is the callers responsibility to make sure that the (X, Y) coordinates are matching the index.

member.summary:
Draws the following:
DrawTilesTicks DrawTerrain DrawTrees DrawProps DrawAllDesignations DrawAllStaticEntities DrawAllPorts DrawAllDynamicEntities DrawTrainsPaths DrawAllTrains
Optionally it also draws the following (based on the given arguments):
DrawAllTileHeights (drawTerrainHeights) DrawPathabilityOverlayFor (drawNavOverlayFor)
